steps.m: to be free from, to clear oneself

steps.n: Forms a noun indicating the state or condition of being free/clear from something

steps.m: a complete act of clearing/freeing oneself
steps.n: The ة (ta marbutah) makes the abstract quality more definitive and complete

steps.m: a complete disassociation/declaration of being free (from obligations to the polytheists)
steps.n: The ٌ (tanween) at the end makes this word indefinite, meaning 'a' declaration of freedom, not 'the' specific declaration
This is the basic preposition that shows something moving away from or separating from something else
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound. This often happens after certain words in Arabic.
Added a fatha at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (the polytheists)
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the first letter merged with the ل making it have a shadda
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to something else. In this ayah, it's connected to رَسُولِ (messenger), showing 'messenger of Allah'
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Added ِ to show this word is connected to Allah in the sentence
Added هِ to show the messenger belongs to Allah
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to Allah mentioned before it
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or movement towards something. In this ayah, it connects 'His messenger' to 'those who'
Added vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce the word: 'i' sound under the first letter (إِ) and 'a' sound above the second letter (لَ)
Added ين at the end to make it refer to many people instead of just one person

steps.m: those who, the ones who (referring to the polytheists mentioned later)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to point to specific people. Also added shadda on ذ to show the ل and ذ combine together when spoken.
Basic vowels added to make 'he promised/made an agreement' in past tense
Added alif after first letter to show mutual action between two parties
Added تُ at the end to show first person past tense
Changed from 'I' to 'you all' by removing the ا and adding م at the end, with a stronger ت sound
This is a basic preposition showing something coming from or belonging to Allah
The shadda (ّ) makes the م sound stronger to emphasize the meaning
The fatha (َ) at the end helps connect this word smoothly to the next word (اللَّهِ)

steps.m: to join or associate something with something else
steps.n: This root is about joining or associating things together
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: one who joins partners with Allah
steps.n: The مُ prefix and middle ِ (kasra) turn this into a noun for someone who does this action

steps.m: the one who joins partners with Allah
steps.n: ٱلْ makes it refer to specific polytheists rather than polytheists in general
The ين ending makes it plural, referring to multiple polytheists
The َ (fatha) shows this word is being acted upon by the verb in the sentence
Added vowels to make basic command form
Added وا to make the command for multiple people

steps.m: so travel! (all of you) (connected to previous verse)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect this command with what came before it
This is a basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it shows where the action happens - in the earth/land.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in the Uthmani script. This is just a writing style change and doesn't affect the meaning or pronunciation.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make them pronounceable
Made definite by adding 'al' meaning 'the'
Added the mark ِ under the last letter because it comes after في (in/on) - words after prepositions get this mark
Added vowel marks (fatha) between the root letters to make a basic past tense verb meaning 'to be four'
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'cause something to be four'
Added ة to make it the special form used when counting things (like 'four books')

steps.m: to appear, become visible, make known

steps.n: Root letters ش-ه-ر were given vowels to make the common noun 'month' (related to moon appearing)
Changed to broken plural form used for small quantities (3-10)

steps.m: months (being acted upon by أَرْبَعَةَ - four)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this word is being counted by the word 'four' (أَرْبَعَةَ) that comes before it
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Changed from 'he knew' to 'you know!' by adding alif at start and changing the middle vowel - makes it a command
Added وا to make it plural command
Added و at beginning to connect with previous phrase, and hamza wasl replaced regular hamza
This particle introduces a new statement with emphasis. The shadda (double letter mark) makes the meaning stronger.
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is talking about multiple people being addressed. This word connects to غَيْرُ (not) in the ayah to say 'that you all are not'.

steps.m: to change or be different from something

steps.n: Basic noun form showing something being different or other than something else
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject of مُعْجِزِى (those who can escape) in this ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'one who' and changed vowels to show causing someone else to be weak/unable

steps.m: ones who make me unable (connected to غَيْرُ - not)
steps.n: Added ِى at the end to show this affects 'me'. This word comes after غَيْرُ (not) in the ayah, so together they mean 'not able to escape from me'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became the proper name for God in Islam.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on ل to emphasize it. The hamza at the start becomes a connecting hamza (ٱ) because it's in the middle of speech.
This is a particle that introduces a new statement with emphasis. It connects to the previous command to 'know' (وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟) in the ayah.
Added و at beginning to connect this statement with what came before it
Changed first letter to ل and added shadda to make it specifically refer to Allah, the One True God

steps.m: Allah (as the object of مُعْجِزِى - those who escape)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is being acted upon. In this ayah, it shows who cannot be escaped from.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: to cause someone to be disgraced
steps.n: Changed to Form IV pattern to show causing someone else to be disgraced
Changed to active participle form to show who does the action. This word describes Allah's action of disgracing in this context.

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed to describe someone who does this action
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple people
Adds 'the' to specify these particular rejecters of truth
Changed alif to long alif (ٰ) and added final fatha for grammatical connection
Adding alif after second letter changes this from an action (to announce) into a thing (an announcement)
The ٌ (double damma) at the end shows this is the main subject starting a new sentence and means 'an' rather than 'the' announcement

steps.m: and an announcement (from اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ - Allah and His Messenger)
steps.n: The و at the beginning connects this to what came before. This announcement is specifically from Allah and His Messenger as shown in the next part of the ayah.
This is the basic preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or someone
The shadda is added because this word follows a word ending in a vowel (وَ), causing the first letter to be doubled for smoother pronunciation
The fatha is added at the end to connect this word smoothly to the next word ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (the polytheists)
Changed إِلَه to اللَه, making it a proper name for the One God. The ل is doubled with a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word مِّنَ (from) before it. The alif at the start changed to a special connecting alif (ٱ).
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by something after it
Added هِ to show it belongs to Allah (mentioned earlier in the ayah)
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it

This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. It connects two words by showing that something goes 'to' or moves 'towards' something else. In this ayah, it shows the proclamation is going 'to' the people.

steps.m: to be close and friendly with others

steps.n: Root means being social - changed to mean 'people' since humans are social beings who live together
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the ن letter to help pronunciation
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by something before it in the sentence
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to make the basic word for 'day'. The middle letter و keeps its original sound.
Adding the َ (fatha) at the end shows this word is being acted upon. Here it tells when something happens - specifically when the proclamation (أَذٰنٌ) takes place.

steps.m: to go on pilgrimage, to journey with sacred purpose
pilgrimage (noun), the act of performing Hajj
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter turns the verb into a noun meaning the act itself
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific religious duty of Hajj pilgrimage

steps.m: of the pilgrimage (connected to يَوْمَ meaning 'day of')
steps.n: The kasra ( ِ ) shows this connects to the word يَوْمَ before it, meaning 'day of the pilgrimage'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'big' to 'bigger than' by adding أ at start - like adding '-er' in English
Adds kasra because it's describing يَوْمَ (day of) in the ayah
Makes the word definite, which changes the meaning from comparative (bigger) to superlative (biggest) in this context
This is the basic particle that connects parts of a sentence, like saying 'that' in English. It introduces what comes next as a fact or statement.

steps.m: that (emphasizing what follows is definitely true)
steps.n: Adding the shadda (double letter mark) and fatha makes this word stronger, showing what comes after is definitely true. In this ayah, it emphasizes the truth that Allah is free from the polytheists.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.
Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being talked about after أَنَّ (that). Also changed alif to wasl alif since it comes after another word.

steps.m: to be free, clear, or innocent

steps.n: The root letters are connected with vowels and ي is added to make it describe someone who has this quality. This word shows Allah and His Messenger are free from the polytheists.

steps.m: one who is completely free/innocent (of المُشْرِكِينَ)
steps.n: The double dots (tanween) at the end show this is a complete state of being free from something
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something coming from or away from something else
The shadda (ّ) is added to emphasize the word when it comes after certain letters
The fatha (َ) is added at the end to connect this word to the next word (اللَّهِ) in a flowing way

steps.m: to make partners or join others with something

steps.n: Added مُ at the start and ِ in the middle to show who does the action

steps.m: multiple people who make partners (with Allah)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people

steps.m: the people who make partners with Allah (مِنَ these people)
steps.n: Added ال at the start to mean 'the' and َ at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from)
The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'
Added هُ (his) and ُ (shows this is doing the action of being free from). This connects to the word بَرِىٓءٌ (free from) in the ayah.
Added وَ (and) to connect this word with اللَّهَ (Allah) as they are both free from the polytheists
This is a compound particle made of فَ (then) and إِن (if) used to introduce conditional statements

steps.m: to return back (from wrong to right)
he returned back (from wrong to right)
steps.n: Connected the root letters together. The middle letter و changes to ا which is common for this type of word.
Changed vowels to make it a command form. The middle letter ا drops out.

steps.m: you all returned back (from wrong to right)
steps.n: Added تُمْ at the end to show that many people did this action in the past
Compound of فَ (then) and هُوَ (he/it)
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create comparative form. This pattern is used to show something is better or the best.
Added double-damma at the end to show this is describing something in general terms. In this ayah, it's telling us that repentance is better for you all.
Added fatha to show basic pronunciation of the particle ل which shows purpose or direction
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning, making it stronger
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is directed to multiple people being addressed
Basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else

steps.m: and if (connects to تُبْتُمْ - you repented)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement. This shows that what follows is connected to the previous discussion about repentance.

steps.m: to turn, follow, be close to

steps.n: Added vowels to make past tense 'he' form
Doubling middle letter makes the meaning stronger and changes it to turning away rather than following
Adding تَ shows the person is doing the action to themselves
Changed final ي to sukun for adding suffix
Added تُمْ at the end to change from 'he turned away' to 'you all turned away' - like adding 'you all' in English
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense verb
Changes from 'he knew' to 'know!' by adding 'i' at start and changing ending - makes it a command to one person
Makes the command plural by adding وا at the end
Adds فَ at beginning to mean 'then/so' and changes spelling of alif
This is a particle that emphasizes certainty and introduces what follows as definite fact

steps.m: that you all (subject of غَيْرُ مُعْجِزِى)
steps.n: Adds كُمْ to show who the statement is about. In this ayah, it connects to غَيْرُ مُعْجِزِى (not able to escape) to show who cannot escape.
Added vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'other than'

steps.m: other than (subject of not being able to escape)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject of the phrase, connecting to مُعْجِزِى (escaping/defeating) in the ayah
This root carries the basic meaning of making something weak or unable to do something
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who causes the action to happen to others

steps.m: one who makes others unable (in genitive case)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show it's being used after a preposition or as part of a possessive phrase
Changed إِلَه to اللَه by adding ال and merging letters. This special word refers only to The One True God in Islam.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on ل to emphasize it. The word is possessed by مِّنَ (from) in this ayah.
Doubles middle letter to intensify meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda doubles the middle letter to show doing something with more emphasis

steps.m: give good news! (command to one person)
steps.n: Changed to a command form by modifying vowels and adding kasra at end
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'
Starting with the basic relative pronoun form. Added kasra and ي to make the singular form.
Added ن to make it refer to multiple people or things
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it mean 'those who'. This word connects to كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) to tell us which people are being talked about.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا at the end to show a group of people (they) did this action
Forms noun meaning 'punishment'
Changes the verb into a noun by adding alif after second letter. This makes it mean the actual punishment rather than the act of punishing.
Adds -in sound at the end to show it's describing something

steps.m: with a painful punishment (connected to بَشِّرِ - give news)
steps.n: Adds preposition meaning 'with/by'. This word is connected to بَشِّرِ (give news) in this ayah, sarcastically announcing punishment to disbelievers.
Connected the root letters with vowels to create the basic verb meaning
Added ي between second and third letters to show something causes intense pain
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this describes the punishment (عَذَابٍ) mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: except for (introduces an exception to ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ)
steps.n: This is an exception particle that introduces something being excluded from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it excludes some of the polytheists (ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ) - specifically those who kept their treaties.
Added ن and fatha to make it refer to multiple people

steps.m: those who (referring to some polytheists mentioned in عٰهَدتُّم)
steps.n: Added the definite article ال with a shadda on the ل because it's followed by ذ. This makes it specific to certain people being discussed.

steps.m: to make a promise or agreement

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) over the ع for pronunciation
Added تُ (tu) at the end to change from 'he' to 'I' in past tense
Changed from 'I made' to 'you all made' by adding م and putting shadda on ت
Basic preposition showing source, origin, or separation from something
Added emphasis by doubling the م letter with a shadda
Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next word ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (the polytheists)
Adding vowels makes this a complete past tense verb
Added مُ prefix and changed vowels to make a noun meaning 'one who does this action'
Added ين to make the word plural and show it follows the word مِنَ (from)
Added ٱلْ to make it definite and َ at the end because it follows مِنَ

steps.m: then (connects this part to what came before)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about those you made treaties with.
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يَنقُصُوكُمْ) from 'they diminish you' to 'they did not diminish you'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added و to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: they decrease you all (object of لَمْ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being decreased. This verb is negated by لَمْ (not) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to form the basic noun

steps.m: a thing (object of يَنقُصُوكُمْ - diminish from you)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon. This word is what is being diminished in the phrase 'they did not diminish from you anything'
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce this negation particle. لَمْ makes verbs after it negative and changes them to past tense.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This و means 'and'.

steps.m: to appear, become visible, or help

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding alif makes it mean doing the action with someone else - helping each other
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changed to plural 'they' by adding وا and replacing alif with ٰ
This is a common preposition in Arabic showing something is on top of or affecting something else
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning

steps.m: upon you all (object of يَنقُصُوا - they diminished)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this affects multiple people being spoken to. This word is receiving the action of يَنقُصُوا (they diminished) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic noun

steps.m: anyone (being helped by not helping others against them)
steps.n: Added two dots (tanween) at the end to show this word is the object - the one being affected by the action. It means 'anyone' here instead of just 'one'.
Added shadda to double the middle letter and connected letters with fatha
The أ at the start makes it about making something complete. The word عَهْدَهُمْ (their covenant) is the object being completed.
The ف prefix connects this to the previous statement with a sequential 'so/then' meaning
Adding وا makes this a command for a group of people
This is the basic preposition showing direction or movement toward something
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns
Added هِمْ at the end to show this is directed toward multiple people (them)

steps.m: to promise, make a covenant, or commit to something
their covenant (related to مُدَّتِهِمْ - their term/period)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show the covenant belongs to them. This covenant is mentioned in relation to their term/period (مُدَّتِهِمْ) in the next part of the verse.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. It connects 'their covenant' (عَهْدَهُمْ) to 'their term' (مُدَّتِهِمْ), showing to complete it until their term.
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show proper pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added damma at start and doubled middle letter to create base form
Added ة and fatha to change from 'extended' (verb) to 'a period of time' (noun)

steps.m: their period (referring to عَهْدَهُمْ - their treaty)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show possession, with ِ on ت because it comes before the possessive suffix. This period refers back to their treaty mentioned earlier in the verse.
Adding the shadda (double letter mark) changes this from a conditional word to one that adds emphasis to what follows. Here it emphasizes that Allah (ٱللَّهَ) truly loves the righteous.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that refers specifically to the One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يُحِبُّ - He loves)
steps.n: Allah gets a line over the ل and a small 'a' sound at the end because it's receiving the action of loving
When two identical letters come together, they combine with a shadda
Added يُ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to show ongoing action

steps.m: to protect oneself or be cautious

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed from basic verb 'protected' to mean 'someone who protects themselves from sin/is God-fearing'
Added plural ending to show multiple people
Added definite article to specify these particular God-fearing people
This is the basic conditional time word that means 'when' something happens. In this ayah, it connects to the verb ٱنسَلَخَ (passed) to show when something should happen.

steps.m: and then when (connecting to ٱنسَلَخَ - passed)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens as a result of or following something else. It connects this phrase to what came before it.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: it ended/passed (referring to ٱلْأَشْهُرُ ٱلْحُرُمُ - the sacred months)
steps.n: The ان prefix shows this happens on its own - the sacred months naturally came to an end

steps.m: to mark time, to make known

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the singular noun for 'month'
Changes singular 'month' to its plural form of 'months' (3-10)
Added the definite article to show these are specific months. This word is the subject of ٱنسَلَخَ (passed/ended)
Added the nominative case ending because this word is the subject of the verb ٱنسَلَخَ (passed/ended)

steps.m: to make something forbidden or sacred

steps.n: Changed the vowel pattern to make it plural. In this ayah, it refers specifically to the sacred months mentioned earlier (ٱلْأَشْهُرُ)

steps.m: the sacred months (subject of have passed)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific to these particular sacred months. The ُ ending shows this word is doing the action in the sentence - these months are what 'passed' (ٱنسَلَخَ)
Adds vowels to make past tense 'he' form
Changes to command form by removing first vowel and changing middle vowel to 'u'
Adds helper alif to make command easier to pronounce
Adds وا to make command for multiple people
Adds فَ (meaning 'so/then') at the start - the command was already plural in both words

steps.m: one who joins partners with Allah
steps.n: The مُ at the start and ِ in the middle work together to make this word mean 'someone who does this action'. This is like changing 'teach' to 'teacher' in English

steps.m: those who join partners with Allah (multiple men)
steps.n: Adding ين at the end makes it refer to multiple men, like adding 's' in English (but only for male groups)

steps.m: the ones who join partners with Allah (object of فَٱقْتُلُوا۟)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ at the start makes it specific, like 'the' in English. This word is being acted upon by the command 'kill' (فَٱقْتُلُوا۟) in this verse

steps.m: the ones who join partners with Allah (object of فَٱقْتُلُوا۟)
steps.n: The َ at the end shows this word is receiving the action of the verb 'kill' (فَٱقْتُلُوا۟)
This is a special word used to talk about locations
Adding vowel marks to show how to pronounce this location word
Adding damma ending to complete this location word
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding تُ at the end changes it from 'he found' to 'I found'
Adding و at the end makes it refer to 'him' as the object
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was found (them)
Added basic vowel marks to show it's past tense

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of

steps.n: Basic command form telling one person to take something
Changes command from singular to plural
Adds 'and' to connect this command with previous commands in the verse

steps.m: and seize them! (command to many people)
steps.n: Adds 'them' to show who should be seized (referring to the polytheists mentioned earlier in the verse)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle/end vowels to make it a command (telling someone to besiege)
Added alif at start and وا at end to make plural command
Added و with fatha to connect with previous command (وَخُذُوهُمْ)
Added هُمْ to show who is being besieged, connecting to previous commands about the polytheists
Basic meaning of sitting or staying in one place
Added short vowels (fatha) to make it 'he sat' in past tense
Changed from 'he sat' to 'sit!' by adding 'u' at start and changing middle/end vowels - this makes it a command
Changed from singular to plural command by adding وا at the end
Added و at beginning to mean 'and', plus hamza connector
This is a preposition that shows direction or purpose, like pointing to where something is going

steps.m: for them (shows direction towards the مُشْرِكِينَ mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who the preposition is pointing to. Here it refers back to the polytheists (مُشْرِكِينَ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This root represents the idea of completeness or totality
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Adding fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb 'sit/wait' (وَٱقْعُدُوا۟) in this ayah

steps.m: to watch or keep an eye on something

steps.n: Adding مَ at the start makes this word mean the place where watching happens, like how 'kitchen' is a place for cooking

steps.m: any place for watching (used with كُلَّ meaning 'every')
steps.n: The ٍ at the end shows this is talking about any watching place in general, not a specific one. In this ayah, it's used with كُلَّ to mean 'every watching place'
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it introduces what should happen if the polytheists repent.
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement. It shows this is what should happen next.

steps.m: to return from wrong to right
he returned from wrong to right
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا when we make it past tense. Added fathas (َ) to show it happened in the past.

steps.m: they returned from wrong to right

steps.n: Added لِلْ (lam + al) at beginning to mean 'for the'
This is a connecting word that joins two parts of a sentence. Here it connects 'it was not for the polytheists' with 'maintain Allah's mosques', showing what they shouldn't do.

steps.m: to build, develop, or maintain something to make it prosperous

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they build/maintain (referring to المشركين - the polytheists)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action. This word is connected to المشركين (the polytheists) mentioned earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: to prostrate, to put one's forehead on the ground

steps.n: Adding مَ at the beginning and ِ in the middle creates a noun meaning 'the place where people prostrate'
Adding alif after س changes the word from singular (one mosque) to plural (many mosques)

steps.m: mosques (as the object of يَعْمُرُوا - to maintain/frequent)
steps.n: Adding َ (fatha) at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb يَعْمُرُوا (to maintain/frequent)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god' by adding vowel patterns
Added 'al' to make it specific/definite, like changing 'a god' to 'the god'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being described by another word
Basic past tense verb meaning 'to witness'
Changed to active participle form showing who does the action
Changed alif to alif with bar (ٰ) - this is just a writing variation with no meaning change
Added ين ending to make it plural, referring to multiple witnesses
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or against something else. In this ayah, it connects to أَنفُسِهِم (themselves) to show they are testifying against themselves.
Added an alif that shows the long 'aa' sound at the end of the word. This is just a spelling variation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word. This is just a pronunciation mark and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to make the basic noun form meaning 'self' or 'soul'
Changed from singular 'self' to plural 'selves' using the broken plural pattern أَفْعُل

steps.m: their selves (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هِم at the end to show possession by a group. This word comes after عَلَى (upon) in the phrase عَلَى أَنفُسِهِم (against their own selves)
Changed the root into a noun by adding ُ and ْ to show the action as a thing
Added ال to point to a specific rejection, like saying 'the' in English
Added بِ to show how something is done. Also, اَلْ changes to ٱلْ after بِ

steps.m: by/with the rejection (of truth) (describing how مَا كَانَ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ - it was not for the polytheists)
steps.n: Added ِ at the end because it follows the word بِ. This connects it to the main statement about the polytheists.
Basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'those'
Adding ئِ shows these things are far away
Adding كَ shows you're talking to someone about these far things
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word describes what happened to أَعْمٰلُهُمْ (their deeds) in this ayah.
Adds feminine marker to indicate the subject is feminine
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from 'he did' (past tense verb) to 'a deed' (noun meaning one action)
Regular plural form of عَمَل (deed) - like changing 'deed' to 'deeds'

steps.m: their deeds (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show these deeds belong to them

steps.m: in, within (connects to النَّارِ - the Fire)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects to the word النَّارِ (the Fire) that comes right after it, showing they will be in the Fire.
The وَ at the start means 'and'
Root meaning 'light/fire' becomes the noun 'fire' by putting a fatha (a) after ن and changing و to ا
Added a shaddah (doubled letter mark) on the ن to emphasize how strong and intense the fire is
Added kasra at the end because this word follows في (in/within) in the ayah
Added ال to make it specific, referring to the fire of Hell in this context

steps.m: they/them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers back to multiple people (specifically male or mixed gender). In this ayah, it refers back to the disbelievers (المشركين) mentioned earlier who were trying to maintain the mosques.
Changed the verb into a word describing someone who does the action, like changing 'last' to 'lasting'
Changed the long alif to an alif with overbar (ٰ) - this is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: those who last forever (subject of هُمْ)
steps.n: Added ُونَ to show this refers to multiple people. This word is connected to هُمْ (they) in this ayah.
Adding shadda makes this word stronger, changing it from 'if' to 'indeed/certainly'

steps.m: only/just (restricts what follows to be the only possibility)
steps.n: Adding ما changes 'indeed' to 'only/just'. This combination is used to restrict what follows, making it the only possibility. In this ayah, it tells us that ONLY those who have the qualities mentioned can maintain Allah's mosques.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past-tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, damma on middle root letter, and damma at end. This word shows who is maintaining Allah's mosques.

steps.m: to prostrate, bow down in worship

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning to show this is a place where the action happens, like how 'play' becomes 'playground' in English
Changed to broken plural form by adding long alif (ٰ) after س to make it mean 'many mosques'
Added َ at the end to show this is what is being maintained/frequented in the phrase 'maintains the mosques'
Changed to the proper name for God by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis.

steps.m: of Allah (object of belief in ءَامَنَ بِ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word ءَامَنَ بِ (believed in). The ٱ is just a different way of writing the same sound as ا.

This is a special word that refers to people in a general way. In this ayah, it introduces the people who maintain/frequent the mosques (يَعْمُرُ means 'maintains/frequents'). It's like saying 'The one who...' at the start of describing someone.
This is the basic concept the word comes from - when you trust or feel safe with something, you believe in it
Basic root letters got short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make past tense verb meaning 'he was safe'
Changed the first letter to hamza and added alif, which makes the meaning about actively putting trust in something rather than just being safe. In this ayah, it shows belief specifically in Allah.
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. The word comes from the root meaning 'divine' but has special doubled letters and is always written this way.
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is being done with or by Allah. Added ِ at the end to connect it to other words in the sentence. The ٱ is just another way to write ال.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make it a noun meaning a single day
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it follows the word بِ (in/with/by)
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect it with the previous phrase about believing in Allah
Changed the word to mean 'someone/something that is last'. This word is describing اليوم (the Day) in the phrase اليوم الآخر (the Last Day).
Added ال to make it specific ('the last' instead of just 'last'). The ِ at the end shows it belongs to or describes the word 'Day' in the phrase 'the Last Day'.
The root letters ق-و-م became قَامَ (he stood) by adding vowels and changing و to ا in the middle - this is a common pattern for past tense verbs with و in the middle
Added أَ at beginning to make it causative - meaning changes from doing the action to making something/someone else do it
Added و at beginning to connect with previous phrase. This word is connected to صَّلَوٰةَ (prayer) which comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to connect with something through bending/bowing

steps.n: Root letters connected with short vowels (fat-ha) to make a basic verb form
Changed و to ا and added ة to make it a feminine noun for a single prayer
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the prayer' instead of just 'prayer'
Changed ا to وٰ for proper spelling in this position
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb أَقَامَ (established)
Connected the root letters and added fatha marks to make it past tense
Changed to Form IV to show giving to someone else (takes الزَّكَوٰةَ as what is given)
Added و at beginning to connect this action with the previous actions mentioned (believing in Allah, establishing prayer)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he purified' to the noun 'purification' by adding alif and round taa at the end
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the ز with shadda because it comes after ال
Changed spelling to standard Quranic form with و and alif above it, and added a fatha (a-sound) at the end
This is a negative particle that makes verbs past tense. It's like adding 'did not' before a verb in English.
Added و at start to connect this with the previous phrase. This word negates the verb يَخْشَ (fear) that comes after it.

steps.m: to fear or be afraid of something

steps.n: Basic past tense verb showing the feeling of fear
Added يَ at the start to show present tense (happening now). The ending changed from يَ to ى because this is a special verb that ends in ى when in present tense
The ى is dropped because it comes after لَمْ (not) which requires this special form
This is a particle that shows exception - it connects the verb يَخْشَ (fear) with ٱللَّهَ (Allah) to say 'fear none except Allah'
Changed إِلَه to الله, which is a unique name specifically referring to The One True God in Islam

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يَخْشَ - fears)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being feared in the phrase 'fears none except Allah'
Adding fatha vowels creates a special kind of verb that shows hope or expectation

steps.m: so it is hoped (connects to previous statement about believers)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this hope is a result of what was mentioned before about the believers
Added pronunciation mark (ٰٓ) to show how to say the word correctly
Basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'those'
Adding ئِ shows these things are far away
Adding كَ shows we're talking about specific things that were just mentioned (in this case, the believers who maintain mosques, pray, and give charity)

This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence, showing that what comes after it (يَكُونُوا۟) is the result or outcome of what came before. It's like saying 'Perhaps they will be...' in English.
The vowels َُ َ show this is a basic verb meaning 'to become'
Added يَ at the start to make it present tense (he/it), like changing 'was' to 'is'
Added وا at the end to show multiple people doing the action
Added small circle above وا to show it's for a group of men
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it belongs to
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word الْمُهْتَدِينَ (the guided ones). This is a helper vowel that makes it easier to pronounce.

steps.m: to guide or show the way

Added مُ at start and ت in middle to change from 'guiding' to 'being guided' - like changing from teacher to student
Added ٱلْ to specify we're talking about particular guided ones
Added ينَ to show multiple guided ones and to show it follows the word مِنَ (from/among)

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by adding تَ at the end

Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to noun pattern showing the task or profession of giving drink
Adding ة turns the action of giving drink into a specific role or position of being a drink provider

steps.m: the act of providing drink (object of أَجَعَلْتُمْ - did you make/consider)
steps.n: Adding fatha shows this word is being acted upon by the verb أَجَعَلْتُمْ (did you make/consider)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he did) to a noun (person who does) by adding long 'aa' sound and doubling last letter
Added the definite article ال to specify a particular pilgrim

steps.m: the one who performs pilgrimage (possessed by سِقَايَة)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show possession relationship with سِقَايَة (providing water). The alif is written as آ for proper pronunciation.

steps.m: to build, maintain, develop, make prosperous

Adds 'and' at beginning and accusative case ending because it's coordinated with سِقَايَةَ (providing water) as objects in the sentence

steps.m: to prostrate, bow down in worship

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning and ِ in the middle to make it mean 'the place where prostration happens'

steps.m: the mosque (connected to الْحَرَامِ - the Sacred)

steps.m: to prevent access or make sacred

This is a basic particle word meaning 'whoever' or 'the one who'. It's used to refer to people in a general way.
Added كَ at the beginning to show comparison. In this ayah, it's comparing those who maintain the mosque to those who believe in Allah.
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added alif to make it mean 'to believe/have faith' instead of just 'to be safe'
This is Allah's personal name, referring to The One True God. It's a unique word that doesn't come from a typical Arabic root.

Added 'the' to make it specific

steps.m: and the day (connected to what follows)

steps.m: and he put forth maximum effort (connected to ءَامَنَ - believed)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this action with the previous action of believing mentioned in the ayah

Added ي between second and third letters to make the word a noun
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by Allah (اللَّهِ) in the phrase 'path of Allah'

steps.m: of Allah (object of belief in ءَامَنَ بِ)

Adding reflexive/medial meaning with ت after first root letter
Changed the final vowel and added و to show plural subject
Added ن to complete the plural form

Added vowel marks to show this word is connecting to اللَّهِ (Allah) that comes after it. Together they mean 'with Allah' or 'near Allah'.
Changed from a verb root meaning 'to worship' into a noun meaning 'god' by adding special vowel marks
Added ال to make it specific and changed the first letter, making it refer specifically to Allah, the One True God

Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to or is connected to something (in this case, it's possessed by the word سَبِيلِ meaning 'path')
This is the proper name of God in Islam. It comes from the word for deity (ilah) but is a unique name that can only refer to the one true God.
The shadda shows that the letter ل is doubled for emphasis in this special name.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.m: he guides (the subject is اللَّهُ - Allah)
