Changed the root into a noun meaning a group of people who stand/exist together as a community
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object being acted upon
Added ال to specify these particular people. This word is the object being guided in the phrase 'Allah does not guide the people'

steps.m: to wrong or act unjustly against others
steps.n: The root gives the basic concept of wrongdoing or injustice
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the pattern to show someone who does the action regularly
Added ending to show this refers to many wrongdoers
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the ظ letter. This word is what Allah does not guide in the ayah.
This is the basic demonstrative word used to point to something
Changed to the relative pronoun form that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ال to make it specific and definite
Added shadda for emphasis and proper pronunciation
Changed to plural form by adding ين at the end. This word refers to the believers who are the subject of the following verb ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the first letter and added a long 'aa' sound to show stronger meaning
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word is part of describing who has the greatest rank (أَعْظَمُ) in this verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he migrated (with others) (one of the actions of الَّذِينَ)
steps.n: Adding alif between first and second root letters makes it mean doing the action with/to someone else. This word is one of the verbs describing what الذين (those who) did - they migrated.

steps.m: they migrated (with others) (one of the actions of الَّذِينَ)
steps.n: Adding وا makes it plural masculine past tense

steps.m: and they migrated (with others) (one of the actions of الَّذِينَ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action with the previous one (believed)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Adding alif intensifies the meaning and shows mutual action - striving against something
Added conjunction to connect with previous verbs (believed and migrated)

steps.m: and they strived hard (referring to those who believed)
steps.n: Added plural suffix to match the subject 'those who' (الذين)

steps.m: in (connects سَبِيلِ with what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects سَبِيلِ (path) with the previous actions.

steps.m: in (connects سَبِيلِ with what comes before)
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'fee'

steps.m: in (connects سَبِيلِ with what comes before)
steps.n: Changed the final ي to ى which is a common spelling variation at the end of words. Both are pronounced the same way.

steps.m: to move along a path or way
steps.n: Basic root meaning showing the concept of moving or traveling along a path
Adding ي and vowels changes the word from a basic concept into a concrete noun meaning 'path'
Added kasra at the end to show this path belongs to or is connected to Allah, as shown by اللَّهِ coming right after it in the ayah
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to the One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, since this is describing 'the path of Allah'. Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
Root letters م-و-ل form مال by putting alif in middle to make a noun meaning wealth
Changes from single wealth/money to many by adding 'a' at start and 'waa' in middle - a common way to make Arabic plurals
Shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else
Adds هِمْ to show possession by a group (their)
Adds بِ to show they did something using or by means of their wealth

steps.m: to breathe, be precious, or be a self

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'
Regular word 'self' changed to special plural form 'selves' by rearranging letters and vowels
Added هِمْ to show possession (their)
Added conjunction وَ meaning 'and' to connect with the previous word أَمْوٰلِهِمْ (their wealth)
Added vowels to connect the root letters in the basic past tense form
Changes to comparative form to show higher rank. This word describes درجة (rank/level) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added ة to show one specific instance of the concept
Added the -an ending to show this word is receiving the action of being 'greater/higher'

steps.m: to be present or located with/at/near
steps.n: This is a common preposition in Arabic that comes from the idea of being present with something
Adding these vowel marks turns it into the common preposition meaning 'with' or 'at'
Changed إِلَه to الله by adding ال and merging letters. This special word is only used for The One True God in Islam.
Added ِ at the end to show this belongs to/describes the path (سَبِيلِ) mentioned before it in the phrase 'path of Allah'
This is the basic form of the word that points to people or things that are far away, like saying 'those people over there'

steps.m: those (pointing to specific people far away)
steps.n: Added ئِكَ at the end to point to specific people/things we can see far away (like saying 'those ones there')

steps.m: and those (referring to the believers, emigrants, and strivers mentioned before)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous description. Here it's connecting to the description of the believers who emigrated and strived in Allah's cause.
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'they' for a group of males or mixed gender. It's one of the fundamental building blocks of Arabic, like 'they' in English.
Added damma marks (ُ) to show this is the subject form of 'they'. This word connects to الْفَآئِزُونَ (the successful ones) to say 'they are the successful ones'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The verb 'he succeeded' changes to 'successful person' by adding an alif and changing the middle letter. This word tells us what 'they' (hum) are like.
Adds ون to make it plural, showing it refers to multiple people
Adding ال ('the') makes it 'the successful ones'. The long alif (آ) is written differently but pronounced the same. The ending changes to match its role in the sentence.

steps.m: to give good news intensively (to many people or repeatedly)
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter shows this is done to many people or multiple times
The prefix يُ shows this is happening now or will happen, and the doer is 'he'

steps.m: he gives them good news (subject of رَبُّهُم)
steps.n: Adding هُمْ shows who receives the good news. This word connects to رَبُّهُم (their Lord) who is doing the action.

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop
one who cares for and helps others grow (subject of يُبَشِّرُ - gives good news)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this person does a lot of caring and helping. This word is connected to يُبَشِّرُ (gives good news) in this ayah.

steps.m: one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: Added ُ to show this word is the main subject

steps.m: their caring lord (subject of يُبَشِّرُ - gives good news)
steps.n: Added هُم to show who this caring lord belongs to - it's their lord
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Adding ة and vowel pattern makes this a noun meaning the quality of mercy
Adding بِ changes it to mean 'with mercy' or 'by mercy'
Adding tanween makes the word indefinite (a mercy)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or its source
Added shadda because it comes before a letter م (meem). This is just a pronunciation change where the ن (noon) merges into the م (meem).
Added هُ which means 'him/it' to show this mercy is from Allah. This connects to the word رَحْمَةٍ (mercy) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Changed from basic root meaning 'to be pleased' into noun meaning 'pleasure' by adding vowel marks (kasra and sukoon)
The ان ending makes it mean 'a lot of pleasure' or 'complete pleasure'
Changed ending to show it's receiving the action (like saying 'with pleasure' instead of just 'pleasure')
Added وَ to connect this word with رَحْمَةٍ (mercy) mentioned before it
Added vowels and connected the letters to make a basic past-tense verb
Forms the noun 'garden' as a place that provides shade and covering
Changes singular 'garden' to plural 'gardens' by replacing ة with ات
Adding ٍ shows this word is being described by what comes after it in the ayah (نَعِيمٌ مُّقِيمٌ - lasting pleasure)
Adding وَ connects this to the previous blessings mentioned (رَحْمَةٍ - mercy and رِضْوٰنٍ - pleasure)
The shadda (doubled ل) adds emphasis and shows strong connection to the previous word

steps.m: for them (receiving good news from يُبَشِّرُهُمْ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which means 'them' to show who receives the benefit. This connects to the verb يُبَشِّرُهُمْ showing they are receiving good news.
This is a particle showing location or containment. In this ayah, it's specifically talking about being in the gardens of Paradise.
Added هَا which refers back to جَنَّات (gardens) mentioned earlier in the ayah. The feminine form is used because جَنَّات (gardens) is feminine in Arabic.

steps.m: to be at ease, comfortable, or blessed

steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is an ongoing state of blessing. The vowels َ (fatha) and ِ (kasra) connect the letters.

steps.m: everlasting bliss and comfort (describing جَنَّاتٍ - gardens)
steps.n: The ٌ (tanween dammah) shows this is describing the gardens mentioned in the ayah, indicating the gardens have this quality of eternal bliss

steps.m: to stand firmly or remain in place

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at the start and changed vowels to make it mean 'one who does something'. The و changed to ي because it's between two vowels (a common Arabic rule).
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on م and double-u sound at end to show this describes a lasting quality of نَعِيمٌ (bliss)
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and changed vowels to show someone who does the action
Added ين to make it plural - means multiple people who remain
Added fatha to show this word is in accusative case
This is a preposition that shows location or containment
Added هَا which refers back to جَنَّات (gardens) mentioned earlier. This shows who or what is being stayed in.

steps.m: in it (referring to جَنَّات gardens, with emphasis)
steps.n: The alif is elongated (آ) to add emphasis and help with pronunciation at the end of the word
Connected the letters with fatha to make it a noun
Added an alif at the end to make it an adverb meaning 'forever' - it tells us how long they will stay
This is the basic form of the word that connects parts of a sentence, like saying 'that' in English
Adding the shadda makes this word stronger, changing it from a simple connector to a word that emphasizes the great reward (أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ) mentioned after it
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the object being described as having a great reward)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a shadda (double letter), a fatha ending (to show it's being acted upon), and a special connecting alif since it joins with the word before it
This word is used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'at' or 'with' someone
The sukun (circle mark) helps make the word easier to pronounce
Added هُ to show this refers to Allah. This word describes something that is with or near Allah.

steps.m: with Him (referring to Allah, with emphasis)
steps.n: The lengthening marks (ۥٓ) add emphasis when referring to Allah

steps.m: to give payment or reward for work

steps.n: Changed from action to thing by adding vowel marks. This makes it a noun meaning the reward itself rather than the act of rewarding.
Added ٌ at the end to show this is the subject (doer) after the word إِنَّ in the sentence. The double damma (ٌ) shows it's indefinite, meaning 'a reward' not 'the reward'.
The ي between ظ and م makes this describe something as having a strong quality. Here it's describing the reward (أَجْرٌ) mentioned in the ayah.
The ٌ at the end shows this is indefinite - meaning 'a great (reward)' rather than 'the great (reward)'
Two ي letters combine into one strong ي with shadda to make a question word meaning 'which'
يا is added to call out to someone, like saying 'hey!' or 'O!'
Adding marks to make it a proper way to call out to someone (like saying 'hey you!')
Adding ها makes it a complete way to address people
This is the basic form of the word that means 'who'
Added ين to show we're talking about multiple people
Added ٱلَّ at the beginning to point to specific people. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) to tell us which people we're talking about - the ones who believed.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added a long alif after hamza to make it mean 'to believe' instead of just 'to feel safe'
Added وا at the end to show that 'those who' (الذين) are the ones doing the believing
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تَتَّخِذُوٓا۟ (take) into 'do not take'. It's like adding 'don't' before a verb in English.
Basic meaning of taking or grasping something
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added اِتّ at the start to show he took something specifically for himself (like adopting or choosing)
Changed to present tense with تَ prefix and ُ ending
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Basic concept of fatherhood
Root letters أ-ب-و become the simple word for 'father'
Changes from 'father' to irregular plural 'fathers' - not a simple plural like adding -un
Changes alif to alif madd for proper pronunciation
Adds 'your (plural)' and shows this is what's being taken/adopted in the phrase 'do not take your fathers'
Connected root letters with fatha to form basic noun
Forms broken plural indicating group/collection of brothers

steps.m: and your brothers (accusative case, object of لَا تَتَّخِذُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added 'wa' (and) at start + 'kum' at end to show 'your brothers'. The word is being acted upon by the verb 'do not take'

steps.m: to be close to and protect
steps.n: Basic concept of closeness and authority
Added vowels and doubled the last letter to make it mean 'someone who protects'
Changed from single protector (وَلِيّ) to multiple protectors by making it plural (أَوْلِيَاء) - like changing 'friend' to 'friends'

steps.m: protectors, guardians (as the object being taken)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this word is the object of تَتَّخِذُوا (do not take)
Basic conditional particle that introduces a condition (like 'if' in English). In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if they prefer disbelief over faith'
Added kasra at the end because it's followed by a word starting with a hamza that's dropped (ٱسْتَحَبُّوا۟). This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters

steps.m: he preferred/chose to love (over something else)
steps.n: The prefix ٱسْتَ shows actively choosing or preferring something, while the doubled ب shows intensity
Changed to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, they are preferring disbelief (الكفر) over faith (الإيمان)

steps.m: to reject, to disbelieve, to be ungrateful
steps.n: Basic concept of covering or denying
Changed the verb into a noun showing the state or act of rejecting faith

steps.m: the disbelief (contrasted with الْإِيمٰنِ/faith in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and َ to show it's the object being preferred (from استَحَبُّوا/they preferred). This disbelief is directly contrasted with faith (الْإِيمٰنِ) in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing preference of one thing over another in this ayah. Here it shows the disbelievers preferring disbelief (كُفْر) over faith (إِيمٰن).
Basic concept of safety and trust
Changed to noun pattern that shows the result of trusting/believing
Added 'the' at the beginning to make it specific
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it
This is a special word (relative pronoun) that refers to any person who does something

steps.m: and whoever (subject of يَتَوَلَّهُم - takes them as allies)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before. This word introduces the person who might take the disbelievers as allies.

steps.m: to be close to or support someone
steps.n: Basic concept of closeness and authority
Changed root letters to past tense by adding vowels (fatha) to make 'he turned away'

steps.m: he takes as a close friend
steps.n: Added يَ at start to show present tense, تَ after first letter to show doing something for oneself, and doubled ل to show intensity

steps.m: he takes them as close friends
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show who is being taken as friends
This is a basic preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or being part of a group
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being talked to
The shadda on the مِ is due to it coming after a word ending in a similar sound, making the pronunciation stronger. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is the base demonstrative pronoun meaning 'those'. The long alif (ٰٓ) helps extend the sound.
Adding ئِكَ makes it point to something far away, like saying 'those over there'
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this happens as a result of what came before. It's like saying 'so' or 'then' in English.
Basic independent pronoun meaning 'they' (masculine plural)
The sukun changes to damma because this pronoun connects to the word after it (ٱلظَّالِمُونَ). This is like adding a bridge between the words to make them flow together better when speaking.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
This pattern shows someone who does the action - like adding '-er' in English (teach → teacher). The word is the predicate (khabar) of the pronoun هم, telling us what 'they' are.
Adding ون makes the word plural, like adding 's' in English but only for male groups
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Connected the root letters together with fatha to make the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding ُ on first letter, 2) Adding ْ on second letter, and 3) Dropping the middle weak letter و. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it starts a condition about if certain things (family, wealth, etc.) are more beloved than Allah and His Messenger.
Added fatha marks to show it's past tense and the subject is 'he' or 'it'

steps.m: he/it was (introducing a list of things in إِن كَانَ)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This word introduces a condition with إِن (if) followed by a list of things.

steps.m: to care for as a father

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic word for father
Changed from singular 'father' to the base form used before making it plural
Added plural ending to show multiple fathers
Changed the ending to show it will connect to another word (like 'your fathers' or 'their fathers')
Added suffix showing the fathers belong to multiple people being addressed
Root 'build/produce' becomes the common word for 'son'
Changes singular 'son' to plural 'sons' using the broken plural pattern
Adds damma (ُ) to show this word is the subject of the sentence
Adds 'your (plural)' meaning by adding the suffix for 'your (plural)'
Adds 'and' at the beginning to connect this word to the previous one
Basic root meaning refers to brotherhood or fraternal relationship
Changed from a root meaning 'brotherhood' into the word 'brother' by adding an alif at the start
Makes the word plural by adding ان, a common way to make plurals in Arabic
Added كم to show the brothers belong to 'you' (when speaking to multiple people)
Added conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'
Basic root meaning relates to pairing or coupling
This is a broken plural form, meaning it changes the inside of the word instead of adding an ending
Added possessive pronoun 'your' (plural)
Added conjunction 'and' at beginning. The alif after و is elongated (ٰ) which is a common writing variation but doesn't change pronunciation or meaning

steps.m: to live together, be close companions
steps.n: Root relates to social interaction and companionship
Root letters ع-ش-ر got vowels and ي added to make a noun meaning someone you live closely with
Added ة to make it refer to a group of related people rather than just one person
Added كُمْ to show this clan belongs to multiple people (you all)
Added وَ (and) at beginning and ُ to connect with other family members being listed in the verse
Root relates to wealth and possession
Connected the root letters with vowels. The middle letter و changes to ا which is common in words with و in the middle.
Makes the word plural to show multiple properties or forms of wealth
Adds tanween dammah to show this is any wealth (not specific)
Added 'wa' (and) at the beginning to connect it to what came before

steps.m: to gain or acquire something (especially through effort)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added ا at start and ت after first letter to show doing action for oneself
Added تُمُ to show that many people (you all) did this action in the past
Added ها (meaning 'it') at the end to show what was gained. The و is added before ها to connect them smoothly.

steps.m: to trade or do business (buying and selling)

steps.n: Added ا after the second letter (ج) to transform the verb root into a noun form

steps.m: a specific instance or type of trade/commerce
steps.n: Added ة at the end to make it mean 'a specific trade' instead of trading in general
Added tanween (double dammah) to show this is any trade, not a specific one

steps.m: and trade/commerce (connected to the list of things you might love)
steps.n: Added و to connect this to the previous items in the list of things that might be loved more than Allah and His Messenger
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from singular 'you' to plural 'you all'

steps.m: to become dull/inactive (like a market with no sales)

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after second letter to make it into a noun meaning the state of being inactive
Added هَا at the end to show this stagnation belongs to the trade mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to live somewhere, stay in a place

steps.n: Added مَ at the start to show this is a place where the action happens, like how 'kitchen' is a place for cooking
Changed the word to show many places instead of just one, like changing 'house' to 'houses'
Added وَ at the start to connect this to the words before it (like saying 'and'), and added ُ at the end to show this is part of the subject of the sentence
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Adds تَ prefix to show 'you' and changes to present tense 'are pleased' instead of past tense 'was pleased'
Adds ن at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
Adds هَا as attached pronoun meaning 'it' (feminine) as direct object
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he loved' by adding vowels
The middle letter gets doubled to show stronger meaning

steps.m: he made (someone) love / caused to love
steps.n: Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or movement towards something

steps.m: to you all (referring to the people who love the things mentioned more than Allah)
steps.n: Added كم at the end to show this is directed at multiple people. The ي with sukun (يْ) is added between the preposition and pronoun to connect them smoothly.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from' that doesn't come from a root
The shadda (double letter mark) adds emphasis to show importance
The fatha at the end shows this connects to the next word اللَّهِ (Allah). Together they form part of a comparison structure with أَحَبَّ (more beloved than).
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making this the proper name for The One True God
Changed first letter to ٱ and added ِ at the end to show this belongs to or comes from Allah. This word appears in a list of things that might be more beloved to people than Allah and His messenger.
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Adds هِ at the end meaning 'his'. In this ayah, this refers specifically to Allah's messenger.
Adds conjunction prefix وَ meaning 'and'

steps.m: to strive hard, exert maximum effort

steps.n: Changes the verb into a noun that describes the action of striving hard, especially in a religious or noble cause
The tanwin shows this is indefinite and connects to 'in His path' (في سبيله) later in the ayah
Adds و at start meaning 'and'
This is a preposition that shows location or being inside something. Here it connects the word جِهَادٍ (striving) with سَبِيلِ (path) to show 'striving in His path'
Added ي between second and third letters to make the word stronger
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by something
Added هِۦ at the end to show this is Allah's path
Added vowels and doubled middle letter to show intensive meaning
Adding ت at start shows the person is doing the action to/for themselves
Added فَ at beginning to mean 'so/then' as a response to the previous statement
Added وا at the end to show this is a command for multiple people
Added fatha and shadda to show this is a complete particle. The shadda makes the ت stronger to emphasize the meaning.
Added alif maqsura (ىٰ) at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to the verb يَأْتِىَ (comes) to mean 'until (Allah) comes with His command'.
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and 'he' as the doer. Added vowel marks to help pronunciation.
Added fatha at end because it comes after حَتَّىٰ (until) which requires this change. This shows the word is affected by what comes before it.
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped because this word is used so often

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَأْتِىَ - He comes)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added damma to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence
Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'command' by adding vowel marks. The sukun (ْ) makes it a simple noun instead of an action word.
Added kasra to show this word will be possessed by something and follows the word 'with/by'
Added بِ at beginning to mean 'with/by'. This word relates to Allah's command in this ayah.
Added هِۦ at the end to show this is Allah's command. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark.

steps.m: Allah (proper name of God in Arabic)
steps.n: This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a standard root. It's the personal name of God in Arabic.

steps.m: Allah (proper name of God in Arabic)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show Allah is the one doing the action in this sentence - specifically, Allah is the one who guides

steps.m: and Allah (as the doer of يَهْدِى/guides)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and'. Also added ٱ (wasl) which helps connect the words smoothly when reading.
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen. Here it negates the verb يَهْدِى (guides) that comes after it, changing its meaning to 'does not guide'

steps.m: to guide, show the right path

steps.n: The root letters got vowels to make them pronounceable. The last letter ي became ى (alif maqsurah) because it's at the end of the word, making it 'he guided'
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to ِ, and added sukun after first letter to help pronunciation

steps.m: to stand firmly or rise up

steps.n: Adding fatha at the end shows this word is receiving the action of the verb يَهْدِى (guides)
Adding ال makes it refer to specific people. The fatha shows it's receiving the action of guiding.

steps.m: one who breaks away from obedience (object of يَهْدِى/guides)
steps.n: This form shows who does the action. In this ayah, these people are the ones Allah does not guide.

steps.m: ones who break away from obedience (plural)
steps.n: The ين ending makes it plural (more than two) and shows it's being acted upon by the verb يَهْدِى (guides)

steps.m: the disobedient ones (who Allah does not guide)
steps.n: The ال makes it refer to specific disobedient people. The َ at the end shows they are being acted upon by the verb يَهْدِى (guides). The long ا is written as ٰ above the س.
Added sukun at the end to show this is a complete particle. قَد is used to emphasize that something definitely happened.
Added لَ at the beginning for extra emphasis. This combination لَقَدْ strongly emphasizes that the next statement (Allah helped you) is definitely true.
Basic meaning of helping or supporting someone
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This verb follows لَقَدْ which emphasizes that this definitely happened.

steps.m: He (Allah) definitely helped you all (followed by ٱللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added كُمُ to show that Allah helped 'you all'. The ُ at the end connects this word to the next word ٱللَّهُ (Allah) which names who did the helping.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name 'Allah' that only refers to the one true God. The ُ ending shows Allah is doing something in this verse.

steps.m: in (connects مَوَاطِنَ 'places/situations' to نَصَرَكُمُ 'He helped you')
steps.n: Basic preposition showing location or containment. Here it connects how Allah helped the believers in many situations/places.

steps.m: to settle or stay in a place
steps.n: Basic meaning of settling in a place
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'place where the action happens'
Makes the word plural to show multiple places
Added fatha at the end to show this word comes after the word في (in/at)
Basic meaning of abundance
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective meaning 'many' or 'much'
Added ة to make the word feminine - now it describes feminine nouns
Added the kasra with tanween (ٍ) because this word follows مَوَاطِنَ (places) in the phrase 'in many places'
Basic concept of a day or time period
The middle letter و stays as a consonant here and isn't changed despite being a weak letter. This word is in a possession relationship with حُنَيْنٍ (Hunayn), forming the phrase 'the day of Hunayn'.
Added وَ (and) at the start and changed the ending to show it's 'on the day' rather than just 'day'
This is a proper noun naming a specific battle location where Muslims initially faced difficulty but ultimately achieved victory

steps.m: at/in Hunayn (location where battle took place)
steps.n: The ٍ (kasratan) at the end shows this word comes after فِى (in/at) in the phrase 'in many places and on the day of Hunayn'
This is a particle that points to a specific time in the past. Here it introduces the time when 'your numbers impressed you' (أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ).
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning changes the meaning from experiencing amazement to causing amazement in others. This makes it transitive - needing someone to receive the action.
The تْ shows the subject is feminine - in this case referring to كَثْرَتُكُمْ (your great numbers) which comes right after in the ayah

steps.m: it (your great numbers) amazed/pleased you all
steps.n: كُمْ shows who was amazed/pleased - referring back to the Muslims who were initially pleased with their large numbers before the battle
Changed the root letters into a simple noun by adding vowel marks (fatha and sukun)
Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance of having many. This word is what amazed them (أَعْجَبَتْ) in the verse.
Added كُمْ to show it belongs to 'you all' (the Muslims being addressed). This refers to how the Muslims were impressed by their own large numbers at the battle of Hunayn.
This is a basic negative particle that changes present tense verbs to past tense negative meaning
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. This word negates the verb تُغْنِ (benefit) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel from fat-ha to sukoon
Changed from masculine (he) to feminine (she) by replacing يُ with تُ
Final ي dropped because لَمْ makes the verb negative and unfinished (like 'did not benefit')
Basic preposition meaning 'away from' or 'from'
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
Changes from verb 'to want' into noun meaning 'thing'

steps.m: any thing (being affected by تُغْنِ - did not benefit)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is what's being affected by the verb تُغْنِ (did not benefit). The tanween (double marks) shows it's any general thing, not a specific thing.
Adding ا makes this a past tense verb meaning 'became narrow'

steps.m: and it became narrow (connected to أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ)
steps.n: Added و to connect this action with the previous one (أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ - it amazed you)

steps.m: and it (the earth) became narrow (feminine because الْأَرْضُ is feminine)
steps.n: Added ت to show the subject is feminine (الْأَرْضُ - the earth)
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
When adding a suffix, عَلَى changes to عَلَيْ. The ى changes to ي with sukun
Added كُمُ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
Adding vowel marks to make the basic word for 'earth'
Adding damma to show this is the subject doing the action of becoming narrow in the ayah

steps.m: the earth (subject of ضَاقَتْ - became narrow)
steps.n: Adding definite article to make the noun definite
This is a compound particle made up of the preposition بِ (with) and the relative pronoun مَا (what)
The vowel pattern shows this describes something naturally becoming wide/spacious

steps.m: it (feminine) became spacious (referring to الْأَرْضُ/the earth)
steps.n: Added ت to show this refers to a feminine subject (the earth) in the past tense

steps.m: then/thereafter (connects to وَلَّيْتُم - you turned away)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows something happened after a pause or gap in time. In this ayah, it connects to how the people turned away after earlier events.
Added shadda to show strong or intensive turning away
Added stop mark to show end of word/pause
Added تُ to show past tense first person (I)
Changed تُ to تُم to show plural 'you' as the subject
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to make it describe someone who does the action
Added emphasis by doubling the م with a shadda

steps.m: those who definitely turn back (in retreat)
steps.n: Added ين to show this refers to multiple people, with َ at end to show it's the object of وَلَّيْتُم (you turned away)
Starting with the basic particle that shows one thing follows another

steps.m: then (connects to أَنزَلَ - He sent down)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and shadda with fatha (َّ) to complete the word. In this ayah, it connects to what happens next - that Allah sent down His tranquility.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he sent down (subject of Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the start makes someone cause the action instead of doing it themselves. Here Allah is making something descend rather than descending Himself.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel patterns

steps.m: Allah (the one true God) (subject of أَنزَلَ - He sent down)
steps.n: Changed to the unique name of the one true God by adding ال and merging letters. The ُ shows Allah is doing the action in this ayah - He is the one who sent down tranquility.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Makes it a feminine noun meaning the state of calmness
Adds 'his' to show it belongs to Allah
Added هُۥ (His) at the end to show it belongs to Allah. This word (tranquility) is what Allah sent down.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of, over, or upon something else. It's one of the basic words in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.
Added fatha marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly
Added the small alif (ٰ) to show this is the final form. In this ayah, it shows something coming down upon/over Allah's messenger.
Changed from basic root meaning 'send' to 'messenger' by adding vowels, with -i ending showing it belongs to someone
Added هِۦ at the end to show this messenger belongs specifically to Allah
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or over something else. In this ayah, it connects to the word 'believers' (الْمُؤْمِنِينَ) to show Allah's tranquility came upon them.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, showing this is another thing that happened along with Allah sending down His tranquility.
Changed the root to mean 'believe' by adding آ at the beginning. This makes it about causing the feeling of trust/safety.
Added مُ at the start and changed middle vowels to make it mean 'the person who believes'
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple believers
Added ال at the start to show we're talking about a specific group of believers

steps.m: the believers (who received Allah's tranquility in أَنزَلَ)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show the believers are receiving the action of Allah sending down tranquility upon them
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'coming down' to 'causing to come down/sending down'. This is Allah sending something down.
Added 'and' to connect this action with what came before in the verse
Changes from a root meaning 'gather troops' into a plural noun meaning 'armies' by adding vowel marks and و
Shows this word receives the action of 'sending down' (أَنزَلَ) in the verse
Basic negative particle that changes verbs to past tense and makes them negative, like changing "he sees" to "he did not see"
Added emphasis by doubling the ل sound. This happens because the word comes after ثُمَّ in this ayah.

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added تَ prefix to show 'you' and changed to present tense form. The weak letter ي changes to ى in present tense.
Changed ending from ى to وْ to make it 'you all' instead of 'you'
Added هَا to show the feminine object being seen

steps.m: he punished severely (action Allah did to الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Adding shadda doubles the middle root letter ذ to intensify the meaning. This word describes what Allah did to those who disbelieved, as mentioned in the next part of the ayah.

steps.m: and he punished severely (action Allah did to الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Adds و at the beginning to connect this action with the previous actions in the ayah (sending down tranquility and sending armies)
This is the basic form meaning 'who' for one person or thing
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people
Adding ال makes it mean 'those who' instead of just 'who'. The shadda (ّ) shows emphasis. The final َ (fatha) shows this word is the subject of كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved).

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb. This shows one person doing the action in the past.
Added plural masculine suffix وا with damma before it to show multiple people (they). The small ۟ mark (known as a small wow) is added above to show proper pronunciation.

steps.m: that (pointing to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a demonstrative pronoun pointing to something far or previously mentioned. The ك at the end makes it point to something farther away. In this ayah, it's referring back to the punishment mentioned before.

steps.m: and that (pointing to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it, showing this is an additional point about the punishment.

steps.m: to give back what is due (reward or payback)

steps.n: Root letters connected with vowels to make past tense verb meaning 'he rewarded'
Changed the word to show the result of the action (the reward itself) instead of the action of rewarding
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of what follows in the sentence

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changes verb into noun for someone who does the action
Changes from one disbeliever to many disbelievers by adding ين at the end
Adds 'the' (ال) to make it specific disbelievers, with a long 'aa' sound
This is the basic form of the connecting word that shows something happens after something else

steps.m: then (connects to يَتُوبُ - He accepts repentance)
steps.n: Added marks to show this word strongly connects two ideas - what happened before and what happens next
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels. This word refers to Allah's action of accepting repentance.

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God who is the subject of يَتُوبُ - He accepts repentance)
steps.n: Added ال to specify THE one true deity, doubled the ل with shadda, and changed the first letter to show this is a unique proper name. The ُ shows Allah is the one doing the action (accepting repentance) in this verse.
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'that' (ذٰلِكَ) with 'after' (بَعْدِ) to mean 'after that'.
Added a small ن (called tanwin) at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create the basic noun meaning 'after'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it (ذٰلِكَ meaning 'that')
This is the basic demonstrative word that points to something. It starts by pointing to something close.
The alif is replaced with a special mark (ٰ) to show we're pointing to something farther away or something mentioned earlier
Added لِ to make the pointing stronger and clearer

steps.m: that (referring to Allah's acceptance of repentance mentioned before)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show we're specifically pointing to something mentioned before in the ayah - in this case, Allah's acceptance of repentance
Shows something is on top of or affecting something else
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition showing one thing being above or affecting another

steps.m: upon (connected to يَتُوبُ - He turns in mercy)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is the standard written form. In this ayah, it shows who Allah turns to in mercy.
This is a relative pronoun that refers to an unspecified person. In this ayah, it's the subject of يَشَآءُ (He wills), meaning 'whoever He wills'.
Contains the hamza (ء) which can change form
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it a complete past tense verb meaning 'he wished'
The weak letter ي changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs
Added يَ at start to show present tense and ُ at end to show he is doing the action. This verb describes what Allah does after He turns in mercy (يَتُوبُ)
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. The shadda (doubled ل) emphasizes the uniqueness and greatness of Allah.
Added fatha on the doubled ل to help with pronunciation
The damma at the end shows that Allah is the one doing the action in this sentence
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement

steps.m: to forgive or cover up faults
steps.n: This root carries the basic meaning of forgiveness and covering up faults
Added fathas to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show this is a strong, constant quality
Added two small dashes (tanween) to show this is one of Allah's names meaning He is Most Forgiving
Root ر-ح-م became رَحِيم by adding vowels and ي to make it mean 'one who shows a lot of mercy'
Added tanween dammah (ٌ) to show this is indefinite - meaning 'a merciful one' rather than 'the merciful one'
The ر has a shaddah (doubled) because it comes after the word الله

steps.m: to point to or direct attention

steps.n: Root letters أ-ي-ي became the question word أَيّ by adding the vowel 'a' and combining the two ي letters into one doubled letter
Added the calling particle يٰٓ at the beginning to show we're calling out to someone
Added a small u-sound (damma) to make it into 'O you' for calling someone's attention
Added ها to make it address a group of people
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to refer to someone or something
Added ن to make it refer to multiple people or things
Added ال at the beginning to make it more specific, like saying 'those who' instead of just 'who'
Changed the spelling of ال to ٱلَّ with a shadda, but the meaning stays the same
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added an alif at the start to make the meaning 'to cause/make someone else feel safe' instead of just 'feeling safe' yourself
Changed from 'he believed' to 'they believed' by adding وا at the end - this is the group of believers being called out to

Adding مَا changes the meaning from emphasis to restriction. It tells us that what comes next is the only thing that applies, like saying 'just' or 'only' in English
Adding vowels makes this a complete past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does this action

steps.m: the one who associates partners (with Allah)
steps.n: Added ال with proper spelling (ٱلْ) to make it specific
Added ون to show multiple people who do this action
Added fathas to connect the letters together and show this is a past tense verb

Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. It connects to the previous statement about polytheists being unclean.
This pattern shows something naturally becoming close/near
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they come close (negative command: 'let them not come close' in context)
steps.n: Changed to plural form and negative command context. This word follows فَلَا (so do not) in the ayah, making it a negative command.
The مَ prefix creates a noun meaning 'place where the action happens'
Added the definite article ال and fatha to show this is the object that should not be approached (يَقْرَبُوا۟ meaning 'they should not approach')
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

Added ال to make it specific and َ at the end because it describes ٱلْمَسْجِدَ

steps.m: to be after or follow something

steps.n: Root letters got vowel marks (fatHa and sukoon) to make the word mean 'after'

Combination of و (and) with إن (if)

The weak letter و is dropped and the vowels change due to Arabic phonetic rules

Changed from 'I feared' to 'you all feared' by adding تُم at the end

steps.m: he will enrich you all (subject: اللَّهُ)

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Adding 'the' makes it refer to a specific god
Shows this word is the subject of the sentence
Basic preposition showing where something comes from. In this ayah, it shows that Allah's bounty (فَضْلِهِ) is the source that will make them rich.

steps.m: to give extra or be generous

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha and sukun to create a noun meaning the result of being generous
Added kasra to show this word belongs to something
Added هِ to show this belongs to Him (Allah)
Added pronunciation helpers that don't change the meaning

The weak letter hamza (ء) combines with alif to make a long 'aa' sound

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يُغْنِيكُمُ - will enrich you)

Added ي to show this is a permanent, intense quality
Added two dots (tanween) to show this is describing Allah - like saying 'Allah is All-Knowing'
Added vowels and ي to create an intensive adjective describing Allah's perfect wisdom
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is one of Allah's divine attributes mentioned in this ayah

steps.m: they believe (referring to those who don't believe in Allah)

Adding the shadda on ل makes this the specific name for The One True God in Islam
Adding بِ at the beginning shows this is being used to express doing something by or with Allah

This is the basic negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This negation applies to the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) in the ayah.
The middle letter و is weak but stays because it's essential to the meaning
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'day'

steps.m: in/by the day (connected to يُؤْمِنُونَ - they believe)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to show connection and ِ at end because it follows بِ. This word is connected to يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) in the ayah.

steps.m: to come later or be delayed
steps.n: Forms active participle meaning 'last' or 'final'

Added 'the' at the beginning and kasra at the end because it describes 'the Day' in this ayah
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous phrase. This negates the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe), showing they do not believe.

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot
Added ُ to show this is doing the action, and هُ to show it belongs to Him (Allah)

steps.m: and not (connects to يُؤْمِنُونَ - they believe)

steps.m: to give freely or hand over something

steps.m: from (in relation to يُعْطُوا - they give)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows how something is given - in this case, willingly/by their own hand

steps.m: hand (something used to grasp or give)
steps.n: The root letters are reduced to just two in this word

This is a personal pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' when talking about a group of males or mixed group. It's the subject of the word صٰغِرُونَ (humbled) in this ayah.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'. Also added a sukun (ْ) at the end to show where to stop when reading.
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb showing a state or quality

steps.m: those who are humbled and lowly (describes هُمْ)

steps.m: to follow Jewish practices or traditions

steps.m: the Jews (subject of قَالَتِ - they said)

Added tanween (double damma) to show this is the subject of the verb قَالَتِ (they said)

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

steps.m: and she said (subject of عُزَيْرٌ ٱبْنُ ٱللَّهِ)

steps.m: the Christians (subject of قَالَتِ - they said)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite. This word is the subject of قَالَتِ (they said) in this ayah.

steps.m: to wipe or anoint with oil

Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject of the sentence. This is a special title for Jesus in the Quran.

The ٱ is a different way of writing اِ. The ُ shows this word is the subject of what's being said. In this ayah, it's part of what the Jews said about Uzair.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added a long vowel mark (small alif) after the second letter to extend the sound
Added 'the' to make it refer to a specific deity
The kasra shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else

steps.m: that (pointing to what was just mentioned)

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

steps.m: how (questioning the way they are being deluded in يُؤْفَكُونَ)
steps.n: Base interrogative word used to ask about manner. In this ayah, it questions how people could be so deluded to make such claims.

steps.m: how (questioning the way they are being deluded in يُؤْفَكُونَ)
steps.n: Added marks (fatha and shadda) to show this is a question word meaning 'how' - connects to يُؤْفَكُونَ to ask 'how are they being turned away?'

steps.m: how (questioning the way they are being deluded in يُؤْفَكُونَ)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ى) at the end to show the word is complete - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning or pronunciation

steps.m: to turn away from truth, to be deluded

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Added يُ prefix to make it present tense passive, with hamza getting sukun. The passive form shows someone is being affected by the action rather than doing it.
Added ون suffix to show multiple people are being affected by the action

Added vowel marks to form the singular noun for a religious scholar. This word specifically refers to Jewish religious scholars in this context.
Forms broken plural indicating multiple religious scholars. This word is what was 'taken' in the phrase 'they took their religious scholars'
Adding هُمْ at the end shows these are 'their' religious scholars. This word is what was 'taken' in the phrase 'they took their religious scholars'

steps.m: to be intensely devoted in worship from fear

steps.n: Changed from root letters to a noun by adding vowels - shows the feeling of fear
Changed from single feeling of fear to plural noun meaning monks/religious devotees
Changes word structure to match special plural noun pattern
Added pronoun suffix to show possession
Added conjunction و to connect this word to the previous one. This word is one of the objects of اتَّخَذُوا (they took/adopted)

steps.n: Root letters combined with a vowel mark (fatHa) to make the word 'lord' - like turning 'r-b-b' into 'rabb'

steps.m: lords, masters (as objects of اتَّخَذُوا - they took)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is the object of the verb اتَّخَذُوا (they took)
This is a basic preposition that shows the starting point or source of something
Added shadda (ّ) to show this word connects strongly to what comes after it. Here it connects to the word دُونِ to mean 'besides/other than'.

steps.m: to be below or less than something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'below' or 'besides'
Added kasra at the end because it's followed by اللَّهِ (Allah) in a possessive relationship
This is a unique word that became the proper name for God in Arabic. The ال here is part of the name itself, not just meaning 'the'.
Added shadda on ل and kasra at the end to show this belongs to something else. In this ayah, it shows Allah belongs to دُونِ (besides/other than).
Forms a special title for someone who has been consecrated or blessed. In this context, it specifically refers to Jesus.

steps.m: and the anointed one (title of عِيسَى/Jesus)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning and 'the' to make it definite, connecting it to the previous items in the list

steps.m: and the Messiah (object of ٱتَّخَذُوٓا۟/they took)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being taken as a lord alongside others, connecting to the verb ٱتَّخَذُوٓا۟ (they took)

Changed from 'he built' to 'son' - the word ابن (son) appears with a fatha mark because it's being acted upon by the verb اتَّخَذُوا (they took). It's part of the phrase 'son of Mary'.
This is a proper noun (name) that comes from Hebrew/Aramaic 'Maryam' meaning 'wished-for child' or 'beloved'. It doesn't come from an Arabic root.
Added fatha at the end to show Mary is the object being taken (along with priests and monks) as lords besides Allah in this verse.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb أُمِرُوٓا۟ (they were commanded) in this ayah
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this phrase with the previous statement about taking religious leaders as lords
The alif is elongated (آ) to emphasize the negation. This is a common Quranic writing style that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to order/command someone to do something

steps.n: Changed to passive voice - showing the action was done to someone instead of them doing it. This word is connected to إِلَّا (except) in this ayah.

steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people (they) were commanded. This word is connected to إِلَّا (except) in this ayah, showing what they were commanded to do.
The sukun (small circle) shows the word stops here, helping with pronunciation

The ي prefix and ُ ending change it from past to present tense

This is a negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'. It is a fixed form that does not derive from a root.
The shadda (double letter) makes the word stronger, like saying 'definitely not' instead of just 'not'. This often happens after the word 'except'.
Added fatha on ل and elongated the alif to further emphasize the negation. This elongation helps show this is an important statement of monotheism (there is NO deity except Allah).

Added fatha to show this is the object being worshipped in the phrase 'to worship one god'

steps.m: except (what comes after it from what came before it)
steps.n: This is an exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before it. It is a frozen form that doesn't follow regular morphological patterns.

steps.m: He (referring to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a personal pronoun that refers to Allah in this context. It's used to emphasize that there is no deity except Him.

Added ان to make it a special word used before Allah's name

When عَنْ combines with مَا, the ن changes to م and gets a shadda (doubled). This happens because it's easier to say عَمَّا than عَنْمَا.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb يُطْفِـُٔوا (they want to extinguish)

Added ال and merged it with the word, then added shadda to show emphasis. This makes it a proper noun for The One True God.

The fatha vowel mark shows this is a noun meaning 'mouth'

Adding ِ (kasra) to show this is part of a possession/ownership phrase (like 'mouths of')

steps.m: except, only (in this ayah: except that Allah completes His light)
steps.n: The alif is written with a special mark (آ) when it comes at the end of a word. This doesn't change the meaning, just the writing style.

steps.m: you all made yourselves heavy/lazy (clinging to comfort instead of going to battle)
steps.n: Added past tense plural 'you all' ending. In this ayah, it refers to those who were reluctant to go out for battle, preferring to stay in comfort.
Basic preposition showing direction or movement towards something. In this ayah, it connects 'you cling heavily' (اثَّاقَلْتُمْ) with 'the earth' (الْأَرْضِ) to show what they're clinging towards.
Basic noun form of 'earth' with standard vowel marks
Added ال at beginning to make it specific and ِ at end because it comes after إِلَى (to/towards)

steps.m: to be content or pleased with something

steps.n: Added vowels to make past tense. The و at the end changed to ي because it's easier to say.
Added تُم at the end to show we're talking to multiple people
Added أ to make it a question - 'Did you?' instead of just 'You'

steps.m: to live, be alive, have life

steps.n: Root meaning 'live' becomes the noun 'life' by adding و and ة to make it a thing instead of an action
Adds 'the' (ال) at the start and changes spelling slightly but means same thing
Adds 'with/by' meaning and shows connection to other words in sentence

steps.m: to be close or near to something

steps.n: Changes the root into a noun meaning 'worldly life' or 'this world'. Not related to meaning 'closer'.
Makes the noun definite
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from, originates from, or is being compared to something else
Added َ (fatha) at the end to help connect it smoothly to the next word ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ (the Hereafter). This is like adding glue between words to make them flow better when speaking.

steps.m: to come after, to be last

steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show this describes something that comes after or is last. The pattern فَاعِل makes nouns that describe who or what does something.
Added ة at the end to make this word feminine - now it describes something that is last that is feminine
Added ال to make it definite and ِ because it comes after مِنَ (from). The word refers specifically to the afterlife - the final state that comes after this worldly life.
Basic interrogative particle meaning 'what'

steps.m: then what (connects to أَرَضِيتُم - 'are you pleased')
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this question follows from and responds to the previous statement about being pleased with worldly life
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense form
Added alif after second letter to turn the verb into a noun meaning the result of the action
Changed to show this is the main subject - notice the 'u' sound at the end and the longer alif in the middle
Added basic vowel marks to connect the root letters
Added و between second and third root letters and ة at the end to make it a noun for one instance
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this word
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific
Added ِ at the end to show this word is possessed by الدُّنْيَا (worldly)

steps.c: Feminine, Adjective, Plural, Superlative, Nominative, Accusative, Genitive
steps.n: This root carries the basic idea of nearness or closeness
Changed the root letters to make 'dunya' meaning 'this world/worldly life' - comes from the idea of being close/near (in time) compared to the afterlife
Added ال to make it definite. In this ayah, it's specifically contrasted with ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ (the Hereafter) to emphasize choosing temporary worldly life over eternal life
This is a basic preposition that shows location or connects words. Here it connects 'go forth' (ٱنفِرُوا۟) with 'path' (سَبِيلِ) to say 'go forth in the path'.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Changes root to make word mean 'more/most last' or 'final' - follows special pattern for comparisons
Adding ة makes this word refer specifically to the afterlife - the final/last life after this one
Adds definite article to make the noun definite

steps.m: of the afterlife (compared to الدُّنْيَا - worldly life)
steps.n: The kasra shows this word follows مِنَ (from/of) when comparing the afterlife to worldly life
This is a complete particle that doesn't derive from a root. It's used here to show that what comes after it (قَلِيلٌ meaning 'little') is an exception to what was mentioned before.
The ي in the middle makes it an adjective describing something that has the quality of being small in amount

steps.m: a little, a few (describing متٰعُ - enjoyment)
steps.n: The double damma (ٌ) at the end shows this word is describing something (in this case, how little the enjoyment is)

steps.m: unless (followed by تَنفِرُوا - you go forth)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that introduces an exception or condition. Here it connects to تَنفِرُوا (you go forth) to mean 'unless you go forth'.

steps.m: to run away or flee from something

steps.n: Added fathas to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added وا at the end to make it a command for a group (like saying 'all of you go forth!')
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The shadda doubles the middle letter to show the action is stronger or more intense
Adding ي prefix creates present tense masculine singular form

steps.m: he will punish you all severely (if you don't go forth)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show who receives the punishment. This connects to the previous phrase 'if you don't go forth' (إِلَّا تَنفِرُوا۟)

Added ًا at the end to show this is the thing being described as painful (أَلِيمًا) in the phrase 'a painful punishment'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (causing pain) to a strong adjective (painful) that describes how severe the punishment is
Adds tanween to show this adjective follows and describes عَذَابًا (punishment)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added است with proper vowel marks to show seeking to do the action
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start. The sukun at the end shows it comes after another word that affects it

steps.m: and he seeks to replace (connected to يُعَذِّبْكُمْ - he will punish you)
steps.n: Added و to connect this verb with the previous punishment mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: to stand upright or rise up

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters. The root meaning of standing together led to meaning a group of people who unite as one.

steps.m: a people (object of يَسْتَبْدِلْ - will replace)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of replacement. Allah will replace these people with others if they don't go forth.

steps.m: to change or be different from

steps.n: Basic noun form showing something being different or other than something else

steps.m: other than you all (object of يَسْتَبْدِلْ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this refers to multiple people being addressed. The word maintains its role as the object of يَسْتَبْدِلْ (will replace).

Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense. The middle letter gets doubled (shadda) because this is an emphasized form.

Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being acted upon in the phrase 'you will not harm Him anything'

The shadda shows that the ل is doubled for emphasis
The damma at the end shows that Allah is the subject being described as قَدِيرٌ (capable/able) in this ayah
The و at the beginning connects this part to what came before it in the ayah
The alif changes to a waṣla (ٱ) because it comes after another word
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, over, or upon something else. Here it connects to the words كُلِّ شَىْءٍ (everything) to say 'over everything'.
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show how to pronounce the word correctly. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added damma (ُ) and doubled the middle letter to make it mean 'every' or 'all'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, making it mean 'of every thing'
Connected the letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make the basic noun form. The ء at the end shows this is a complete thing.

steps.m: any thing (in the phrase 'not harm Him in any thing')

Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from a past tense verb (he had power) to an adjective meaning 'very powerful' by adding ي and changing the middle vowel
Added tanween dammah to show this is describing Allah's general attribute of being All-Powerful
This is a basic word that means 'except'. In this ayah, it's used before the phrase 'you help him' to mean 'if you do not help him'. It's like saying 'except you help him' in old English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
The تَ at the start shows it's happening now (present tense)
The و at the end shows multiple people are doing the action
The ه suffix indicates 3rd person masculine singular object (him)
This is a basic particle that adds emphasis or certainty to what follows

steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who received the help. In this ayah, this word shows how Allah (the subject) helped Muhammad ﷺ (shown by هُ) when he was driven out.

steps.m: when (referring to the time he was in the cave)

Added ين to make it plural, showing we're talking about multiple people or things

Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: to make something second or to double

steps.n: Changes to active participle form to show who does the action

steps.m: to double or make into two

steps.n: Root ث-ن-ي became the number 'two' by adding ٱ (al) and putting sukun on ث

This is the basic third person masculine singular pronoun

Added يَ at start to show it's happening now (present), middle vowel changed to ُ, and added ُ at end to show 'he' is doing it. Speaking to his friend.
Basic meaning of companionship
Added vowels to show who does the action - a companion is someone who accompanies
Added لِ at beginning to mean 'to' or 'for' the companion
Added هِ at the end to show this is his companion. This is who Abu Bakr is speaking to in the cave.

Changed from 'he' to 'you' by adding ت at start

Adds the nominative case ending because this word is the subject of هِىَ (it is) in this ayah

Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to or describes كَلِمَةُ (word/decree)

Changed و to ي and added ا at the end to make it feminine superlative form

Added tanween (double damma) at the end to show this is describing Allah in this ayah
This is the basic past tense form of the verb

Added وا to make it a command for multiple people

steps.n: Forms broken plural indicating multiple heavy/burdened people. In this ayah, it describes the believers who find it difficult to go to battle.

steps.m: to travel or move along a way

Adding ل with kasra (ِ) makes the word stronger and more emphatic

The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb. This word comes after لَوْ (if) in this ayah, showing a hypothetical past situation.

steps.m: to show up, appear, or present itself unexpectedly
something temporary or worldly that shows up
steps.n: Added vowels to make it a noun referring to temporary worldly things like money or goods

steps.m: near (describing عَرَضًا as something easily reached)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is describing how the عَرَضًا (gain/goods) is - it's easily reachable or close by

Added وا at the end to show many people did the action
Added ك at end to show you are being followed
