Added emphasis by doubling ل and changing spelling of ا to ٱ
This is a particle that introduces contrast or exception

steps.m: and but/however (connects to لَّٱتَّبَعُوكَ - they would have followed you)
steps.n: The و connects this to the previous statement about 'they would have followed you', showing contrast
Added vowel marks to make it mean 'he became far away' - like adding motion to a basic word
Changed vowel pattern to show this is a natural state or quality rather than an action done to something

steps.m: it/she was naturally distant/far away (describing الشُّقَّةُ - the distance)
steps.n: Added feminine marker ت with sukun to show this describes الشُّقَّةُ (the distance) which is feminine
Basic preposition showing something being on top of or over something else
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun
Added هِمُ to show this applies to a group of people (them)
Root letters ش-ق-ق became شَقّ by adding vowel marks and doubling middle letter to make a noun meaning hardship
Changed first vowel to damma and added feminine ending to create noun for specific instance
Added definite article ٱل to specify this particular journey
Added damma ending to show this is the subject of the verb بَعُدَتْ (became distant)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adds plural marker to indicate multiple subjects
Adds س (sa) at the start to show it will happen in the future

steps.m: and they will swear/take an oath
steps.n: Connects this action to what came before it in the verse
This is the unique name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity and worship, but has become a proper name.

steps.m: by/with Allah (object of يَحْلِفُونَ - they will swear)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show they are swearing by Allah's name, and ِ at the end to connect it to the verb يَحْلِفُونَ (they will swear). The ٱ is just a different way to write the same ال sound.
This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations

steps.m: if (hypothetical condition that didn't happen, connects to لَخَرَجْنَا - we would have gone out)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show pronunciation. This word introduces the first part of a conditional statement that continues with لَخَرَجْنَا (we would have gone out), showing what they claim they would have done in an impossible situation.

steps.m: to be able, to have capability

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding the vowel 'aa' in middle and 'a' at end
Added است at beginning to show achieving or gaining ability
Removed long alif and added sukun before adding suffix
Added نا to show 'we' did the action, and changed initial alif to wasl form
Added vowels to make past tense verb
Added نا to show 'we' did the action

steps.m: we would have gone out (in response to لَوِ/if)
steps.n: Added لَ at beginning to show this is the result of a conditional statement starting with لَوِ (if)
This is an adverb of accompaniment
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is addressing multiple people. In this ayah, it's specifically addressing the hypocrites who are making excuses.
Added alif at beginning to make the action causative - showing someone making the destruction happen
Changed to present tense by: 1) adding يُ at start, 2) adding ِ after second letter, and 3) adding ُ at end
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, they are destroying their own selves (أَنفُسَهُمْ)

steps.m: to be precious, to be valuable

steps.n: Root letters got vowels (َ and ْ) to make the common noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'
Added hamza at beginning and changed middle vowel to make plural form

steps.m: their selves/souls (objects being destroyed in يُهْلِكُونَ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show possession. This word is the object (thing being acted upon) in the phrase 'they destroy themselves'
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the doer of the action in يَعْلَمُ (He knows)
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement, like saying 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first letter, and adding ُ at end. In this ayah, Allah is the one who knows.
This is an emphatic particle that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English. Here it emphasizes that they are liars.
Added هُمْ (hum) at the end to show who is being talked about - 'they/them'. This connects to the word لَكٰذِبُونَ (liars) to say 'indeed they are liars'
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to show someone who does the action
Added ending to show it's talking about many men
Added لَ at the beginning to emphasize that they are definitely liars
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb meaning 'he forgave'
Changed final weak letter و to ا which is common in past tense verbs
Added ال to specify THE one God, and the hamza merged with the lam creating a double lam
Added shadda on lam to show emphasis, damma at end to show this is the doer of the action عَفَا (pardoned), and changed alif to wasl alif since it connects with previous word
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added كَ at the end to show this applies to 'you' (when speaking to one male). This word connects to عَفَا (pardoned) to show who was pardoned.
Starting with the two separate parts: لِ (for) and ما (what)
The ما is shortened to مَ when combined with لِ to make a question word. This word is asking about the permission mentioned in أَذِنتَ (you permitted).
Adding vowels to make it a complete past tense verb meaning 'he permitted'

steps.m: you gave permission (addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action. This word is part of Allah's gentle question to the Prophet ﷺ about why he gave permission to some people.
This is a particle that shows direction or purpose. Here it connects to the word أَذِنتَ (you permitted) to show who was permitted.

steps.m: to/for them (object of أَذِنتَ - you permitted them)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which refers to a group of males being discussed. This shows who was being permitted.
This is a basic particle that shows limits or endpoints. Starting with the basic letters connected with fatha.
The shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing a definite endpoint.
The ىٰ (alif maqsura) at the end makes this word connect what comes before it with what comes after it. Here it connects the previous action with يَتَبَيَّنَ (becomes clear).
Added vowels to make it past tense - 'it became clear'
Adding تَ at start shows something happening by itself
Added ي at beginning to show present tense. This verb comes after حَتَّىٰ (until) in the ayah, showing something that hasn't happened yet.
This is a preposition that shows direction or purpose. Here it connects to the verb أَذِنتَ (you permitted).

steps.m: to you (object of أَذِنتَ - you permitted)
steps.n: The كَ at the end shows this is happening to 'you' (when talking to one male person). In this ayah, Allah is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ about why he gave permission to certain people.
Added kasra and ي to form the basic relative pronoun
Added نَ to make it plural, referring to multiple people
Added ٱلْ at beginning which combines with ذ to create ٱلَّ (with shadda). This word points to the specific people who are the subject of صَدَقُوا (they spoke truth).

steps.m: to be truthful, to tell the truth

steps.n: Changed from root letters to a past tense verb meaning 'he was truthful' by adding the vowel marks
Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding وا at the end
Added superscript circle (۟) as pronunciation guide for plural ending
Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to change from 'he' to 'you', and changed vowels to make it present tense instead of past

steps.m: and you know (connected to يَتَبَيَّنَ - becomes clear)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this verb with the previous verb يَتَبَيَّنَ (becomes clear)
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes from describing the action to describing the person who does it
Makes the word plural to show many liars. This word is what is being known in the phrase 'and you know the liars'

steps.m: the liars (object of تَعْلَمَ - you know)
steps.n: Adds 'the' to specify these particular liars, and adds the final vowel mark to show this is being acted upon by the verb 'know'

This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it (يَسْتَـْٔذِنُكَ - 'they ask permission') into its opposite meaning ('they do not ask permission'). It has no root since it's a basic particle.
Added fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how it's pronounced
Added يَسْتَ at the beginning to show someone is asking for permission instead of giving it
Added كَ at the end to show who is being asked for permission
This is the basic form meaning 'who' for one person
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding ال makes it point to specific people being talked about

steps.m: those who (subject of يُؤْمِنُونَ - they believe)
steps.n: The word gets special marks: a different 'a' at start (ٱ), a double-l sound (ّ), and an 'a' sound (-َ) at end. Points to 'they believe' later in the verse.
Changed from basic root meaning 'safety/trust' to Form IV verb meaning 'to believe/have faith' by adding hamza and alif at start
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start. The hamza changed position and got a sukun because of this change.
Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity
Added shadda on ل to emphasize this is the one true God, not just any deity
Changed initial alif to wasl form since it connects with previous word
Added بِ at beginning to show this is the means/instrument of believing, and kasra at end to connect it grammatically. This word shows what the believers (يُؤْمِنُونَ) have faith in.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make a proper word
The 'و' at the start means 'and'

steps.m: and the day (connected to بِٱللَّهِ - by Allah)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to 'by Allah' earlier in the verse
Changes from basic root meaning 'delay/after' to mean 'the last one' by following a pattern for describing things
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the last' instead of just 'last'. This word is describing 'Day' in the phrase 'the Last Day'.

steps.m: the last, the final (describing 'Day' in 'the Last Day')
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word is describing 'Day' (اليوم) in the phrase 'the Last Day'

This is a connecting particle that joins two parts of a sentence. Here it connects the verb يَسْتَـْٔذِنُكَ (ask permission) with يُجٰهِدُوا۟ (they strive), showing what they don't ask permission for.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to show mutual or intensive effort
Added يُ at start to show present tense and changed middle vowel to kasra
Added وا at the end to show plural (they) and changed alif to alif with overbar
Root letters م-و-ل combined to make مال (māl), a common word for money/wealth
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple properties or amounts of wealth
Added هِمْ to show possession by a group (referring to the believers mentioned in the ayah)

steps.m: with their wealth (in يُجٰهِدُوا - they strive)
steps.n: Added بِ to show how they strive - with/using their wealth. This connects to the verb يُجٰهِدُوا (they strive) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a basic noun form
Changed to broken plural form by adding أ at start and changing vowels
Added kasra at the end to prepare for possessive pronoun attachment
Added possessive pronoun هِمْ meaning 'their'
Added conjunction و (and) at beginning
This is Allah's proper name, which is treated as a complete word rather than being derived from a root
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show Allah is the subject doing the action in this sentence

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject of عَلِيمٌۢ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and', and added hamzat wasl (ٱ) which helps pronounce the word when it comes after و
Added vowel marks to connect the letters into a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: one who knows deeply (as an eternal quality)
steps.n: Added ي to show this is a permanent quality of Allah, not just a temporary action
Added two dots (tanween) above the م to show this is a complete description of Allah. The small م shows it's the end of the word.

steps.m: to protect/guard against harm or evil

steps.n: Added مُ at the start to show 'one who does the protecting' (like adding -er in English to protect→protector)

steps.m: one who is consciously protective against evil
steps.n: Added ت and doubled middle letter to show doing action to oneself - in this context meaning being consciously protective against evil/wrong

steps.m: those who are consciously protective against evil
steps.n: Changes from singular (one person) to plural (many people) by adding ين at the end

steps.m: the ones who are consciously protective against evil
steps.n: Added 'ال' (the) at the start to make it specific/definite - like changing from 'righteous people' to 'the righteous people'

steps.m: with the God-conscious ones (object of يَسْتَـْٔذِنُكَ)
steps.n: Added ب to show 'with'. This word is the object that completes the meaning of يَسْتَـْٔذِنُكَ (ask permission from you) in this ayah.
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning of certainty
Adding ما changes the meaning from 'indeed' to 'only'. This combination restricts what comes after it, like saying 'nothing but' or 'only' in English.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to permit'
Added است at beginning to show seeking or requesting permission
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he seeks your permission (doer of لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ)
steps.n: Added ك at end to show whose permission is being sought. This word describes those who don't believe (لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ)
The basic word ذا (meaning 'this') changes to ذي to create a word that connects descriptions to people or things, like 'who' or 'which' in English
Adding ال makes it more specific, like adding 'the' in English
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one person
Added proper spelling and pronunciation marks. This word describes the people who don't believe (لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ) in the verse.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe), making it mean 'they do not believe'
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation

steps.m: to trust, feel secure in something

steps.n: Root أمن becomes present tense 'he believes' by adding يُ at start. The hamza gets a seat (ؤ) to help pronounce it.
Adds plural ending to show multiple people who don't believe, connecting to لَا (not) in the verse
This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Islam.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added بِ at start to show 'by/with', added ٱ to help pronunciation, and ِ at end to connect it to the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe). This shows who they don't believe in.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a proper word
Adding definite article changes the word from indefinite to definite
Adding conjunction prefix to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: and the day (connected to بِٱللَّهِ - 'in Allah')
steps.n: Added kasra (i-sound) at the end to show it's connected to 'in Allah' earlier in the sentence

steps.m: to come after or behind something

steps.n: Changed the root into a word meaning 'last' by adding a hamza with fatha (ءَ) at the start and a kasra (ِ) in the middle
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added kasra at the end to show this word describes 'day' (يَوْمِ) in the phrase 'the Last Day'

steps.m: to doubt or be uncertain about something

steps.n: Added 'ta' and 'alif' to the root to show someone actively becoming doubtful about something
Added ت at the end to show a female subject did the action

steps.m: and she became doubtful (subject of يَتَرَدَّدُونَ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it. This word describes the subject of يَتَرَدَّدُونَ (they waver/hesitate) in this ayah.

steps.m: to turn over, to flip (especially referring to hearts changing state)

steps.n: Root meaning 'turn over' becomes the noun 'heart' - like how hearts can turn and change feelings
Changed to broken plural pattern by adding و and changing vowels
Added final damma to show this is the subject of وَٱرْتَابَتْ (became doubtful)
Added هُمْ to show possession - these are the hearts of those who don't believe in Allah and the Last Day
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' (for a group of males or mixed group)
Added diacritical marks to show proper pronunciation
Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result or consequence. This pronoun is the subject of the verb يَتَرَدَّدُونَ (they waver/hesitate) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects to رَيْبِهِمْ (their doubt) to show they are 'in their doubt'.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to doubt or be uncertain about something

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to doubt' into the noun 'doubt'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it
Added هِمْ at the end to show the doubt belongs to them

steps.m: to waver back and forth, to hesitate

steps.n: Adding fatha vowels creates the basic past tense active verb form
Adding تَ at start and doubling middle letter shows the action is done repeatedly to oneself
Adding present tense prefix for 3rd person masculine singular

steps.m: they hesitate/waver (referring to الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ - those who do not believe)
steps.n: The plural ending ون shows this refers to a group. In this ayah, it describes the actions of those who don't believe.
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces hypothetical scenarios. It's different from إِنْ which is used for more likely conditions.
Adding وَ (and) at the beginning connects this condition to the previous statement. The combination وَلَوْ is stronger than just لَوْ alone - it means 'even if' or 'although'.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create basic past tense verb
Adding أ (hamza) at the start makes the wanting more active/intentional
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action. This word is connected to لَأَعَدُّوا (they would have prepared) in the ayah.
Changed the verb 'to exit' into a noun meaning 'exiting' by adding vowels and و
Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about a specific instance of exiting (related to أَرَادُوا۟ - they wanted)

steps.m: the exiting (object of أَرَادُوا۟ - they wanted)
steps.n: Added َ at the end because this word is the object of أَرَادُوا۟ (they wanted)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding alif makes it mean 'to cause/make something happen'
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action

steps.m: they would have prepared (in response to أَرَادُوا)
steps.n: Added لَ at beginning to show this is what would have happened if they had wanted to go out, connecting to the word أَرَادُوا (they wanted) earlier in the verse
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added هُ (with damma) at the end to show this means 'him'
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed first vowel to damma and added ة to make it mean the tools/equipment for something
Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being prepared in the phrase 'they would have made preparation'
This is a basic contrasting word that shows the next part will be different from what came before

steps.m: and but/however (connects while contrasting with أَرَادُوا)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this contrasting statement with the previous thought. Here it connects to أَرَادُوا (they wanted) to show a contrast between what they wanted and what actually happened.

steps.m: to dislike or hate something strongly
he disliked (subject of فَثَبَّطَهُمْ - then He discouraged them)
steps.n: Added vowels to show this happened in the past and was done by 'he'. This word connects to فَثَبَّطَهُمْ (then He discouraged them) in the ayah, where Allah is the one who disliked.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Changed to the unique name of the one true God by adding ٱل (the), doubling the ل, and adding ُ to show this is the subject of كَرِهَ (He disliked)

steps.m: to send forth, to raise up

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb

steps.m: he was sent forth/he went forth
steps.n: Added ان at beginning to show the action happens on its own (like 'was sent' instead of 'sent')

steps.m: the act of being sent forth
steps.n: Changed to a noun showing the action itself rather than who did it

steps.m: their going forth (object of كَرِهَ/he disliked)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this belongs to them. This word is what Allah disliked in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks and doubled the middle letter ب to show strong discouragement (like saying 'really discouraged' in English)
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was discouraged (them)
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of what came before (Allah disliked their going forth, and so as a result, He discouraged them)
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that someone or something else did the speaking (passive voice)

steps.m: and it was said (connects to فَثَبَّطَهُمْ - then He discouraged them)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this word to the previous action in the verse

steps.m: to sit or remain in place

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he sat' to 'sit!' by adding u- at start and changing middle vowel to make it a command
Changes command from singular to plural by adding وا at the end and changing the alif to hamzat wasl
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows being together with something or someone

steps.m: with (the قٰعِدِينَ - those who remain behind)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. This preposition connects to قٰعِدِينَ (those who remain behind) to show who they should stay with.

steps.m: to sit or remain in place

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed pattern to show who does the action
Added ين to make it plural, referring to multiple people who sit/remain
Added ال to make it specific and added final vowel because it comes after the word مَعَ (with)
This is a basic conditional word that introduces scenarios that didn't actually happen. It's like saying 'if' when talking about something that could have happened but didn't.
Added vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce the word - َ (fatha) on ل and ْ (sukun) on و

steps.m: to leave, exit, or go out

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb meaning one male person did the action
Added وا at the end and changed the last vowel to ُ to show that a group of people (they) did the action. This word comes after لَوْ (if) in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing location or containment. Since this is a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
Added kasra vowel mark to show proper pronunciation

steps.m: among you all (connected to سَمَّاعُونَ - listeners)
steps.n: Added كُم which means 'you all' when attached to a word. In this ayah, it's describing that there are listeners (سَمَّاعُونَ) among the group being addressed.
This is a basic negative particle that means 'not'
The shadda (double mark) on م makes the negation stronger. This word negates زَادُوكُمْ (they would increase you) in this ayah, showing they would not increase you at all.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to show this happened in the past
Added و and ُ at the end to show a group did this action

steps.m: they increased you all (object of يَبْغُونَ - they seek)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who received the action. This word describes how 'they' would affect 'you all' in the larger phrase where they are seeking to cause trouble.

steps.m: except (makes an exception from زَادُوكُمْ/they would have increased you)
steps.n: Added kasra under إ to show this is a particle that makes exceptions. In this ayah, it's saying they would NOT have increased you except in causing trouble.

steps.m: except (makes an exception from زَادُوكُمْ/they would have increased you)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation

steps.m: damage/harm (object of زادوكم - they increased you)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun showing the harmful result. This word is what they would be increased in, connected to زادوكم (they increased you).

steps.m: in damage/harm (object of زادوكم - they increased you)
steps.n: Added ًا (tanween) at the end to show this is a direct object - the thing being increased
Adding أ at start makes it mean 'to make someone do the action'
وا at the end means a group of men did the action

steps.m: surely they would have made haste
steps.n: Added لَ at beginning to show emphasis and certainty

steps.m: and surely they would have made haste
steps.n: Added وَ at beginning to connect this phrase with what came before

steps.m: to pass through or between things

steps.n: Root خ-ل-ل becomes خِلَالَ (through/among) by adding vowels. Shows where the rushing happened - between/through people
Added pronoun 'you all' to show who is being passed through/among. The alif is written as alif maqsura (ٰ) when كُمْ is attached.

steps.m: to seek or desire something eagerly

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed final ي to و to prepare for adding نَ to make it plural (they seek)
Added نَ to show multiple people doing the action
Added كُمُ to show who they are seeking (you all)
Changed the word from an action into a thing by adding ة at the end and changing the vowels

steps.m: the division/conflict (object of يَبْغُونَكُمُ - they seek among you)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and َ at the end to show it's being acted upon by the verb يَبْغُونَكُمُ (they seek)
Basic preposition showing location or presence within something
Added conjunction to connect this phrase with previous statement

steps.m: and among you all (referring to the believers being addressed)
steps.n: Added plural 'you' pronoun to show who is being addressed. This refers to the believers being talked about in the ayah.
The doubled م (with shadda) and added ا show this person does a lot of listening

steps.m: those who listen intensively (to spread gossip)
steps.n: The ون ending makes this plural, referring to multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to those who eagerly listen to spread gossip and cause discord.
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose. Here it connects to the verb يَبْغُونَ (they seek) to show who is being affected.
Added pronoun 'them' to show who the action is directed towards. This word shows who is being affected by the seeking (يَبْغُونَ) mentioned in the ayah.
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. The word comes from the root meaning 'deity' but has become a unique name.
Added shadda to show the doubled 'l' sound
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in عَلِيمٌۢ (All-Knowing)

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of عَلِيمٌۢ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of عَلِيمٌۢ)
steps.n: Changed alif to wasl form since it's not pronounced separately after و

steps.m: one who is deeply knowledgeable (describing اللَّهُ)
steps.n: The pattern with ي shows someone who has this quality as a permanent, deep characteristic. Here it describes Allah's perfect knowledge.
The tanween shows this is one of Allah's permanent attributes or names
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
This pattern shows someone who does the action - like adding '-er' in English (teach → teacher)
The ين ending makes it plural (more than one wrongdoer)
Adding ال makes it specific - these particular unjust ones
Adding بِ shows this is about being with or among these people
The base word قَد is a particle that shows something definitely happened or is happening. Adding ِ helps connect it to the next word.
Added لَ at the beginning to make the meaning stronger, like adding 'most certainly' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show doing action thoroughly
Changed اِ to ٱ and added وا at the end to show many people did this action
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and change it from an action to a thing
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific instance of testing or trial
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific. The َ at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb ٱبْتَغَوُا۟ (they sought)
This is a basic preposition that connects to the word قَبْلُ (before) to show something happened from before or previously
Root meaning 'to come before' becomes a word meaning 'before' - like changing from a verb to a time word
The ُ at the end shows this word is the main topic being discussed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: to turn something over and over
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter shows doing the action many times or with force
Adding وا shows many people (they) did the action
Adding conjunction و meaning 'and'
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose
Added ك at the end to show this is directed to a single person (you)
Changed from root letters to a noun meaning 'matter' by adding vowels (fat-ha and sukoon)
Makes the word plural to show multiple matters or affairs
Added definite article to specify these particular matters
Added fatha at end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb قَلَّبُوا (they turned/manipulated)
Basic form of this preposition that shows when something ends
Added vowel marks and doubled the middle letter to make the meaning stronger
Added ى at the end to show this word means 'until' and leads into what happens next (connects to 'came')
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he came' in the past tense
Changed to standard spelling of past tense 'came' by adding alif
Alternative spelling showing the extended sound of 'came'
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'truth'
Added the definite article ٱلْ to specify 'the truth'
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb جَآءَ (came) in this ayah

steps.m: it became apparent (subject of أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Basic past tense verb form with fatha voweling. This verb is describing what happened to 'the command of Allah' (أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ) in this ayah.

steps.m: and it became apparent (subject of أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Adding و at the beginning connects this with the previous statements about how they tried to cause problems (ٱبْتَغَوُا۟ ٱلْفِتْنَةَ) until the truth came (حَتَّىٰ جَآءَ ٱلْحَقُّ) and Allah's command became apparent.
Added vowel marks to turn the action into a noun. This word is connected to اللَّهِ (Allah) showing it's Allah's command being discussed.
Added damma (u-sound) at the end because this word is the subject/doer in the sentence
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed from the general word for 'god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال (al) and combining letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation - damma (ُ) shows it's the subject form of 'they', and sukun (ْ) shows where to stop
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word is the subject (doer) of كٰرِهُونَ (disliking) in this ayah.
Adding vowel marks to show this is a past tense feeling verb
Added alif after first letter to show who does the action
Changed alif to alif with bar on top (كٰ) - this is just a writing variation that means the same thing
Added ون to make it plural - shows this describes multiple people who hate
This is a common preposition meaning 'from'. The ْ (sukun) shows the 'n' sound stops sharply.

steps.m: from them (referring to some of the hypocrites)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers to some of the hypocrites who were making excuses.

steps.m: and from them (connected to previous discussion of hypocrites)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this to the previous discussion about different types of hypocrites
This is a special word used to ask about or refer to people in a general way, like saying 'who' or 'whoever' in English
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound. In this ayah, this word refers to the person who is doing the saying (يَقُولُ)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: he says (introduces what someone says in this ayah)
steps.n: Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels. The ُ at end shows who is doing the action
This is the basic concept of giving permission
Adding vowels makes this a past tense verb meaning 'he gave permission'
Changed to command form by adding ٱ at start, putting sukun on ذ, and changing vowels. The أ becomes ئ because it comes after the command prefix.
This is a particle showing direction or purpose. In this ayah, it's used with اذن (permit) to show who the permission is for.
The shadda adds emphasis to show importance or certainty

steps.m: surely for me (connected to اذن - permit)
steps.n: The ى at the end shows this is happening to 'me'. This connects to the word اذن (permit) to mean 'permit me'
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb تَفْتِنِّىٓ (test/tempt me) into a negative - 'do not test/tempt me'
Added 'and' to connect this negative command to the previous request ٱئْذَن لِّى (permit me). The person is saying 'permit me and do not test me'

steps.m: to test or put through trials
steps.n: This means to put someone through a test or trial
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Changed to present tense with 'you' as the doer
Doubled the last letter to show stronger/repeated action
Added ى to show the testing is happening to 'me'
Added elongation mark (ٓ) to help with pronunciation
أَ is used to draw attention or start a question
Adding لَا changes it from just attention to a strong warning or emphasis. In this ayah, it's warning about falling into فِتْنَة (trial/temptation).
This is a preposition that shows location or being inside something. In this ayah, it connects to the word ٱلْفِتْنَةِ (trial/temptation) to show 'in the trial'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he tested' (verb) to 'a test/trial' (noun) by following a special pattern
Adds definite article to make noun definite
Adds -i sound at end because it comes after the word في (in/at/on)
This is the basic action of falling down
Connected the root letters together with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb

steps.m: they fell down (subject of يَقُولُ - who says)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word refers to the same group who are speaking (يَقُولُ) in this verse.
This is a special word used to emphasize what comes after it. The shadda (double letter mark) makes it stronger.

steps.m: and indeed (connects to سَقَطُوا۟ - they fell)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this statement to the previous one where it says 'they fell' (سَقَطُوا۟)
This is a proper noun for the place of punishment in the afterlife. It doesn't come from an Arabic root - it's believed to come from Hebrew 'Gehenna'.
Added vowel marks and shadda to show proper pronunciation. The fatha at the end shows this word is being described by what comes after it (محيطة - encompassing).
This means to completely surround something
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding the vowel 'aa' in middle and 'a' at end
Changes verb to active participle (someone/something that does the action)
Makes the word refer to a feminine subject
Adds emphatic لَ to show certainty (Hell will definitely surround)
Adds tanween to show this is the subject of إِنَّ (indeed/verily)
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from verb (he rejected) to noun (one who rejects)
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple people

steps.m: with those who reject/cover up truth
steps.n: Adds the preposition بِ to show accompaniment

steps.m: with the ones who reject/cover up truth
steps.n: Makes it specific by adding the definite article

steps.m: with the ones who reject/cover up truth
steps.n: Adds fatha at the end to show the word's role in the sentence
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - it tells us what happens if something occurs. Here it introduces what happens if something befalls the Prophet (تُصِبْكَ).
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle letter from ا to ي
Changes from 'he' to 'she' by replacing ي with ت at start
The middle letter ي is removed and the last vowel changes to a stop mark (sukun) because it comes after 'if/when'
Added كَ to show that 'you' are the one being affected
Added vowels to connect the letters and added ة to make it feminine, creating a noun meaning 'good thing'

steps.m: a good thing (subject of تُصِبْ - befalls/happens to)
steps.n: Added tanween (ٌ) to show this is indefinite (a good thing vs the good thing) and is doing the action of befalling/happening to someone
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show 'she/it' and changed vowels to make it present tense 'makes sad' instead of past tense 'made sad'
Added sukun at end to show this is jussive mood (like after لَمْ)
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being made sad
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - it shows that something might happen

steps.m: and if (connects to تُصِبْكَ - befalls you)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement. This word introduces the second part of two related conditions about good and bad things happening.
Added vowels (َ) to make it 'he struck' in the past tense
Added تُ at start to make it 'she/it' in present tense, middle letter و changed to ي, and vowels changed to match present tense pattern
Final vowel removed to show 'if/when' meaning

steps.m: she/it strikes/affects you (object of إِن if/when)
steps.n: Added كَ to show the person being affected. This word follows إِن (if/when) in the ayah.
Added مُ prefix and kasra (ِ) in middle to make it mean 'one who hits/strikes' - like changing 'hit' to 'hitter'
The weak letter و changes to ي due to the kasra before it
Added ة to make it a noun meaning the result of the action
Added tanween (double damma) to make it indefinite
Connected the root letters with vowels. The middle و becomes ا because it's a weak letter.
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا returns to و.
Changes from singular (he) to plural (they) by adding وا at the end. The final ُ is dropped because of Arabic grammar rules.
This is a particle that adds emphasis when used with past tense verbs like أَخَذْنَآ (we took). It shows the action definitely happened.
Added fatha on ق and sukun on د to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of

steps.n: Added vowels (fat-ha) to make it 'he took' in the past tense
Changed final vowel to sukun to prepare for suffix
Added نَآ at the end to show 'we' did the action
Root letters أ-م-ر become the noun 'matter/affair' by adding marks (fatha-sukun)
Added نَا at the end to show possession (our) and fatha before it to show this is the object of the verb أَخَذْنَا (we took)
This is a preposition showing the starting point in time - it connects with قَبْلُ to mean 'from before'
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how the word is pronounced
Changes from root to basic noun meaning 'before'
Adds damma ending to show this is the subject of the sentence
Connected root letters with vowels to form basic past tense verb
Added reflexive meaning showing the person does the action to themselves
Changed to present tense
Changed to plural form
Added conjunction prefix to connect to previous phrase
This is a pronoun meaning 'they' (masculine plural). The ُ shows it's in subject form and مْ shows it's plural.
Added و with fatha (َ) meaning 'and'
Added shadda (ّ) on و to emphasize the connection
Added vowels to make it past tense - 'he became happy' instead of just the basic idea of happiness

steps.m: they are joyful/happy (describing the hypocrites in this ayah)
steps.n: Changed to plural form to show multiple people are happy. In this ayah, it's describing the hypocrites who are happy when something bad happens to the Prophet ﷺ.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form by adding damma on first letter and dropping the weak middle letter و. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to say what follows in the ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes something impossible in the future. It is a fixed form that does not derive from a root.
Adding the shadda makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic. This word negates يُصِيبَنَآ (it will reach us) to say something will absolutely never happen.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يُ at the start to show present tense. The middle letter changed to ي. After لَّن it means 'will never reach'.
Added نَآ at the end to show who is being reached (us). The long alif (آ) shows we're stopping at this word when reading.
This is a basic word that shows an exception to what came before it. In this ayah, it connects to 'what Allah wrote for us' to show that nothing will happen to us except what Allah has written.
This word connects what comes before it to what comes after it. Here it connects with كَتَبَ (wrote) to mean 'that which was written'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is something Allah did in the past. This word is connected to اللَّهُ (Allah) who is doing the writing/decreeing.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject/doer
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific/definite
Added fatha to the letter ل to show its pronunciation
Added نا which means 'us' to show who the action is for. This word connects to كَتَبَ (wrote) to show what Allah wrote for us.
This is a personal pronoun that refers to what Allah has written for us (ما كتب الله لنا). It's the subject of the word مَوْلَىٰنَا (our protector) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be close to and support

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding vowel marks (fatha)
Added مَ at the beginning to show who does the action, and changed the middle of the word to show it's a role or title
Added نَا at the end to show this protector belongs to or supports us. This word describes هُوَ (He) in the sentence.
Basic form of the preposition على which shows something being on top of or upon something else
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) which comes after it in the ayah.
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' as a proper name by adding ال and merging letters
Added the kasra at the end to show this is being acted upon by another word. Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form. In this ayah, Allah is the one who wrote/decreed.

steps.m: to trust or depend on someone/something

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha) and doubled the middle letter (kaaf) to make it mean 'he entrusted' in the past tense
Added ت at beginning to show trusting/depending on someone else (usually Allah) rather than others
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Added فَلْ at beginning to mean 'so let' and changed the ending vowel to show command form
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Changed to show someone who believes regularly or as part of their character

steps.m: the believer (one of those who trust in Allah)
steps.n: Added 'Al' (ال) at the start to mean 'the' - makes it specific instead of any believer

steps.m: the believers (those who put their trust in Allah)
steps.n: Shows this refers to multiple believers. In this ayah, it refers to those who trust in what Allah has written for them.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.
This is a question word that turns a statement into a yes/no question, like adding a question mark in English. Here it's asking the person being commanded (قُلْ - say!) if they are doing something.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The ت at start and shadda in middle show doing the action yourself with extra effort - waiting deliberately
Adds masculine plural suffix to indicate 'you all'
Base pronoun meaning 'us'. This pronoun is being acted upon by the verb يُصِيبَ (to afflict/strike) in this ayah.
Added the preposition بِ which shows how something is done. Here it shows that something will happen through or by means of us.
Added the elongated alif (آ) at the end for pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

This is a basic particle used to show exception - it means whatever comes after it is different from what came before. Here it connects to تَرَبَّصُونَ (you await) to show that what follows is the only thing being awaited.
The alif at the end is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's being stretched in pronunciation, but this doesn't change the meaning.
Changed the first letter to إِ and added sukun on middle letter to prepare for feminine form
Added ى at the end with fatha before it to make it feminine and show it means 'one of' something. In this ayah, it refers to one of two good outcomes mentioned (الْحُسْنَيَيْنِ).
Root letters got vowels to make a noun meaning 'beauty' instead of a verb
Added feminine superlative ending
Added ي to make it dual form (meaning 'two')
Added dual ending ين
Added ال to make it specific
This is the basic pronoun meaning 'we' in Arabic. It's the subject (doer) of the verb نَتَرَبَّصُ (we await) in this ayah.
Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this statement to what came before it in the conversation.
Added extra letter ب to show doing something with extra effort/intensity
Adding ت shows the person is doing the action to/for themselves
Changed from 'he waited' to 'we are waiting' by adding نَ at the start to show 'we' are doing the action
Starting with the preposition بِ which shows how something is done

steps.m: with you all (connected to يُصِيبَ - strike/afflict)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. This word connects to يُصِيبَ (strike/afflict) in the ayah to show who might be struck.
This is a connecting word that introduces what is expected or anticipated to happen. Here it connects to the verb يُصِيبَكُمُ (strikes you), showing what is being waited for.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Present tense form (starts with يُ) of 'to strike/affect'. The middle letter changes from و to ي, which is common in Arabic verbs.

steps.m: it strikes/affects you all (object of أَن)
steps.n: The كُمُ at the end shows who receives the action. This verb is connected to أَن (that) in the ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Adding ا after the second letter turns the action into the result of that action - the punishment itself

steps.m: with a punishment (that Allah may afflict you with)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to mean 'with' and ٍ at end to show it's any punishment, not a specific one. This word connects to يُصِيبَكُمُ (may afflict you) in the ayah.
This is a basic particle (helper word) that shows where something comes from or starts. Here it connects with عِندِهِ (from His presence) to show where something originates.
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on م to show stronger pronunciation because it comes after another م sound in the previous word (مَعَكُم).
This is a special word that shows location or possession
Added short 'i' vowels (kasra) to make it 'indi', showing it's connected to the word after it
Added هِ to show it belongs to him (referring to the punishment mentioned)
Added ۦ after هِ to help with pronunciation when connecting words

steps.m: or (connecting two possibilities: that Allah will afflict you with punishment or by our hands)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows there are two possible ways something could happen. In this ayah, it connects two ways Allah's punishment could come: directly from Him or through the believers' hands.
Connected the three root letters with vowels to make the basic word for 'hand'
Changed from singular 'hand' to plural 'hands' by using a special pattern that starts with أَ
Added ي at the end to make it a feminine plural, showing multiple hands
Adds genitive case marker
Added the preposition بِ (with/by) to show how something is done or accomplished

steps.m: with our hands/power (referring to Allah's power to punish)
steps.n: Added نَا (our) to show first person plural possession. In this ayah, it refers to Allah's power to bring punishment, using the royal 'We'.
The shadda shows this is an intensive form - it means to wait with purpose
Adding تَ shows this is something done deliberately for oneself
Adding وا makes this refer to a group of people
The فَ shows this is a response or result of what came before
Adding shadda makes this word stronger, changing it from a conditional 'if' to an emphatic 'indeed'
Adding ا at the end shows this applies to 'we' (first person plural)
This is a preposition that shows being together with someone or something. Unlike English 'with' that can mean 'using', this specifically means being together.

steps.m: with you all (connected to مُّتَرَبِّصُونَ - waiting)
steps.n: The كُم shows it's talking to multiple people. In this ayah, it's connected to مُّتَرَبِّصُونَ (waiting), showing who is being waited with.
Added vowels and doubled middle letter to show intense action
ت at beginning shows the person is doing the action to/for themselves

steps.m: one who is waiting and watching
steps.n: Changed to active participle form to show who does the action

steps.m: those who are waiting and watching (plural)
steps.n: Added plural ending and merged first letters with shadda
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: say! (command to one person) (followed by أَنفِقُوا۟ - spend!)
steps.n: The middle weak letter و is dropped and the vowels change to make a command. This tells one person to do something. In this verse, it's telling the Prophet ﷺ to say the next part.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he spent' to 'spend!' by adding أ at start and changing middle vowel to i-sound - this makes it a command to one person
Added وا to make it a command to multiple people. This command is followed by طَوْعًا (willingly) in the ayah.
Changed into a noun that describes the state of willing submission. This word works with كَرْهًا (unwillingly) to show opposite ways of giving.
Added tanween to make it describe how something is done. This describes how the spending (أَنفِقُوا) should be done - with willing submission.
This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two options: طَوْعًا (willingly) or كَرْهًا (unwillingly). It's one of the basic Arabic particles that can't be broken down into smaller parts.
Changed the word from an action into a feeling or state. This word describes how the spending (أَنفِقُوا) is done - with dislike.
Added ًا to show this describes how the action is done. Like changing 'force' to 'forcefully' in English. This describes how they should spend (أَنفِقُوا) - whether they like it or not.
This is the basic negative particle that makes verbs negative in the future

steps.m: will never (makes يُتَقَبَّلَ impossible in the future)
steps.n: Adding emphasis by doubling the ل with a shadda. This makes the negation stronger, like saying 'absolutely not' or 'never' instead of just 'not'. This word makes the verb يُتَقَبَّلَ (be accepted) impossible in the future.
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding the vowels a-i-a
Adding تَ and doubling the middle letter shows doing something willingly/eagerly

steps.m: it is accepted (from مِنكُمْ - from you)
steps.n: Changed to passive present tense, showing the action is being done to something. In this ayah, it's connected to مِنكُمْ (from you).
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphatic 'indeed'

steps.m: indeed you all (referring to the disbelievers being addressed)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show who is being addressed - in this case, the disbelievers who are being told their spending won't be accepted

steps.m: to be or exist (in a state/place/time)

steps.n: Added vowels (fat-ha) to make it past tense 'he was'
The middle weak letter و drops out and changes the vowels around it

steps.m: you (all) were (referring to the disbelievers being addressed)
steps.n: Added تُمْ to show this refers to a group being spoken to in the past
Changed the root into a noun meaning a group of people who stand together. This word completes the meaning of كُنتُمْ (you were) in the phrase 'you were a people'.
Added an alif and two small dashes (tanween) at the end to show this is 'a people' rather than 'the people'

steps.m: to break rules, to disobey limits

steps.n: The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) above the letter instead of the usual alif letter. This is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Added ين to show we're talking about many people who break rules. This matches with قَوْمًا (people) in the ayah.
Added fatha to show this is the basic negative particle ما. In this ayah, it negates the verb مَنَعَهُمْ (prevented them).
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form. This shows who did the action and when it happened.

steps.m: he prevented them (from having their charity accepted)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who was prevented. This connects to the next part of the verse which explains what they were prevented from - having their charity accepted.

steps.m: that (introduces what was prevented from happening)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces what was prevented from happening. It connects to تُقْبَلَ (being accepted) to show what was prevented - their spending being accepted.
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed first vowel to damma to make it passive - showing the action happened to something rather than something doing the action

steps.m: it be accepted (from نَفَقٰتُهُمْ - their expenditures)
steps.n: Added ت at beginning and changed vowels to show present tense passive. The word نَفَقٰتُهُمْ (their expenditures) is what's being accepted.
Combination of preposition مِنْ (from) and pronoun هُمْ (them)
Changed to noun form meaning 'one instance of spending'
Made plural by changing the ending. This word refers to the expenditures that were prevented from being accepted (مَنَعَهُمْ أَن تُقْبَلَ).
Added attached possessive pronoun هم meaning 'their'
