steps.m: the obligatory charitable donations (subject of إِنَّمَا)
steps.n: Adds a 'u' sound at the end to show this word is the main subject, and lengthens the 'a' sound for emphasis
Root letters got vowels (َ ُ) to make past tense verb meaning 'became poor'
Added ي to show someone who has this quality
Changed from singular (one poor person) to plural (many poor people) by changing the word pattern
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: for the poor people (recipients of الصَّدَقٰتُ)
steps.n: Added لِ meaning 'for' and the final vowel ِ to show this word receives the action. This word shows who should receive the charity mentioned earlier.

steps.m: one who is needy (because they have no settled means)
steps.n: Adding مَ at the start and ين at the end turns the root 'to be still/settled' into 'someone who lacks settled means'
Changes to make plural: 1) Add ا after second letter 2) Change ِي to ي
Adding ال makes it refer to specific needy people
Adding و connects this to the previous word (الفقراء - the poor) in the list of who can receive charity
Adding ِ (kasra) at the end shows this word is affected by something before it in the sentence
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed from verb (he worked) to noun (worker) by adding ا after first letter
Makes the word plural to show multiple workers
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Adds 'and' to connect this to the previous categories of people who receive zakat. The long alif (ٰ) is a spelling variation.
This is a preposition derived from the concept of being high/elevated. The final ى represents the weak letter from the root.
The ى changes to يْ before adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: upon it/her (referring to الصَّدَقٰتُ - the charities)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to the charities (الصَّدَقٰتُ) mentioned earlier in the verse. This shows who is working upon/managing these charities.
Changed from basic root to past tense verb with doubled middle letter for extra meaning of 'bringing together firmly'
Added مُ at start to show something is being done to someone/something, and added ؤ to keep the أ sound
Added ة to make it feminine, since it's describing a group (groups are usually feminine in Arabic)
Added ال to point to a specific group

steps.m: and the group being brought together
steps.n: Added و to connect this word to the words before it in the list

steps.m: and those whose hearts are to be reconciled (connected to قُلُوبُهُمْ)
steps.n: Added ِ at the end because this word is connected to قُلُوبُهُمْ (their hearts) in the next part of the verse
This is a broken plural - instead of adding letters at the end, it changes the pattern of the word to show many hearts
Adding damma case ending to show nominative case
Adding attached pronoun هم meaning 'their'
Added kasra vowel mark to show basic pronunciation of the preposition
Changed the final ي to ى which is just a writing style variation - doesn't change meaning
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. This word connects to سَبِيلِ (path/way) to mean 'and in (the) way (of)'
Forms the singular noun for 'neck' which came to mean 'slave' since slaves wore collars around their necks
Changes the word from singular (one slave) to plural (many slaves) by adding an alif
Adding 'the' (ال) at the start makes it specific necks/slaves

steps.m: for freeing the slaves (in context of charity)
steps.n: Adding kasra at end shows this is one of the categories for giving charity mentioned in this verse
The word means 'necks' but is used to refer to slaves. The 'ال' (al) at the start shows it's talking about specific slaves/necks

steps.m: to be in debt, to owe money
one who is in debt (a debtor)
steps.n: Changed from root meaning 'debt' to 'debtor' by adding vowels in active participle pattern
Added ين to show we're talking about multiple debtors
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to the previous groups mentioned in the verse who can receive charity

steps.m: and the debtors (one of the groups eligible for zakat)
steps.n: Added ال to specify this refers to the specific group of debtors who are eligible to receive zakat. Also added َ at the end to show this word is part of the list of recipients.
This is a basic preposition showing location or being inside something. In this ayah, it's used to show what categories the charity (صَّدَقٰتُ) should go into.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing, but the meaning stays the same

steps.m: and in (connects to previous categories of charity recipients)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this to the list of previous categories mentioned in the ayah about who can receive charity

steps.m: to travel or follow a path

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes this a past tense verb
Changes from verb 'to travel' into noun 'path' by adding ي
Added kasra at the end to show this path belongs to Allah in the phrase سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ (the path of Allah)
This root relates to worship and divinity
Root meaning 'worship' became a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making it a proper noun for The One God
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word. This word shows who the obligation (فَرِيضَةً) is from.
Root ب-ن-ي becomes 'son' by adding اِ at start and putting a sukun mark on ب. Like how a son comes from (is built from) parents.
The اِ becomes ٱ which shows it's a helping sound for pronunciation
Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and'
Added ِ at the end to show this is one of the recipients of الصَّدَقٰتُ (the charitable gifts)

steps.m: to travel or follow a path

steps.n: Basic noun form showing a path or way
Added ي (yaa) to make it a proper noun meaning 'path' - this is the standard form used in Arabic
Added definite article and emphasized the س with shadda
Added kasra at the end to show connection to the word اللَّهِ that follows it

steps.m: to make something required or necessary

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to make it into an adjective meaning 'required'
Added ة to make it a specific instance of something required
Added tanween to show this describes how the charity is given - as an obligation from Allah
This is a basic preposition showing source or origin
The م gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after a word ending in ن, making it easier to say
Added fatha (َ) at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word اللَّهِ (Allah)
The word Allah is unique - it comes from combining ال (the) with إله (deity), but the initial hamza is dropped and the lam gets a shadda to show emphasis
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to/describes something else. In this case, it shows this is Allah's path (سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ meaning 'path of Allah')
This is the proper name of God in Islam. It comes from the root meaning 'divine' but is a unique word that only refers to the one true God.
Added shadda to show the doubled 'l' sound
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action in 'Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise'
Added و at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before
Changed the alif to a waṣla form since it connects with the و before it in pronunciation
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Adding ي between second and third root letters makes it mean someone who has this quality very strongly
The tanween dammah (ٌ) shows this is one of Allah's attributes mentioned in this ayah alongside حَكِيمٌ (All-Wise)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into an adjective describing someone who has this quality

steps.m: wise, full of wisdom (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added tanween dammah to show this is describing Allah's attribute of wisdom in this context
This is a preposition meaning 'from'
Added و to connect this word to the previous phrase, showing these ideas are related

steps.m: and from them (referring to the hypocrites mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هُمُ to show this applies to a specific group (the hypocrites) being discussed in this part of the Quran
This is a special demonstrative word composed of the definite article ال + ذي (who/which) + ين (plural suffix). It's used to point to people who do something.
Added يُ at the beginning to show present tense, and changed vowels to match present tense pattern

steps.m: they are harming (subject of لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word is connected to لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ (they have a punishment) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic noun form

steps.m: someone who brings important news from Allah
steps.n: Changed from 'news' to 'news-bringer' by adding -iy ending to make it mean a person
Added ال (the) at the start which made the ن double in sound - now means THE prophet instead of just any prophet

steps.m: the prophet (being hurt by يُؤْذُونَ - they hurt)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show the prophet is being hurt by the people mentioned in يُؤْذُونَ (they hurt)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action
Added وَ to connect this with the previous phrase
This is a pronoun that means 'he'. In this ayah, it connects to the word أُذُنٌ (ear) to say 'he is an ear', meaning people were claiming the Prophet ﷺ believes everything he hears.
Added damma vowels to transform the root into a noun for the body part that does the hearing
The ٌ shows this is indefinite (like saying 'an ear' instead of 'the ear'). In this ayah, it describes what 'he' (هُوَ) is.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form. The middle letter و drops out because it's between two other letters. This is common in Arabic with weak letters like و.
Changes from verb 'to listen' to noun 'ear' by adding damma vowels
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the verb يَقُولُونَ (they say)
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to form the basic noun
Added tanween kasra to show this word is indefinite and is being acted upon by something else in the sentence
Added fatha to the basic preposition ل to make it pronounceable
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning, making it stronger
Added the pronoun كُمْ which means 'you all' (when talking to multiple people)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added a long 'aa' sound at the start to mean 'to cause/make someone trust', which came to mean 'believe'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end. The word shows belief in Allah specifically in this ayah.
This is the base word Allah, the proper name of God in Islam. It's treated as a complete word rather than being derived from a root.
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation
Added بِ at beginning to show relationship and ِ at end because it's being acted upon by the verb يُؤْمِنُ (believes in)

steps.m: to trust, believe in, have faith

steps.n: The hamza أ changes to ؤ after the damma, and we add a sukun on it. This is just a spelling rule and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: and he believes (connected to يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: The و connects this word to يَقُولُونَ (they say) earlier in the verse, showing these actions happen together

steps.m: to trust, feel secure, be faithful

steps.n: Added مُ at start and vowels to show someone who does the action of believing/having faith. The ؤ comes from the root's أ.
Added ين at the end to show this refers to more than two male believers
Added لِ at beginning to show this is 'for' or 'to' the believers
Added ال (with ل becoming لْ) to specify these are 'the' believers, not just any believers
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters together
The ة at the end makes this a noun meaning one instance or type of mercy
Adds 'and' to connect this word to what came before it
Adds double dammah to show this is indefinite (like 'a' in English)
This is the basic relative pronoun that points to someone or something specific
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one person
Adding لِ shows this is talking about something being 'for' or 'to' these people
When two ل letters come together, they combine with a shadda to emphasize 'for those who'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Adding the long alif at start changes it from 'believe' to 'make someone believe'
Added وا at the end to make it 'they' instead of 'he', showing a group of people believed
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or someone
Added the pronoun كم to show who is being referred to
Starting with the base demonstrative pronoun
The word gets 'the' (ال) added at the start, making the ذ doubled with a shadda
Adding plural suffix ين
Adding conjunction و at the beginning to connect with previous phrase
Added يُ at start to show present tense. The hamza changes to ؤ after damma. The middle letter gets sukun and the last letter gets kasra.

Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The ي at the end changes to و before the plural ending. This word is connected to يَقُولُونَ (they say) in this ayah, showing the same group doing both actions.
Changed the root letters to رَسُول pattern to show someone who delivers messages as their job
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of 'harming' in the phrase 'they harm the messenger'
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God. The first ا changes to ٱ when connecting to other words.

steps.m: of Allah (object of يُؤْمِنُ - believes in)
steps.n: Added ِ at the end to show this is the object of the verb يُؤْمِنُ (believes in). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial ا to ٱ for connection with previous word.
Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning
Added هُمْ to show this applies to multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to those who harm Allah's messenger.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past-tense verb

Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'punishment'. The double-damma (tanween) at the end shows this is indefinite (like saying 'a punishment'). This word is the subject in the phrase لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ (they have a punishment).
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Forms intensive adjective meaning 'causing severe pain' - describes the punishment (عَذَابٌ) mentioned in the ayah
Adding double dammah makes it indefinite - can be translated as 'a painful punishment'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows this is happening now

steps.m: they swear (referring to the hypocrites in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding ون changes subject from 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural)
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation of Allah's name
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection

steps.m: by Allah (object of يَحْلِفُونَ - they swear)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show swearing by something. This word is what they are swearing by in the phrase 'they swear by Allah'.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the letter ل
Added ك with damma to show it refers to multiple people being addressed

steps.m: to/for you all (object of يَحْلِفُونَ - they swear)
steps.n: Added م with sukun to complete the plural pronoun. This word shows who they are swearing to in the phrase 'they swear to you all'
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb meaning 'he became pleased' by adding the vowels fatha-kasra-fatha
Changed from 'being pleased' to 'making someone pleased' by adding yu- at start
Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding و at the end
Added لِ to show purpose - they do this in order to achieve something

steps.m: so that they may please you all
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who they are trying to please - you all
This is the unique proper name of God in Islam. While it comes from the root meaning 'divine/deity', it has become a distinct proper noun.
Added shadda on ل to show it is doubled in pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb أَحَقُّ (is more worthy) in this ayah

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of أَحَقُّ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement, plus the helper alif (ٱ) that comes with the definite article ال
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Added damma (u sound) at the end to show this is the subject - the messenger is the one who is more worthy
Added هُ to show this is Allah's messenger, connecting back to the word الله mentioned earlier
Added وَ to connect this with Allah being worthy of being pleased
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show emphasis on middle letter

steps.m: more/most deserving (of أَن يُرْضُوهُ - that they please Him)
steps.n: Added أَ at beginning to make it comparative. The ُّ at the end shows this is the subject of the sentence. This word relates to the phrase after it 'that they please Him', showing who is more deserving of being pleased.

steps.m: that (connects to يُرْضُوهُ - they should please Him)
steps.n: This is a fundamental particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it connects the idea of pleasing Allah to the previous statement. It works like 'that' in English when you say 'I want that you come' - it joins the two ideas together.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense and causative form - showing someone making another person pleased
Changed to plural form to show multiple people doing the action
Added pronoun to show who is being pleased. This connects to أَحَقُّ (more worthy) showing Allah and His Messenger are more worthy of being pleased.

This is a conditional particle that shows a condition - if something happens. Not derived from a root. This is the base form of the word. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if they were believers' for the previous statement about pleasing Allah.
Added kasra (ِ) under the إ to show how it's pronounced (in).
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed to common irregular form with alif
Added وا to show multiple people did this action. This word describes the same people who are doing the swearing (يَحْلِفُونَ) in this verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes verb 'trusted' into 'one who trusts' by adding مُ at start and changing middle vowels
Adds masculine plural ending

steps.m: believers (as the object in this sentence)
steps.n: The fatha (small diagonal line) at the end shows this word is the object - the ones being acted upon - in this sentence about swearing by Allah
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of verbs to 'did not' when used with them. In this ayah, it negates يَعْلَمُوا (they knew) to mean 'they did not know'
Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question that expects the answer 'yes'. It's like saying 'Didn't they know?' when you want to emphasize that they should have known.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
The يَ at the start with ُ at the end shows it's happening in the present
The وا at the end changes it from singular 'he' to plural 'they'. This verb follows أَلَمْ (did not) in the ayah, making it a negative question: 'Did they not know?'
This is an emphasis particle that connects parts of a sentence and adds certainty to what follows

steps.m: that he/it (object of يَعْلَمُوا - they know)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ (he/it) to show who/what is being referred to. This whole phrase is what they know in the ayah.
This is a conditional word that means 'whoever' or 'anyone who'. In this ayah, it refers to anyone who opposes (يُحَادِدِ) Allah and His messenger.

steps.m: to set a limit or oppose
steps.n: This is about making or crossing boundaries
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Shows the action is done between two parties - they oppose each other
Adding يُ makes it present tense, and changing middle vowel to kasra is part of present tense pattern

steps.m: he opposes/contends with (اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ - Allah and His Messenger)
steps.n: Adding kasra at the end shows this is in a conditional sentence (whoever opposes...)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Added a long vowel mark (small alif) after the second letter to elongate the sound
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يُحَادِدِ - opposes)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' comes from 'al-ilah' (the god), but is a special name. Here it has a fatha mark at the end because someone is opposing Allah in this sentence
This is about sending something or someone
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main job
Added fatha to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يُحَادِدِ (opposes/contends with)
Added the pronoun هُ meaning 'His' to show this is Allah's messenger
Added وَ (and) to connect this to the previous word اللَّهَ (Allah)
This is a basic emphasis particle that makes statements stronger. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'surely' in English.
The فَ at the beginning shows this is a result or consequence of the previous statement 'Do they not know?' (أَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوٓا۟). It's like saying 'so' or 'then' in English.
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: for/to him (connected to نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ - the fire of Hell)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who the word 'for' refers to. In this ayah, it's connecting to the phrase 'the fire of Hell' that comes right after it.

steps.m: for/to him (connected to نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ - the fire of Hell)
steps.n: Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a writing style to show the pronunciation more clearly. It doesn't change the meaning.
This root is about light and fire
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make the root letters into a past tense verb meaning 'he lit'

steps.m: fire (object of فَأَنَّ لَهُۥ - then indeed for him is)
steps.n: The root نور (light) changed to make the noun نار meaning 'fire'. This is what someone will have (لَهُۥ) according to the ayah.
This is a proper noun for Hell. It comes from a non-Arabic source, so it doesn't have an Arabic root to break down.
Added vowel marks and doubled the ن with shadda to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: Hell (as the object of نَارَ/fire of)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the phrase 'fire of Hell'. This connects it grammatically to the word نَارَ (fire) that comes just before it.
This is about something that never ends
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from a past tense verb (he lasted) to a describing word (lasting) by changing the vowels and lengthening the first syllable

steps.m: eternally, forever (describing the state in نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ - the fire of Hell)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this describes how something happens. In this ayah, it describes the eternal state in Hell's fire.
Basic preposition showing location or containment. Since this is a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
Added ها which refers back to نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ (the Fire of Hell) mentioned earlier in the ayah
This is the basic pointing word that shows something specific. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this form.
Adding لِ makes it point to something farther away or previously mentioned, changing 'this' to 'that'

steps.m: that (pointing to نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ - the fire of Hell mentioned before)
steps.n: Adding كَ completes the word, making it point specifically to something mentioned before in the text - in this case, it's pointing back to 'the fire of Hell' that was just mentioned
This is about shame and disgrace
Root meaning 'shame' turned into the noun 'disgrace' - like changing 'to run' into 'running'
The ي at the end changes to ى because it's at the end of the word
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: to be great in size, rank, or status
steps.n: This is about something being huge or important
Added ي between ظ and م with vowels to make it an emphatic adjective showing a strong quality
Adding ال makes it specific and definite. In this ayah, it's describing Allah as one of His attributes

steps.m: the Great, the Mighty (as a name/title of Allah)
steps.n: The ُ ending shows this is being used as a name or title
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he is being cautious (subject of يَقُولُ)
steps.n: Added يَ at the start to show this is happening now, and changed vowels to show ongoing action. The ُ at the end shows this is the main doer of the action.

steps.m: to pass through secretly or spend deceptively

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The alif makes it mean doing the action with/to others, showing the two-faced nature of hypocrisy

steps.m: one who acts hypocritically (subject of يَحْذَرُ)
steps.n: Creates a noun describing someone who does this action. This word is the subject of يَحْذَرُ (fears) in this ayah.
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Makes the word plural to show multiple hypocrites, and adds ُ to show it's the subject doing the action of fearing
This is a special word that connects two ideas together. Here it connects the fear (يَحْذَرُ) with what is feared (تُنَزَّلَ) - the hypocrites fear that a surah will be sent down.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intensive or repetitive

steps.m: it is being sent down repeatedly (about سُورَةٌ)
steps.n: Adding تُ at the beginning changes the verb to passive voice and present tense. This word describes سُورَةٌ (chapter) in the ayah.
This is a preposition that shows something is happening on top of or affecting something else
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns
Added هِمْ at the end to show this affects multiple people (them)
Added vowel marks to transform the root into a noun meaning the thing that surrounds

steps.m: a chapter (one unit that contains verses)
steps.n: Added feminine ending to show one complete unit, like one chapter that contains and surrounds its verses
Added two damma marks at the end to show it's any chapter, not a specific one
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning more intense or thorough
Changed to present tense by adding prefix and changing vowels

steps.m: she/it informs them thoroughly (about ما فِى قُلُوبِهِمْ - what is in their hearts)
steps.n: Changed from 'he' to 'she/it' informs and added 'them' at the end to show who is being informed. Links to 'what is in their hearts' later in the verse.
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'
Added ما which means 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it connects to the verb تَحْذَرُونَ (you fear), so together it means 'with what you fear'
This is a preposition that shows something is inside something else. Here it connects to قُلُوبِهِمْ (their hearts) to show what is inside.
Changed the action into a noun for the physical thing that turns/flips (the heart)
Changed the word to show many hearts instead of just one
Added هِمْ to show these hearts belong to them. This word appears in the phrase بِمَا فِى قُلُوبِهِمْ (with what is in their hearts).
The middle letter و drops out when making a command. The ُ and ْ marks tell us this is a command to one person.

steps.m: say! (command to one person, connected to ٱسْتَهْزِءُوٓا۟)
steps.n: The ِ is added at the end because this word connects to the next word ٱسْتَهْزِءُوٓا۟ (mock!) in speech. They are two connected commands.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The prefix است makes the mocking more intense and deliberate, like going out of your way to mock someone
Changes from 'he mocked' to 'they mocked' by adding وا at the end, which makes it plural
This is the basic form of the word that shows emphasis

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizes that اللَّهَ مُخْرِجٌ - Allah will expose)

steps.n: Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this action with previous statements in the verse about those who came before
Added kasra under ذ to show it's connected to what comes after it
Added ال with shadda on ذ to make it specific

steps.m: like the one who (similar to those mentioned before)
steps.n: Added كَ at beginning to show similarity to something mentioned before. The alif gets a special mark (waslah) because it connects with كَ
Changed the root letters into past tense by adding an alif (ا) in the middle and fatha marks
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action
Basic demonstrative form for pointing to things
The alif with line (ٰ) shows distance - pointing to something far away
Adding ئِ makes it specifically for plural (more than two)

steps.m: those (far away, plural, being pointed to)
steps.n: The كَ at the end shows you're pointing directly at them, like saying 'right there'
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form showing something becoming worthless

steps.m: it (feminine) became worthless (referring to أَعْمٰلُهُمْ - their deeds)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to show this is talking about their deeds (أَعْمٰلُهُمْ) which is feminine in Arabic
Forms the plural pattern for 'works/deeds'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic noun for a single action
Changes the singular noun into its plural form using the pattern أَفْعَال
Changed the middle 'a' sound to a longer 'aa' sound and added 'u' ending to connect with other words

steps.m: their deeds (subject of حَبِطَتْ - became worthless)
steps.n: Added pronoun suffix to show possession. This word is the subject of حَبِطَتْ (became worthless) in this ayah.
Basic preposition showing location or time. Here it connects to الدُّنْيَا to show 'in this world'
The ي changes to ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing style change and doesn't affect the meaning.
This is the basic concept the word comes from - things that are near or close in time/space
Root د-ن-و means 'near' - changed to دُنْيَا to mean 'this world/worldly life' (literally: the nearest life). The ي and feminine ending ا make it a special word for 'worldly'.

steps.m: the worldly life (contrasted with الْآخِرَة - the hereafter)
steps.n: Added ال and shadda to specify 'the worldly life'. In the Quran, this often contrasts with 'the hereafter' to show the difference between temporary earthly life and eternal afterlife.

steps.m: to come later or be delayed
steps.n: Forms pattern for 'the last/final one'
Makes the word feminine by adding the ة ending
Shows this word belongs to/is connected to another word
Added definite article to specify 'the' hereafter
Added conjunction 'and' to connect with previous phrase about 'the worldly life'
Added kasra ending because it follows the preposition في (in) from the previous phrase

This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to people or things that are far away (like saying 'those people over there'). In this ayah, it refers back to the people mentioned before who were stronger and had more wealth.

steps.m: and those (subject of حَبِطَتْ/their deeds became worthless)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. This word is the subject of حَبِطَتْ (became worthless) - showing that these are the people whose deeds became worthless.
Basic form of the third person masculine plural pronoun, used to refer to a group of males or mixed group
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence. This pronoun connects to the word ٱلْخٰسِرُونَ (the losers) to say 'they are the losers'
Adding vowels to show this is a past tense verb meaning 'to lose'
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does the action. This word is the predicate (describes هم) in this ayah.
Makes the word plural to show multiple people who lose
Makes the word definite and adds final vowel. This describes who هم (they) are in the ayah.

steps.m: introduces a question (like adding ? in English)
steps.n: This letter turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Adding لَ starts to form the negative particle that will make the question negative
Adding مْ completes the negative particle لَمْ which makes the question ask about something that did not happen in the past
These are the basic letters that mean moving or coming towards something
Added يَ at start to show it's happening now or in future, and added vowel marks to complete the word

steps.m: comes to them (in أَلَمْ context: did not come to them)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being come to. The ي disappears because it's a weak letter meeting the هِمْ suffix. This word follows أَلَمْ which makes it mean 'did not come to them'
Added vowels to connect the letters and turn the action into a noun meaning the news itself
Added ُ at the end to show this news is doing something - it's the thing that comes/reaches them in the verse
This is the basic form of the word that means 'who' when talking about one person or thing
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people, not just one
Added ال at the beginning to make it more specific, like adding 'the' in English

steps.m: those who (referring to previous nations mentioned in this verse)
steps.n: Added final vowel mark and emphasis on the ل. This word refers back to the previous nations mentioned in the verse like the people of Noah and others.
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. Here it introduces where the news/stories come from - specifically from before their time.
Root letters ق-ب-ل got vowel marks (َ and ْ) to make the word 'before'
Added هِمْ meaning 'their/them' and the ِ (kasra) that shows possession

steps.m: to stand firm, rise up, establish
people/nation (as a group that stands together)
steps.n: Changes from action to noun meaning a group that stands together. This is the base noun form before showing ownership.
Shows this word belongs to or is connected to what comes after it (نوح/Noah), making it mean 'people of Noah'
This is the basic proper name Noah
Added damma (ُ) on first letter to show proper pronunciation
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this word follows قَوْمِ (people of) in the phrase 'people of Noah'
This is the name of an ancient Arab tribe mentioned in the Quran. While it comes from the root meaning 'to return', it became a proper name.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to the previous ones in the list of peoples mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: and Aad (being mentioned in this list of peoples)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is part of a list of peoples being mentioned in the ayah
This is a proper noun with no root - it's the name of a people mentioned in the Quran who were destroyed for rejecting their prophet Salih
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being talked about in the sentence
Added wa at the beginning to connect this word to the previous people mentioned (the tribe of 'Ad)
Root meaning 'stand' became a noun for 'people' by adding vowels - like how people stand together as a group
Added conjunction 'and' to connect with previous words in the ayah
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to 'Ibrahim' that follows it, meaning 'people of Ibrahim'
Added vowel marks to show proper pronunciation of this proper noun (name). This is a non-Arabic name that was Arabized.
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of قَوْمِ (people of). Also replaced the alif with alif maqsurah (ٰ) which is a common spelling variation in the Quran.

steps.m: to accompany or be a companion

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to accompany' to 'one who accompanies'
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple companions
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it

steps.m: and companions (plural, possessed by/connected to something)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
This is a proper noun naming a specific ancient city
Basic vowel marks (fatha and sukun) added to make the root letters readable as 'mad-n'
Added ي to form the specific place name Madyan, an ancient city mentioned in the Quran

steps.m: Madyan (ancient city mentioned in this list of previous nations)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being mentioned in the list of previous nations
Added مُ at start to show it happened to something, and ت in middle to show it was done to cities that were flipped over and destroyed
Added ات to show we're talking about multiple cities that were destroyed
Added ال to point to specific destroyed cities mentioned in the Quran (like the cities of Lot's people)
Added و to connect with previous words in the list, and added ِ at the end because it follows other words in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks and changed final ي to ى due to weak letter rule
The word رُسُلُهُم (their messengers) is feminine plural in this context, requiring feminine verb form

steps.m: she came to them (their messengers came to them)
steps.n: Adding 3rd person masculine plural object pronoun suffix
Changes the verb into a plural noun for people who deliver messages
Adds هُم to show these are the messengers belonging to them. This word is the subject of أَتَتْهُم (came to them) in this ayah.
Added shadda on middle letter to intensify meaning
Added feminine plural ending ات to create plural noun
Added 'with/by' and 'the' to show these are specific clear proofs

steps.m: with the clear proofs/signs (object of أَتَتْهُمْ)
steps.n: Changed ending to show this word is being acted upon by the verb أَتَتْهُمْ (came to them)
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement about messengers coming to them. It connects this negation to that previous fact.
This root expresses the basic concept of existing or being in a state
Added fatha vowels to make past tense verb meaning 'he was'
The middle weak letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha before and after it. This word is negated by ما in the ayah, showing 'Allah was not...'
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Changed to the unique name of the one true God by adding ال with a shadda, replacing the initial hamza with alif-wasl, and adding a damma to show this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: to act unfairly or harm someone's rights

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added لِ to show purpose and changed the ending vowel to fatha

steps.m: in order to wrong them (connected to كَانَ ٱللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who is being wronged. This word explains what Allah was not doing to them.
Added the alif mark (ٰ) and kasra mark to show proper pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this contrasting statement with what came before it
Added vowels to make past tense. The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the middle.
Added وا at the end to change from 'he' to 'they'
Added optional pronunciation marks (the small و above and alif) that don't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Forms plural of nafs (self/soul)
Adds هُمْ meaning 'their' and َ to show this is the object being wronged in يَظْلِمُونَ (they wrong)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action
Changed the word to show someone who trusts/believes by adding مُ at the start and changing the vowels
Added ون to show we're talking about many male believers
Added ال to point to specific believers, not just any believers

Adding ات makes it refer specifically to women who believe. This ending is used for groups of women.

steps.m: and believing women (connected to وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ - and the believing men)
steps.n: Adds و to connect this word to 'believing men' mentioned just before it

steps.m: and the believing women (subject with وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to a specific group of believing women, not just any believing women

steps.m: and the believing women (main subject with وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ)
steps.n: The ُ at the end shows this word is one of the main subjects doing the actions in the verse

steps.m: to separate into parts or portions

steps.n: Root letters got vowels (ba got fatha, ayn got sukoon) and damma ending shows this word is the subject

steps.m: some of them (referring to the believing men and women)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show we're talking about 'them' (the believers mentioned before)

steps.m: to protect, to be close to

Added ُ to show this word is doing the action in يَأْمُرُونَ (they command). The آ is just a writing style for ا.

steps.n: Connected the letters together with vowel marks to make a noun meaning a portion or part of something
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by others in the sentence
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now/future, and the ُ endings show it's ongoing
The ون ending shows multiple people (they) are doing the commanding (related to وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ - the believers - mentioned earlier)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: with/by what is proper/good (object of يَأْمُرُونَ)

Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Transforms to imperfect/present tense verb
Changes subject from singular to plural masculine

steps.m: and they stop/prevent (from الْمُنكَرِ - what is wrong)
steps.n: Adding و at the start means 'and'

Added kasra at the end to help connect it smoothly to the next word (ٱلْمُنكَرِ). This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to reject or disapprove of something

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb meaning 'he rejected'
Adding مُ at the start and changing the vowels turns this into something that is rejected or disapproved of
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific and ِ shows it follows the word عَنِ (from/away from)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into past tense form
Changed to Form IV to show causative meaning - making something happen rather than doing it directly
Changes from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Added conjunction و (and) at beginning. This word connects to صَّلَوٰةَ (prayer) which they establish.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels

The weak letter و changes to an alif with overbar (ٰ) due to pronunciation rules

steps.m: the prayer (object of يُقِيمُونَ - they establish)
steps.n: Added 'the' at beginning, doubled the ص with shadda, and added fatha at end to show this is the object of يُقِيمُونَ (they establish)

steps.m: to give, bring, or come with something

steps.n: Added يُ at start to mean 'he' in present tense, and changed middle of word to show giving something to someone else
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: and they give (connected to يُقِيمُونَ - they establish)
steps.n: Added و at start to connect this action with the previous one (establishing prayer)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from an action to a specific type of purification through giving (zakat)
Makes it refer to the specific Islamic charity (zakat)
Shows this word is the object in the sentence
Added basic past tense form with fatha vowels

The word changes from past tense 'he obeyed' to present tense 'he obeys' by adding يُ at the start and changing the middle vowels. This word is part of يطيعون which shows the believers obeying Allah and His messenger.

steps.m: they obey (referring to the believers mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Adding ون makes it plural masculine

steps.m: and they obey (Allah and His messenger)
steps.n: The و connects this to the previous actions of the believers. They are specifically obeying Allah and His messenger as shown in the next part of the verse.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being obeyed in يُطِيعُونَ)

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و
Added هُ at the end to show possession (his) and َ to show this is the object being obeyed in the phrase 'they obey Allah and His messenger'
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this with the previous word (Allah). The ۥٓ marks are pronunciation helpers.
This is the basic form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to things
Adding ا shows that what we're pointing to is far away
Adding ئِ makes it specifically refer to multiple people or things

steps.m: those (far away, plural, being pointed to)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the end completes the demonstrative meaning, showing we are pointing to specific things or people
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added هُمُ to show who receives the mercy

steps.m: he will show mercy to them (subject: اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added سَ to show this will happen in the future. Allah is the one showing mercy.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of سَيَرْحَمُ (will have mercy).
Adding shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional particle to an emphatic one that strengthens the meaning of what follows. Here it emphasizes that Allah is mighty and wise.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became the proper name for God in Arabic.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being obeyed in يُطِيعُونَ)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a shadda mark (double L sound), a special beginning alif, and an -a ending because it's receiving the action of obeying
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the word pattern to show a strong, permanent quality. This describes Allah in the ayah.

steps.m: to judge wisely or make wise decisions

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: wise, full of wisdom (describes اللَّهَ - Allah)
steps.n: Changed the vowels and added ي to show someone who has the quality of wisdom. The ٌ (tanween) at the end shows this is describing Allah in this ayah.

steps.m: he promised (subject of Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha marks. This word shows Allah making the promise in the past.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added damma to show this word is the subject
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
Makes the word plural and shows it's receiving the action of Allah's promise

steps.m: the believers (object of Allah's promise وَعَدَ)
steps.n: Makes the word specific by adding 'the'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Forms active participle meaning 'one who believes/trusts'

Adding ات to مؤمن changes it from 'a male believer' to 'female believers' (plural). Like changing 'believer' to 'believers' in English but for women only. This word shows who Allah made His promise to.
Added the definite article ال to make the word definite
Added the conjunction وَ (and) at the beginning and genitive case ending ِ because it follows المؤمنين in the list of who Allah promised

steps.m: to cover or conceal (like how garden plants cover the ground)

steps.n: Root means 'to cover' - doubled middle letter shows something that covers a lot

steps.m: a garden (because it's covered with plants)
steps.n: Added ة to make it a specific noun for a place that is covered
Changed to plural form by adding ات

steps.m: gardens (as the promised reward from Allah)
steps.n: Added two dots under the last letter to show these gardens are being given or promised to someone
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
تَ at start shows this is happening now (present tense)
The ِ vowel mark helps show this is present tense
This is a particle that shows something comes from somewhere else. In this ayah, it connects the verb تَجْرِى (flows) with تَحْتِهَا (beneath it), showing the rivers flow from beneath the gardens.
Connected the root letters with basic vowel marks to form the word
Added kasra to connect this word to what follows it
Added ها to show this refers back to the gardens (جَنَّاتٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to flow like a river, to stream

steps.n: Root letters combined with vowels to make the noun 'river' from the action 'to flow'
Added أ at start to make it plural (like changing 'river' to 'rivers')
Added ُ (damma) at end to show this word is the subject of the sentence
The alif (ا) changed to a long alif (ٰ), but the word still means rivers and is the subject of 'flows' (تَجْرِى)
Adding ال makes the word 'rivers' specific/definite, like saying 'the rivers' instead of just 'rivers'
Added vowels to make it 'he remained' in the past tense
Changed pattern to show who does the action

steps.m: those who remain forever (describes multiple people)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to change from one person remaining to many people remaining forever

steps.m: those who remain forever (describes the believers in جَنَّاتٍ/gardens)

Added هَا at the end to show this refers to the gardens (جَنَّاتٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse. Since gardens is feminine in Arabic, we use the feminine 'it'.

steps.m: to settle, dwell, be at rest

Adding مَ at the start and changing the vowels makes it mean the place where someone dwells
Changed to plural form by adding ا after second letter and changing vowels
Added َ at the end to show this is receiving the action of Allah's promise
Added وَ (and) to connect this to the other things Allah promised. The long alif is written as ٰ.
Added a shadda (ّ) to make the middle letter stronger, which makes the meaning stronger too
Added ة to make the word feminine - like changing 'pure' to 'pure one (feminine)'
Added tanween (ً) to show this word is describing the dwellings (مَسٰكِنَ) mentioned in the ayah
This is a basic particle that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows that people will be inside the gardens of Paradise.

steps.m: to hide or protect by covering

steps.n: Adding ة makes this into a noun for a place that is covered/protected - a garden
Regular feminine plural form for nouns. This word is the direct object of the verb وَعَدَ (promised), showing what Allah promised to the believers.
The kasra shows this word is connected to عَدْنٍ (eternal dwelling) that comes after it in the ayah

steps.m: eternal dwelling (name of a level of Paradise)
steps.n: This is a proper noun referring to a special level of Paradise that represents eternal happiness and dwelling
The tanween (two kasras) at the end shows this word is describing the gardens mentioned before it (gardens of eternal dwelling)
Changed from basic root letters to noun form by adding vowel marks 'i' and sukoon
Adding ان at the end makes it mean 'a lot of' or 'complete' pleasure
Adding tanween (double damma) makes it an indefinite noun - meaning 'a' great pleasure

The fatha (َ) at the end shows this connects to the next word اللَّهِ (Allah), making the complete phrase mean 'from Allah'

Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far

steps.m: that (referring to the great rewards just mentioned)
steps.n: The كَ at the end makes it more specific in pointing to something

Changed the root into a noun by adding vowel marks. This word is part of ذٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ (that is the great triumph).

Added ُ to show this word completes the meaning of هُوَ (it is) in ذٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ (that is the great triumph)

steps.m: to be great/mighty in size or status

steps.n: The ي between the second and third root letters makes this an intensive adjective showing the highest degree of greatness, which in this context refers to Allah's attribute
ال makes it definite, and ُ shows this is describing Allah as the subject of this attribute. This is one of Allah's names showing His absolute and perfect greatness.

steps.m: to call attention, to point to

The small damma mark (ُ) shows we are calling out to get someone's attention

Changed the middle vowel and added ي to show someone who does this action

Added ُ to show this is the one being addressed in يٰٓأَيُّهَا and who should do the action in جٰهِدِ

steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show doing something back-and-forth with someone else

steps.m: to reject or hide the truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb

steps.m: those who regularly reject the truth
steps.n: The doubled ف and added ا make it mean 'people who do this a lot', and makes it plural

steps.m: those who regularly reject the truth (as object of جٰهِدِ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb جٰهِدِ (strive against)

steps.m: the rejecters of truth (as object of جٰهِدِ)
steps.n: Added ال to specify these particular rejecters of truth
Someone who shows one thing but hides another
Makes it plural to mean multiple hypocrites. This word is being acted upon by جٰهِدِ (strive against) in this ayah.
Adds 'and' to connect this word to what came before it
Adds 'al' (ال) and ending sound (-ina) to show these hypocrites are being acted upon by the verb 'strive against'
The ُ (damma) shows this is about gaining the quality of being harsh
Changed from 'he became harsh' to 'be harsh!' by making it a command - added alif at start and made the end silent

steps.m: and be harsh! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ against the disbelievers)
steps.n: Added و to connect this command with the previous one in the verse (جٰهِدِ - strive against)
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns
Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being referred to (them)
Added مَ at the start to mean 'place of' and ى at the end because the word ends in a weak letter
Added هُمْ at the end to show it belongs to them (referring to the disbelievers and hypocrites mentioned earlier in the verse)

steps.m: and their place of shelter/refuge (referring to Hell mentioned after)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this with what came before. The shelter being referred to is Hell (جَهَنَّمُ) as mentioned right after in the verse.
Added vowel marks and shadda to show the intense nature of this word. This is a proper noun for the place of punishment in the afterlife.
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence. In this ayah, Hell is being described as a terrible destination.
Added vowel marks to make this a special word that shows strong disapproval. The ِ after ب and ْ after ء show this is specifically for saying something is very bad.
Added وَ to connect this phrase to what came before it, showing this is an additional comment about the destination mentioned (جَهَنَّمُ)

steps.m: to move or change from one state to another

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning to show where the action ends up
Added ال to make it specific
Added ُ to show this is the subject of بِئْسَ (how evil/wretched) in this ayah
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they make/are making an oath (subject of قَالُوا)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing this. This word connects to قَالُوا (they said) in the ayah, showing the same group is doing both actions.
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity/worship but is a unique word.
Added shadda on ل to show it is emphasized
Added kasra at the end to show possession
Added بِ at beginning to show 'by' and changed alif to alif wasl (ٱ). This word is what they are swearing by in the phrase 'they swear by Allah'.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it negative. In this ayah, it negates قَالُوا۟ (they said), making the meaning 'they did not say'.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Adding وا changes 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural)
Added vowel marks to the base word قد which expresses certainty or completion of an action
Added ل at the beginning to add extra emphasis
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he said'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's easier to pronounce - this is very common in Arabic words with و in the middle

steps.m: they said (referring to a group of people being talked about in this verse)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show that a group of people (they) did the action, not just one person
Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs
Adding ة at the end makes it refer to one specific instance of speech/word

steps.m: the word (object of قَالُوا۟ - they said)
steps.n: Adding fatha at the end shows this is the object being acted upon by the verb قَالُوا۟ (they said)

steps.m: to reject or deny truth by covering it up

steps.n: Changed the vowels to turn the action into the concept or result of that action. Like how 'reject' becomes 'rejection' in English.

steps.m: the rejection of truth (object of كَلِمَةَ)
steps.n:
1. Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
2. Added ِ at the end because it follows the word كَلِمَةَ (word of) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense verb form
Added وا۟ suffix to make the verb plural masculine
Added conjunction وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this action with what came before in the verse
Added vowel marks to create a word meaning 'after' - this makes sense because what comes after is distant/separated from what came before
Added fatha at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence

steps.m: to submit, surrender, be at peace

steps.n: Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changes verb 'submit' into the noun form meaning 'submission'
Adds 'their' to show who the submission belongs to
Removed 'their' (هِمْ) from the end to make it just 'submission' that can be possessed by someone
Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Added plural suffix وا and doubled middle letter م to show multiple people did the action

steps.m: and they planned/intended (connected to كَفَرُوا۟ - they disbelieved)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this action with the previous action of disbelief mentioned in the ayah
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence, like saying 'what' or 'that which' in English

steps.m: with what/with that which (connects to قَالُوا۟ - they said)
steps.n: Added the preposition بِ (with) to show how something is done. In this ayah, it connects to قَالُوا۟ (they said) to mean 'with what they said'

This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it to show something didn't happen in the past. It's like adding 'did not' before a verb in English. Here it works with يَنَالُوا۟ (they attain) to mean 'they did not attain'.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense
Added plural marker وا to show multiple people. This verb comes after لَمْ (not) in the ayah, showing they did not reach/obtain something.
This is a versatile particle that can mean 'what', 'that which', or 'not' depending on context. In this ayah, it means 'not' because it's being used to negate something.
The و connects this phrase to what came before. Here مَا is being used with نَقَمُوا to say 'they did not find fault' or 'they had nothing to criticize'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Word is in its final form showing multiple people (they) disliking something
This is a basic exception particle that introduces what is being excluded. Here it connects to أَنْ (that) to show the only reason for their resentment.
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) when it comes at the end of a word and is followed by a hamza. This is just a writing convention and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: that (connects أَغْنَىٰهُمُ to what came before)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence together. Here it connects the fact that Allah and His messenger enriched them (أَغْنَىٰهُمُ) with what came before.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
The أ at the beginning makes this verb mean 'to cause someone else to be free from need' (to enrich them)
The هُمُ at the end shows who received the action - they were the ones being enriched. This word is the object of أَنْ (that) in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'something worshipped'
Changed to the unique name for The One True God by adding ال and merging letters. The ُ shows this is the subject doing something in this sentence.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (sending) to a noun (person who sends/delivers)
The هُ suffix means 'his'. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb أغنى (enriched) along with الله.
This word is paired with اللَّهُ as the subject (doer) of the verb أَغْنَىٰهُمُ (enriched them)

steps.m: from (connecting فَضْلِهِۦ to what comes before)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing where something comes from or its source. Here it connects 'His bounty' (فَضْلِهِۦ) to the previous context.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to transform the root into a noun meaning 'bounty' or 'excellence'
Added kasra at the end because this word follows مِن (from) in the ayah

steps.m: of His bounty (referring to Allah's bounty)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show this is Allah's bounty, with an elongated pronunciation mark ۦ
This is a compound particle made up of فَ (then/so) and إِن (if). It connects conditions with their results.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb. The middle و becomes ا when used in a verb.
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show this happens in the present/future. The middle letter returns to و.

steps.m: they turn back/repent (if they do this, it will be better for them)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people are doing this action. This is part of a conditional statement in the ayah - if they repent, it will be better for them.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show present/future tense
The word is shortened because it comes after إِن (if) in a conditional phrase. The و and final ُ are dropped.
Added short 'a' (fatha) and silent 'y' marks to the root letters to make the word mean 'good/better'

steps.m: good/better (as the object of يَكُ - it will be)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word is receiving the action of the verb يَكُ (it will be) in the ayah
Starting with the basic preposition ل which means 'for' or 'to'
Added shadda because this word connects with the previous word in pronunciation

steps.m: for them (connected to يَكُ خَيْرًا - it would be better)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who the action is for. This word connects to يَكُ خَيْرًا (it would be better) in the ayah.
This combines وَ (and) with إِن (if) to connect another condition

steps.m: to be close to or take charge of something

steps.n: The shadda doubles the middle letter to show stronger action
Added يَتَ to make it a present tense action that someone does to themselves (like turning themselves away)
Added وْا to show multiple people are doing this action
Added shadda (double letter) to make the punishment stronger, and added vowels to show it happened in the past
Changed to present/future tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added sukun at end because word continues with a suffix
Added هُمُ to show who receives the punishment (them)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'something worshipped'
Changed to the proper name for God by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis.

steps.m: Allah (as the doer of أَغْنَىٰ - enriched)
steps.n: Added ُ to show Allah is the one who did the enriching (أَغْنَىٰ) in this verse. Changed alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun that describes the action. This word is the direct object (accusative case) of the verb يُعَذِّبْ (to punish), showing what Allah will do to them.

steps.m: a punishment (object of يُعَذِّبْ - will punish)
steps.n: The tanween (double marks) at the end shows it's indefinite (any punishment). This word is the direct object showing what Allah will do - He will punish them.
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective showing intense quality
Added tanween alif to show this describes the punishment (عَذَابًا) mentioned in the ayah
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it shows that something happens in or during this worldly life.
Root د-ن-و (close/near) became دُنْيَا (worldly life) by adding special pattern with damma, sukun, and alif at end
Adding ال makes it 'the worldly life' instead of just 'worldly life'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'last' or 'coming after' by adding ا after the first letter. Like changing 'late' to 'later'.

steps.m: the afterlife (what comes after this life)
steps.n: Added ة to make it feminine. This special ending helps create the word for 'afterlife' - the life that comes after this one.
Added ٱلْ at start to say 'the afterlife' and ِ at end to show it's connected to الدُّنْيَا (this world) in the sentence.
Added وَ at start to say 'and the afterlife', connecting it with الدُّنْيَا (this world) that came before it.
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen. In this ayah, it's used with نَقَمُوا (they found fault) to say 'they did not find fault'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects to نَقَمُوا (they found fault) to say 'and they did not find fault'
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added هُ which means 'him/it'

steps.m: for/to them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added م with sukun to change 'him' to 'them', showing this applies to multiple people
This is a basic preposition that shows location. Here it connects the word 'punishment' (عَذَابًا) with where it happens - in this world (ٱلدُّنْيَا).
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to form the basic noun
The word becomes definite with 'al' and takes the genitive case because it follows the preposition 'fi'
This is a basic preposition showing source or origin. In this ayah, it connects 'His bounty' (فَضْلِهِۦ) to the verb 'enriched them' (أَغْنَىٰهُمُ), showing where the enrichment came from.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed from an action (protected) to a title/role (protector) by adding shadda
Added tanween kasra to show this is the object of مِن (from/of) in the phrase 'they have no protector'
This combines 'and' (و) with the negative particle 'not' (لا)
Added ي between the last two root letters to show someone who does this action intensively

steps.m: any helper/supporter (in context: they have no helper on earth)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is any general helper, not a specific one. In this ayah, it's saying they have no helper at all on earth.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added 'them' to show who is being referred to
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect with previous statement
This is a primitive word (not derived from a root) used as a relative pronoun to refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to the person who made a promise to Allah.
Added shadda on the م for emphasis and stronger pronunciation since it comes after و

steps.m: to make a promise or covenant

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb. This word shows the subject making a promise directly to Allah in this ayah.
Changed the fatha over ع to an alif khanjariya (ٰ). This is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Changed to the proper name for The One God by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the object being covenanted with)
steps.n: Added shadda to show emphasis, fatha at end to show Allah is the object being made covenant with in عٰهَدَ (he made a covenant with), and changed alif to wavy alif for Quranic spelling
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if He gives us from His bounty'
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis. This makes the condition stronger, like saying 'I really mean it when I say if...'
Added hamza with fatha at start and alif after it to show past tense. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Added نَا at the end to show who received the action (us). The small alif (ٰ) helps connect the weak letter ى with the suffix نَا.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or its source. Here it connects to فَضْلِهِۦ (His bounty) to show the source of what they're hoping for.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create the basic noun meaning 'favor' or 'bounty'
Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition مِن (from)
Added هِۦ at the end to show it belongs to him
Basic meaning of being truthful
Added نَ at the beginning to mean 'we', and added a شدة (double letter) to make it mean 'give charity' instead of just 'confirm truth'

steps.m: surely we will give in charity
steps.n: The لَ prefix adds emphasis and indicates an oath or strong assertion

steps.m: surely we will definitely give charity
steps.n: Added نَّ at the end to emphasize the certainty of the promise being made to Allah
Basic meaning of existence or being
Added لَ (la) for emphasis + نَ (na) for 'we' + changed verb to present tense 'be'
Added emphatic نَّ suffix to add strong emphasis/assertion to the verb
Added 'and' to connect with previous statement

steps.m: and surely we will be indeed (promising to be among الصَّالِحِينَ)
steps.n: This word is part of a promise being made to be among the righteous people (الصَّالِحِينَ)
Basic preposition meaning 'from' - one of the most common Arabic prepositions
Added fatha (َ) at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الصَّالِحِينَ (the righteous ones). This makes it easier to pronounce.
Basic meaning of being good or proper
Root meaning 'good' became 'righteous one' and the -i ending shows it belongs to something/someone
The ين ending makes it plural for masculine words. The word is in genitive case because it comes after the preposition مِنَ (from/of).
Adding ال with shadda makes the word definite and emphasizes the meaning
Starting with the basic word for 'when', adding shadda to make it stronger
Adding alif at the end to complete the word for 'when'
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this happens after something else
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer
Changed hamza to ء with fatha, added alif, and changed final ي to ى to show past tense giving
Added هُم at the end to show who received the giving
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. Added kasra (ِ) under م to show it's a preposition.
Added shadda (ّ) on م because it comes after a word ending with a vowel (فَلَمَّآ). This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to give extra or do more than required

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun by adding vowel marks. This word refers to the extra good things Allah gives us.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because this word is owned by something and comes after the word 'from' (مِّن)
Added هِۦ at the end to show the favor belongs to Him. This word comes after 'from' (مِّن) in the ayah.

steps.m: to be stingy or hold back giving
he was/became stingy (with فَضْلِهِۦ - His bounty)
steps.n: Added fatha, kasra, and fatha to make the basic past tense verb. This word relates to how they acted with Allah's bounty mentioned earlier.

steps.m: they were/became stingy (with فَضْلِهِۦ - His bounty)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to change from 'he was stingy' to 'they were stingy' - وا shows a group of people did this action in the past
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning with, by, or in

steps.m: with/by/in it (refers to فَضْلِهِۦ - His bounty)
steps.n: Added هِ (hi) at the end to show this refers back to Allah's bounty (فَضْلِهِۦ) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added ۦ after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Added تَ at start and doubled ل to show doing action by choice. The ي changes to ى at the end because it's a weak letter.
Adds the conjunction 'and' to connect to previous phrase

steps.m: and they deliberately turned away (from following Allah's commands)
steps.n: Changes to plural to show many people did this action. The small circle (۟) shows where to stop when reciting.
Basic pronoun meaning 'they' (referring to the people who were stingy with what Allah gave them)
Added و to connect this word to what came before it, showing these actions happened together
Added shadda on و to help with pronunciation when connecting words
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding مُ at the start and changing vowels makes this describe someone who does the action
Adding ون shows this refers to multiple people
The doubled م adds emphasis to show they really/strongly turn away
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes it mean 'to cause' the original meaning. The word نِفَاقًا (hypocrisy) is what was caused to follow.

steps.m: and so he caused to follow
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this happened as a result of what came before

steps.m: and so He caused them to follow (نِفَاقًا hypocrisy)
steps.n: Adds 'them' as the object of the verb. Allah is the implied subject (He), and hypocrisy (نِفَاقًا) is what He caused to follow them.

steps.m: hypocrisy (showing faith outwardly while being empty of it inside)
steps.n: The pattern with kasra-fatha-alif creates a noun showing the state of being empty of faith while pretending to have it

steps.m: hypocrisy (as something that resulted in their hearts from فَأَعْقَبَهُمْ/so He caused them to inherit)
steps.n: Added tanween (double line ending) to show this is the specific hypocrisy that came as a result in their hearts
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows the hypocrisy is inside their hearts.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.
Changed the root into a noun meaning the physical heart, since hearts can turn/change
Changed the pattern to show many hearts by adding و and changing vowels to ُ
Added kasra to show possession
Added هِمْ to show the hearts belong to them
This is a primary preposition that has no derivational root. It exists in this form as a basic word in Arabic.
Added kasra mark under the alif to show correct pronunciation
Changed final ى to ىٰ (alif maksura with small alif above) which is the standard way to write this word in the Quran
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
The kasra (small diagonal line) at the end shows this word 'day' belongs to or is connected to something else
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: they meet him (on the Day they meet Him)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who they are meeting. This refers to meeting Allah on the Day of Judgment.
This is a preposition that shows how or why something happens
Added ما which means 'what' to ask about or refer to something. Together with بِ it means 'because of what'
The alif is written long (آ) to show it's at the end of the word. This word connects to يَكْذِبُونَ (they lie) in the ayah to mean 'because of what they used to lie'
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'to deliberately break a promise' instead of just breaking it

steps.m: they deliberately broke (their promise to اللَّهَ)
steps.n: The وا shows a group of people did this action. This word relates to breaking their promise to Allah mentioned later in the ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity'
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One God. The shadda shows emphasis.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يَلْقَوْنَ - they will meet)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is being acted upon - they will meet Him. Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb أَخْلَفُوا (they broke), showing what they broke. In this ayah, it refers to the promises they made to Allah.

steps.m: to promise or make a pledge

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added fatha marks to show this is something that happened in the past
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action
Added هُ at the end to show who was promised to
Added kasra to show how the letter is pronounced
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: and because of what (they did in أَخْلَفُوا۟)
steps.n: Added ما to make this refer to what they did. This connects to the verb أَخْلَفُوا۟ (they broke) that comes after it in the ayah.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter between two fathas
Added وا to show multiple people did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they lie (referring to the hypocrites mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Added plural marker ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers back to the hypocrites mentioned earlier who broke their promise to Allah.
This letter turns a statement into a question, showing surprise or drawing attention to what follows
Adding لَ makes the question stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'really' to show emphasis

steps.m: Did (they) not...? (expecting 'yes, they did' as answer)
