steps.n: Adding مْ adds the meaning of 'not' to make a negative question. In this ayah, it's asking 'Did they not know?' expecting the answer 'Yes, they did know.'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past-tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show it's happening now instead of in the past
Added وا with special marks to show multiple people are doing the action. This word comes after أَلَمْ (did they not) which is why it has this specific ending.
Starting with the basic connector word أن that joins sentences together

steps.m: that/indeed (emphasizes the connection between يَعْلَمُوا (they know) and what follows)
steps.n: Adding shadda makes the connection stronger and adds emphasis, like saying 'they should definitely know that...'
Added ال to make it specific and merged letters to form the proper name Allah

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يَعْلَمُوٓا۟ - they knew)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show this is the object of the verb 'they knew'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to sukun, and adding ُ at end. This word tells what Allah knows about - their secrets (سِرَّهُمْ)
Added shadda (ّ) to show emphasis and kasra (ِ) to make it a noun meaning 'something concealed/private'
Added هُمْ at the end to show it belongs to them. This word is what Allah knows (يَعْلَمُ) in this ayah.
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'secret talk'
The alif at the end (ىٰ) is just a writing style - it's the same word with the same meaning
Adding هُمْ shows this belongs to them

steps.m: and their private conversation (object of يَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous word. This word is what Allah knows (يَعْلَمُ) in this verse, along with their secrets (سِرَّهُمْ)
This word helps join ideas together by connecting what comes before it (they knew) with what comes after it (Allah knows). The shadda shows emphasis.
و at the start connects this clause with what came before it, joining multiple things that 'they knew'
Added ال to make it specific and changed the first letter, making it a unique name for the One God

steps.m: Allah (as the object being known in يَعْلَمُ - He knows)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a mark (fatha) at the end to show it's receiving the action of 'knowing', like saying 'knows Allah' instead of just 'Allah'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and shows teaching (making others know)
Changed from 'taught' to a title meaning 'knower', making it a special name showing Allah knows everything perfectly. Like changing 'taught' to 'teacher' but much stronger.
Added vowel marks to form basic noun from root
Changes from singular 'hidden thing' to plural 'hidden things' by adding extra vowels inside the word
ال at the start means 'the'

steps.m: of the hidden things (possessed by علّام/the All-Knower)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word belongs to علّام (the All-Knower), indicating Allah knows all hidden matters
This is a special word used to connect descriptions to people or things, like saying 'who' or 'which' in English.
Added ن at the end to make it refer to multiple people or things instead of just one.
Added ال at the beginning with a shadda to make it more specific, like saying 'the ones who' instead of just 'who'.

steps.m: to mock or find fault with others

steps.n: Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, with vowel markings showing it's happening now. This word describes what 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) are doing.
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers back to 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changes to plural form showing people who volunteer
The ت combines with ط to make it stronger
The word gained 'the' (ال) at the start to specify these particular volunteers, plus an ending that shows they are being talked about
This is a helper word that shows something comes from or is part of something else

steps.m: to trust, to believe in, to feel secure

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does the action. The مُ at the start and the vowels show this is someone who does the believing.
Added ٱلْ at the start to make it specific, like saying 'the' in English
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many believers, not just one

steps.m: the believers (being criticized by those who mock them)
steps.n: Added َ at the end because these believers are being acted upon - they are the ones being mocked in this verse

This is a preposition that shows the volunteers (ٱلْمُطَّوِّعِينَ) are doing something in relation to the charitable deeds (ٱلصَّدَقٰتِ). It connects these two words together by showing where or in what way the action happens.

steps.m: to give charitably, to be truthful

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun representing one instance of giving charity
Makes the word plural to show multiple charitable gifts
Adds 'the' (ال) to the word and doubles the first letter with a shadda
Adds kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in/concerning)
Starting with the basic relative pronoun form
Adding plural ending to show it refers to multiple people
Adding ٱلَّ at the beginning makes it mean 'those who' instead of just 'who'

steps.m: and those who (connected to previous phrase)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this phrase to what came before it
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَجِدُونَ (they find) into 'they do not find'. It's one of the most common ways to say 'no' or 'not' in Arabic.
Added the fatha mark (َ) to show how to pronounce the first letter correctly
Added يَ at beginning to show it's happening now, and added ِ and ُ marks to show how to pronounce it correctly

steps.m: they find (referring to the hypocrites who mock the believers)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show many people are doing it. In this ayah, it refers to the hypocrites who mock the believers who give charity.
This is a particle that restricts or excludes what comes after it from what came before. Here it shows that people only find their utmost effort, excluding anything more than that.
Changed from action to the result - shows how much effort or ability someone has
Added fatha to show this is the object being acted upon

steps.m: their effort (object of يَجِدُونَ - they find)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show the effort belongs to them. This word is what they find (يَجِدُونَ) in the ayah.

steps.m: to laugh at or make fun of someone

steps.n: Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he mocked/laughed at'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added فَ to show this happens because of something before it
Added ون to show many people are doing this action
Basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'of'
Added 'them' to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers to those who mock the believers who give charity.

steps.m: to mock or make fun of someone

steps.n: Adding vowels to show this is a completed action of mocking
Changed to the special form used only for Allah, with ال merged into the word
Added shadda to emphasize the ل and added ُ to show this is the doer of the action 'mocked' in this ayah

steps.m: from (preposition showing direction away from something)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows movement or action away from something. In this ayah, it's used with يَسْخَرُونَ (they mock) to show who is being mocked.

steps.m: from them (object of يَسْخَرُونَ - they mock)
steps.n: Adding هم at the end makes it refer to the believers (ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ) who are being mocked. The هم shows it's a group of males or mixed group.
This is a basic preposition showing that something belongs to or is for someone. Here it connects to the word عَذَابٌ (punishment) that follows.
Adding و at the beginning connects this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: and for them (referring to those who mock, connected to عَذَابٌ)
steps.n: Adding هم shows this refers to a group of people (specifically those who mock the believers mentioned earlier in the ayah)

Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this verb into a noun meaning the result of the punishment. This word is what they have in the phrase 'they have punishment' (لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ).

The double damma (ٌ) at the end shows this is any punishment, not a specific one. It's like saying 'a punishment' instead of 'the punishment'. This word is what they have in the phrase 'they have punishment'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (doing pain) to a strong adjective (very painful)
Adding tanween dammah makes this an adjective describing the punishment mentioned in the ayah
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added ٱسْتَ at the beginning to show seeking or requesting forgiveness
Changed vowels to make it a command form - kasra on second-to-last letter and sukun at the end
Third person masculine plural pronoun that shows who is being forgiven
Adds لَ (meaning 'for/to') before هُم to show they are receiving forgiveness
This is a basic joining word that connects two choices: 'ask forgiveness for them' or 'do not ask forgiveness for them'. It shows Allah giving Prophet Muhammad ﷺ a choice between these two actions.
Added fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'aw'
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it mean the opposite. In this ayah, it makes 'you ask forgiveness' into 'you do not ask forgiveness'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting forgiveness

steps.m: you seek forgiveness (condition in إِن تَسْتَغْفِرْ - if you seek forgiveness)
steps.n: Changed to present tense with ت for 'you' and added sukun at end because it comes after إِن (if)
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added هُمْ at the end to show who the action is for. This word is connected to ٱسْتَغْفِرْ (ask forgiveness) in this ayah, showing who forgiveness is being asked for.

steps.m: if (introduces condition about تَسْتَغْفِرْ - asking forgiveness)
steps.n: This is a conditional word that means 'if' and introduces the first part of an if-then statement. Here it connects 'if you ask forgiveness' with what happens as a result.

steps.m: to cover up or remove faults

steps.n: Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting forgiveness

steps.m: you seek forgiveness (in لَا تَسْتَغْفِرْ - do not seek forgiveness)
steps.n: Changed to present tense with ت prefix for 'you'. The sukun at the end shows it's in a command form after لا (don't).
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added هُمْ to show who the action is for. This word is connected to تَسْتَغْفِرْ (you ask forgiveness), showing who you're asking forgiveness for.
This starts with the basic number seven
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the basic number seven
Added ين at the end to change seven into seventy, like how we add -ty in English (seven → seventy)
Added fatha on first letter and shadda on middle letter to form basic past tense verb
Added ة to make it mean 'one instance' of the action

steps.m: one time/instance (of seventy times mentioned in سَبْعِينَ)
steps.n: Added tanween (ً) to show this is indefinite (like 'a' or 'an' in English). This word relates to سَبْعِينَ (seventy) in the ayah, indicating one of those seventy times.
This is a particle that negates future actions absolutely, like saying something will definitely not happen
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence. In this ayah, it connects to يَغْفِرَ (forgive) to say 'Allah will never forgive them' as a result of what was mentioned before.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed ending vowel to fatha to show subjunctive mood
Changed the concept of worship into a word meaning 'a deity' or 'god' in general
Changed from 'a god' to 'THE God' by adding ال and merging letters. The ُ shows this is the doer of the action in this sentence.

steps.m: they/them (referring to the hypocrites being discussed)
steps.n: This is a pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' for a group of males. In this ayah, it refers to the hypocrites that Muhammad ﷺ is being told about.

steps.m: for them (object of تَسْتَغْفِرْ - you ask forgiveness)
steps.n: Added لَ at the start to mean 'for' or 'to'. This word is connected to تَسْتَغْفِرْ (you ask forgiveness), showing who the forgiveness would be for.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is replaced with a special mark (alif khanjariya) to show distance
Adding ل with kasra adds emphasis and makes it clearer we're pointing to something far away

steps.m: that (referring to the previous statement about forgiveness)
steps.n: Adding ك at the end shows we're pointing to something specific that was just mentioned - in this case, referring back to what was said about asking forgiveness
This is a basic particle that emphasizes what comes after it, like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English
Added هُمْ (they/them) at the end to show who is being talked about

steps.m: because they (explains why اللَّهُ won't forgive them)
steps.n: Added بِ at the start to show reason or cause. This explains why Allah won't forgive them in the previous part of the verse.
The root meaning includes the idea of covering up or rejecting truth
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb meaning 'he disbelieved'
Added وا and changed the last vowel to ُ to show multiple people did the action. This word explains why Allah won't forgive them - because they disbelieved.
This is the unique proper name of God in Islam. The word comes from the root meaning 'to worship' but has become a specific name.

steps.m: by/with Allah (object of كَفَرُوا - they disbelieved)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this is what they disbelieved in, and ِ at the end to connect it to the sentence. The ٱ is just a different way to write the same sound as ا.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
The و after second root letter creates pattern فَعُول indicating someone who delivers messages frequently
This word is in the genitive case because it shows possession - it is Allah's messenger. The suffix shows possession, like saying 'the book of Ali' - 'Ali's' shows possession.
The suffix هِ means 'His' and refers to Allah in this context
The 'وَ' is a conjunction meaning 'and'. It joins words or phrases together.
This is a unique word that comes from the root meaning of divinity but has become the proper name of God in Islam. The double ل with shadda makes it special.
The ُ at the end shows that Allah is the one doing the action in this sentence

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject of لَا يَهْدِى)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement. Allah is the one who does not guide in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb تَسْتَغْفِرْ (seek forgiveness) that follows it in this ayah. Together they mean 'do not seek forgiveness'
This is the basic meaning showing the action of guiding someone or something to the right path
Added fathas and an alif at the end to make 'he guided' in the past tense
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels
Adds the long vowel ى to complete the present tense verb

steps.m: to stand firmly, rise up, establish
a group of people who stand together (object of يَهْدِى - guides)
steps.n: The root 'stand' became 'people/group' (like people who stand together). The fatha shows it's receiving guidance.
The ال makes it specific/definite, and the ending َ shows it's receiving the action
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
This pattern shows who does the action
Makes the word plural (three or more)
Added ال to make it specific and َ at the end because it's being acted upon by the verb يَهْدِى (guides). The long ا is written as ٰ in this word.

steps.m: he became happy (subject of the ayah, referring to المخلفون - those who stayed behind)
steps.n: Added vowels to show this is about feeling happy in the past. In this ayah, it refers to those who stayed behind being happy about not going to fight.

steps.m: to stay behind, to come after

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter changes meaning to intensive/causative form

steps.m: one who is left behind (passive participle)
steps.n: Adding مُ at beginning shows who receives the action of being left behind

steps.m: the one who is left behind
steps.n: Adding ال makes the word definite

steps.m: the ones who were left behind (subject of فَرِحَ - they rejoiced)
steps.n: Adding ُونَ makes it plural masculine. This word is the subject of فَرِحَ (they rejoiced) in this ayah.

steps.m: to sit, stay behind, remain behind

steps.n: Changes the verb into a noun meaning the place where the action happens. In this ayah, it refers to the place where they stayed behind instead of going to battle.

steps.m: their place of staying behind (refers to those who stayed behind from battle)
steps.n: هِمْ at the end shows this belongs to them (the people who stayed behind)

steps.m: because of their staying behind (from joining رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: بِ at the start shows the reason or cause - they were happy because of their staying behind from joining the Messenger of Allah

steps.m: to differ from or go against

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to differ' into noun meaning 'difference/opposition'

steps.m: against/in opposition to (رَسُولِ اللَّهِ - the Messenger of Allah)
steps.n: Added long alif to strengthen the meaning and fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon. This word shows how they were acting against the Messenger of Allah.

steps.m: to send or deliver a message

steps.n: The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Added kasra at the end to show this messenger belongs to or is connected to Allah, as we can see from the next word اللَّهِ in the ayah
Added shadda to show emphasis on the ل
Changed alif to wasl-alif and added kasra at the end to show possession. This word is connected to سَبِيلِ (path) to mean 'path of Allah'.
Root letters got vowels to make past tense 'he' form of feeling hatred
Adds conjunction meaning 'and'

steps.m: and they hated (referring to المخلفون - those who stayed behind)
steps.n: Changes subject from singular 'he' to plural 'they'. The long vowel و and hamza above indicate emphasis in recitation. This word refers back to المخلفون (those who stayed behind) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
This is a connecting word that introduces what comes next. Here it connects to the word يُجٰهِدُوا۟ (they strive/fight) to show what was disliked - 'that they should strive/fight'.

steps.m: to put in effort or struggle

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding alif makes it mean doing something with great effort or against resistance
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Root م-و-ل becomes plural by adding أ at start and ا after و to mean 'wealth/money/possessions' (like going from 'money' to 'monies')
Adding the preposition ب meaning 'with'
Added kasra at end because it comes before a possessive pronoun
Added هِم meaning 'their' with proper diacritics and sukun at end
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a basic noun
Changed from single soul to plural souls by adding 'a' at start and changing middle vowels - used for groups of souls
Added 'and' to connect with previous phrase about wealth (أَمْوٰلِهِمْ)
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by someone
Added suffix to show these souls belong to them (referring to those who stayed behind)
Basic preposition showing location or state. Here it connects with تَنفِرُوا (go forth) to show where not to go forth.
Added ي between second and third letters to make the word a noun
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by Allah (اللَّهِ) in the phrase 'path of Allah'
Connected the root letters and added vowels to form the basic noun meaning 'deity'
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال and joining letters together
Added kasra at the end to show possession. In this ayah, this word shows who the messenger belongs to - the Messenger of Allah.
Added vowels and changed middle letter و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has و in middle). This makes it past tense.
Added وا at the end to show many people (3 or more) did the action
Added وَ at beginning to connect this with the previous statement about how they hated to strive in Allah's cause
This is a negative particle that tells people not to do something. Here it's telling them 'do not' before the verb تَنفِرُوا۟ (go forth).
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added ت at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: you (all) go forth (in response to لا - don't)
steps.n: Added وا to show plural 'you all' and the small alif (۟) as a pronunciation guide. This is part of لا تَنفِرُوا۟ meaning 'do not go forth'
This is a basic preposition showing location or position. Here it's used with سَبِيلِ (path) to show 'in the path'.
The ي changes to ى (alif maqsurah) at the end because it's at the end of the word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Doubling the ر makes the heat more intense
Added ال to make it specific/definite (like 'the' in English)
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, and 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.
The و (weak letter) changes to ا and we add َ (fatha) on the ن to make the basic word for 'fire'
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this word is doing something. Here, it's the subject of أَشَدُّ (more intense), making the phrase 'the fire is more intense'.

steps.m: Hell (the proper name for the fire of punishment)
steps.n: This is a proper noun borrowed from Hebrew 'Gehenna', referring to the place of punishment in the afterlife. The shadda on ن shows emphasis.

steps.m: Hell (as the object being described as having intense heat)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is being described by what comes after it (أَشَدُّ حَرًّا - more intense in heat)
Connected the root letters with fatha and added shadda to show doubled final letter
Added أَ at beginning and ُ at end to make it comparative, showing something is more intense than something else
Added shadda to show intensity of the heat
Added an alif and two dots (tanween) at the end to show this word is receiving the action
This is a basic conditional word that introduces 'what-if' scenarios that didn't actually happen. It's like saying 'if only' or 'what if' in English.
The shadda (ّ) was added to make the 'L' sound stronger because it comes after they said (قَالُوا۟). This adds emphasis to what they're saying, like when you stress an important word in English.

steps.m: to be or exist (in a state or condition)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a special type of verb that always makes this change in the past tense
Added وا at the end to change 'he was' to 'they were'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show this happens in the present/future
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added لْ to make it a command/suggestion
Added فَ to connect it to what came before
Added وا to make it plural (many people)
Added ي between the second and third root letters to show this is a quality or state of being little/few

steps.m: a little (describes how much they should laugh in فَلْيَضْحَكُوا)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how much the action happens. This connects to the verb فَلْيَضْحَكُوا (let them laugh) by telling us they should laugh only a little.
Connected the root letters and added fatha marks. The ي changes to ى at the end because it's a weak letter.
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows it happens in the present. The ى changes back to ي.
Adding لْ changes it from a statement to a command meaning 'let him'
Adding وَ connects this command to what came before it
Adding وا changes it from singular (him) to plural (them). The ي at the end is dropped.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
This pattern makes it describe how much of something there is

steps.m: much/a lot (describing how they will weep)
steps.n: The ًا at the end shows this word is describing how the action of weeping (يَبْكُوا) will be done

steps.m: to repay or give what is due

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed the word to show the result of the action rather than the action itself
Added tanween to show this is indefinite and in the accusative case
Added extra mark after tanween for pronunciation
Starting with the basic particle ما which refers to 'what' or 'that which'

steps.m: because of what (they were يَكْسِبُونَ earning)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show cause/reason. This connects to يَكْسِبُونَ (they were earning) to explain why they should cry - because of what they were earning through their actions.
Adding the fatha (َ) makes it a past tense verb
Adding another fatha (َ) completes the past tense verb pattern
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action. This word is connected to يَكْسِبُونَ (they earn) in the ayah.

steps.m: to gain, earn, or acquire something through effort

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels to match present tense pattern

steps.m: they gain/earn (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers back to the disbelievers mentioned earlier who are being warned about their actions.
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition, like saying 'if something happens'

steps.m: and if/then if (connecting to what comes after رَّجَعَكَ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what comes after. It's like saying 'then if' or 'and if' in English
Added vowels (a-a-a) to make it mean 'he returned' in the past tense
Added shadda to emphasize the action and make it explicitly transitive
Added ك at the end to show that 'you' are the object being returned (by Allah)

steps.m: He brought you back (with emphasis) (after فَإِن)
steps.n: Added shadda on first letter because it comes after فَإِن which assimilates its ن into the following ر
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Added the u-vowel at the end to show this is the subject
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific
Basic preposition indicating direction or destination. The alif at the end is written with a small mark above (ىٰ) which doesn't change the meaning - it's just a writing style.
Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'one complete circle' by adding vowels
Changed to active participle form showing who does the action
Makes the word feminine, often used for groups or collections
Added a small mark (ٍ) at the end because it comes after the word 'to' (إلى), and stretched the first vowel (ā)
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it belongs to
The letter ن merges with the letter after it, making a stronger sound. This is a common rule in Arabic reading.

steps.m: from/of them (referring to طَآئِفَةٍ - a group)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show we're talking about 'them' (the group mentioned earlier in the ayah)
This pattern creates a noun from the root
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning 'to permit'
Added است at beginning to show seeking or requesting permission
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Added ك to show the permission was sought from 'you'
Added فَ to show this happened as a result of something before it
This is the basic past tense form of the verb
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun that represents the action itself
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: for the going out (asking permission for going out)
steps.n: Added لِ (for/to) at the beginning and kasra at the end because it follows the preposition لِ
The middle letter و is dropped and we add ُ and ْ to make it a command. This tells one person to do something.

steps.m: then say! (connected to فَإِن رَّجَعَكَ - if He returns you)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this command comes after something else. In this ayah, it comes after 'if Allah returns you to them'.

steps.m: will not (negates يَخْرُجُوا۟ - they will go out)
steps.n: This is a frozen negative particle that makes future actions impossible. It has no root or derivational morphology.

steps.m: will never, absolutely will not (negates يَخْرُجُوا۟ - they will go out)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) makes the 'l' sound stronger, which adds emphasis to show this is a strong or absolute 'no'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: you (all) go out (in response to لَّن meaning 'never will')
steps.n: The وا suffix changes the verb from 'she goes out' to 'you all go out', making it plural and changing who does the action
This is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'along with'
Added ي with kasra (ِ) to show this means 'with me'. The ي at the end shows it's talking about me.

steps.m: with me (in the phrase 'you will never fight with me')
steps.n: Added fatha (َ) at the end because this word is connected to تُقٰتِلُوا۟ (you will fight) in the phrase 'you will never fight with me'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form a noun meaning 'forever'

steps.m: forever (describing لَّن تَخْرُجُوا - you will never go out)
steps.n: Adding ًا makes this word describe how the action happens - they will not go out forever. It's connected to the verb تَخْرُجُوا (you will go out).
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it (تُقٰتِلُوا۟ - you will fight) into a negative future tense (you will never fight). It's like adding 'not' to 'will' in English.

Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects the two things they will never do: they will never go out with the Prophet AND they will never fight with him.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The alif makes it mean two parties fighting each other, not just one person killing
The تُ at the start shows it's happening to someone (you/she), not by someone
Changed from passive (being fought) to active (you fight), and added وا to make it plural 'you all'. Used with لَن to mean 'will never fight'
This is a basic particle meaning 'with', not derived from a root. Added fatha marks to show pronunciation.
Changed the last vowel to kasra (ِ) to prepare for connecting with the pronoun
Added ى with fatha to mean 'me', showing who the word 'with' connects to
This root carries the basic meaning of opposition or hostility
Changes from basic root to word meaning 'enemy' by adding specific vowel pattern
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning and show this is a permanent quality

steps.m: an enemy (as object of تَخْرُجُوا - you go forth)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of the verb تَخْرُجُوا (you go forth)
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English.

steps.m: indeed you all (emphasizes that رَضِيتُم 'you were pleased')
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this is talking about multiple people. This connects to the next word رَضِيتُم (you were pleased) to emphasize that they were definitely pleased.
This root carries the basic meaning of being content or satisfied with something
The و changes to ي when we add the vowels. Added َ (fatha) on ر, ِ (kasra) on ض, and َ (fatha) on ي to show this happened in the past
تُم at the end shows this is talking about a group of people being addressed
Changes verb 'to sit' into noun meaning 'the act of sitting'
Adds 'with/by' meaning through بِ prefix
Shows this word is affected by رَضِيتُم (you were pleased with)

steps.m: to be first or beginning of something

steps.n: Changed root letters into a simple word meaning 'first' by adding vowel marks
Doubled the middle letter to make the word stronger/more emphatic
Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb
Changed the verb 'to pass' into a noun meaning 'one time' by doubling the middle letter and adding ة at the end
Added tanween (two dots under) to show 'a time/instance' - connects to 'first' to mean 'first time'
Changed the root into a command by adding a sukun at start and damma in middle - tells one person to sit
Added helper alif at start to make pronunciation easier
Added وا at the end to make it plural - now it's telling multiple people to sit
Added فَ at start to connect this command with what was said before
This is a basic word that shows being together with someone or something. In this ayah, it's used twice to show the hypocrites won't go out or fight together with the Prophet.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does the action
Added plural ending to show multiple people who stay behind

steps.m: the ones who stay behind (with مَعَ)
steps.n: Added the definite article to specify these particular people who stay behind. The word follows مَعَ (with) in the ayah.
This is the basic negation particle that means 'not' or 'do not'. In this ayah, it's used with تُصَلِّ (pray) to say 'do not pray'.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what comes before it. This connects two different commands in the ayah.
Root letters ص-ل-و became صَلَّ by adding a shadda (double letter) to show doing lots of prayer, with fathas for past tense
Adding ت at start changes it from 'he prays' to 'you pray' or 'she prays'
The ُ mark shows this is happening now, not in the past
Changed from 'you pray' to 'is prayed for/over' (passive form)
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation - fatha on ع and ل
Added alif maksura with superscript alif to show proper pronunciation of final vowel

steps.m: upon (something that will be mentioned next in the ayah)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ٓ) to connect this word to what comes after it. In this ayah, it connects to قَبْرِهِۦٓ (his grave)
Connected the root letters together with fatha to create the basic word for 'one'
Added the tanwin kasra to show this word is affected by the word عَلَىٰٓ (upon) that comes before it
Basic preposition showing something comes from somewhere else
The م gets doubled (gets a shadda) because it comes after a word ending in ن

steps.m: from them (referring to those who died while rejecting faith)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show this means 'them'. In this ayah, it refers to those who died while rejecting faith.
The root letters got vowels (fathas) and the middle letter و changed to ا to make 'he died'
Added shadda on initial م for emphasis and smooth pronunciation after connecting with previous word
Added fatha to connect the letters together and make it a noun

steps.m: forever (describes how long not to pray for them)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how long the action lasts. This connects to the verb تُصَلِّ (pray) in the ayah, telling us to never pray for them.
This is the basic negation word that means 'not' or 'no' in Arabic. It's used to negate what comes after it.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word connects to تُصَلِّ (pray) in the ayah, making the meaning 'and do not pray'.
The root meaning of standing/rising became associated with a group of people who stand together
Changed root letters into a command by adding damma (u sound) - telling someone to stand up
The weak letter و is dropped and replaced with sukun because it's between two vowels
Adding ت at start makes it 'you' (2nd person)
This is the basic form of the preposition showing position or location above something. In this ayah, it shows position above someone's grave.
Added an alif that looks like a small stick (ٰ) at the end to show how to pronounce the word correctly. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to bury or place in a grave
steps.n: Basic meaning of the root
Changes from verb 'to bury' into noun meaning 'grave'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by something
Added هِ with extension marks to show this is specifically 'his' grave. This word follows على (upon) in the ayah.
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added the pronoun هُمْ (they) to show who is being referred to. This word connects to كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The وا at the end shows this refers to a group of men or mixed group who did this action in the past. This word describes the same people who died (مَّاتَ) in this verse.
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation of Allah's name
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Added بِ at beginning to show 'by/in' and added ٱ (alif with wasl) as a pronunciation helper
This form makes it a plural noun meaning 'messengers'
Adding و changes it from a basic verb into a noun meaning someone who does this regularly
Added و at beginning to connect this with the word الله that came before it in the ayah
Added kasra at the end to show this messenger belongs to someone (in this case, it will be Allah)
Added هِ at the end to show this is specifically Allah's messenger. The small ۦ mark is just to help with pronunciation.
The root letters are arranged to form a basic noun meaning death
Connected the root letters with vowels. The middle و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action

steps.m: and they died (connected to كَفَرُوا۟ - they disbelieved)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this action with the previous phrase about them disbelieving. Also added the small و mark (۟) to match the Uthmani script.
Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation - damma (ُ) shows it's the subject form of 'they', and sukun (ْ) shows the end of the word
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word connects to فٰسِقُونَ which describes what 'they' are.

steps.m: to break away from what's right
steps.n: The word becomes a noun describing the action

steps.m: he broke away from what's right
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: one who breaks away from what's right
steps.n: Changed the word to describe someone who does this action

steps.m: one who breaks away from what's right (nominative)
steps.n: Added the tanween damma to show it's indefinite and nominative

steps.m: those who break away from what's right (describing هُمْ/they)
steps.n: Added ون to show it's talking about a group of people. This describes the pronoun هُمْ (they) in the ayah.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word negates the verb تُعْجِبْكَ (impress you/amaze you) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense form
Changed from past to present tense by adding تُ and changing middle vowels

steps.m: it amazes you (subject: أَمْوٰلُهُمْ - their wealth)
steps.n: The كَ shows that 'you' are the one being amazed by their wealth
Forms the basic singular noun by adding vowels and combining the root letters
Changes from single wealth (مَال) to multiple wealth (أَمْوَال) by adding أ at start and وا in middle
Adds damma (ُ) at the end to show this word is in the subject position
Added 'their' to show possession. This word is the subject of تُعْجِبْكَ (amazes you) in this ayah.

steps.m: to give birth or produce offspring

steps.n: Root letters joined with short vowels (fatha) to make the word for child
Changes from single child to many children by adding أ at start and ا after و
Adds و at the beginning to connect this word with the previous word أَمْوٰلُهُمْ (their wealth)
Adds ُ to show this is the subject along with أَمْوٰلُهُمْ (their wealth)
Adds هُمْ at the end to show these are the children of the disbelievers mentioned in the context
This is a basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger, like adding 'indeed' in English
Adding مَا changes the meaning to be more restrictive, like saying 'only' or 'just'. In this ayah, it restricts what Allah wants (يُرِيدُ) to what comes after.
The middle letter و changes to ي because of vowel patterns
Added يُ at the beginning to make it present tense (happening now) and ُ at the end to show Allah is the one doing the wanting/desiring
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject

steps.m: that (connects to يُعَذِّبَهُم - He punish them)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that joins two ideas together. Here it connects Allah's will (يُرِيدُ) with what He wants (to punish them). It's like saying 'Allah wants THAT He punish them'
Added a shadda (double letter) to make the action stronger and more intense, like going from 'hurt' to 'really hurt'
Added يُ at start to make it present tense (happening now) instead of past

steps.m: he punishes them severely (object of أَن)
steps.n: Added هُم to show who receives the action. This word comes after أَن (that) in the ayah, showing Allah's intention.
This is a basic preposition that shows how something is done

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to their wealth and children)
steps.n: Added هَا which refers back to their wealth and children (أَمْوٰلُهُمْ وَأَوْلٰدُهُمْ) mentioned earlier in the verse
This is a basic preposition showing location or time. In this ayah, it connects to الدُّنْيَا (this world) to show where Allah wants to punish them.
The word came to specifically refer to the worldly life (this world) as opposed to the afterlife
Adds ال to specify we're talking about THE worldly life (not just any worldly thing)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: it departs/passes away (subject: أَنفُسُهُمْ - their souls)
steps.n: The تَ shows this is happening in the present. This word describes what happens to 'their souls' mentioned right after this word in the ayah.

steps.m: and it departs/passes away (subject: أَنفُسُهُمْ - their souls)
steps.n: وَ connects this to the previous phrase about Allah wanting to punish them
Added vowel marks to form the basic singular noun
Added أَ at start to make plural form - like changing 'self' to 'selves'
Added هُمْ to show possession by them (masculine plural)
Added vowel marks to show proper pronunciation - ُ (damma) shows it's 'hum' and ْ (sukun) shows to stop at 'm'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. In this ayah, it connects 'they' to the state of being disbelievers (كٰفِرُونَ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changes from a verb (he rejected) to a noun meaning 'one who rejects'
Makes the word plural to show multiple people who reject truth
Adds the nominative case ending to show this is the subject
This is a basic time/condition word that means 'when' something happens
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Changed the final alif to an extended alif (ا → آ) for emphasis in pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changing the vowels makes it passive - something is being sent down instead of someone sending it

steps.m: it (feminine) was sent down (referring to سُورَةٌ/chapter)
steps.n: Adding feminine marker ت to indicate feminine subject
Changed the root into a noun by adding damma, making it mean 'a wall that encloses'
Added ة to show one complete unit, like one chapter that encloses its verses
Added tanween to show this is indefinite (any chapter). This word is the subject of أُنزِلَتْ (was sent down) in this ayah.

This is a particle that connects parts of a sentence and introduces what comes next. Here it introduces the command 'believe!' (ءَامِنُوا۟). It works like saying 'that' in English when reporting what someone said or was told to do.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added alif to make it mean 'believe with faith' instead of just 'trust'
Changed from past tense 'he believed' to a command telling someone to believe - notice the middle vowel changes from a to i
Added وا to make it a command for a group
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one true deity, making it a proper name
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by something
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is being done with or by means of Allah
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to show active participation or mutual action
Changed from past tense 'he struggled' to command form 'struggle!' by changing middle vowel to 'i' and end vowel to 'u'
Added وا to make it a command for a group
Added و with fatha at start to mean 'and'
This is a preposition that shows being together with someone or something. In this ayah, it connects to رَسُولِهِ (His messenger) to show who people are being with.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Changes from a verb (sending) to a noun (person who sends/delivers)
Adds kasra at end to show the word is affected by a preposition
Added هِ to show it belongs to Him (Allah)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show asking for the action instead of doing it
Added ك at the end to show the permission was asked from 'you'
This is a special word that starts with hamza and damma to show ownership. It's used to describe people who have certain qualities.
Added وا at the end to show this refers to a group of people who possess something. This word is connected to قَالُوا۟ (they said) in the ayah, showing these are the people who spoke.
Changed the action into a noun meaning wealth/abundance
Adding ال makes it specific. The ط gets a shadda because it comes after ال
Adding kasra shows this word belongs to أُولُوا (those of/possessors of). The alif at the start changes to a wasl form (ٱ) because it's connected to the previous word.
Basic preposition indicating source, origin, or separation from something
Added the pronoun هُمْ to show who is being referred to. This refers to the wealthy people (أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلطَّوْلِ) mentioned in the previous ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Added وا to show many people (3 or more) did the action
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement

steps.m: to leave something or someone alone

steps.n: The و is dropped because it's a weak letter at the start. Added vowels to make it a command form.

steps.m: leave us! (object of قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show who is being left. This word appears after قَالُوا (they said) to show what they said.
Changed root letters into a command form by adding vowels

steps.m: we be (with القٰعِدِينَ - those who remain behind)
steps.n: Added نَ at the start to mean 'we'. The word connects with 'those who remain behind' to show 'we be with them'
Basic particle meaning 'with' - used to show accompaniment or being together with someone/something
The م gets doubled (with shadda) for emphasis and smooth pronunciation since it comes after و
Changes to active participle form to describe someone who does the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who remain behind from battle.
Adding ين makes it plural, referring to the group of people who stayed behind from battle
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific group - those who chose to stay behind from battle
Final kasra marks genitive case for plural noun
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed middle vowel to ُ and added وا at the end to show multiple people. This word is connected to يَكُونُوا۟ (they are) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows how or by what means something happens

steps.m: by the fact that (introduces reason for رَضُوا۟ - they were pleased)
steps.n: When بِ combines with أَن, it introduces a reason or explanation. Here it explains why they were pleased (رَضُوا۟)
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people. This word connects with مَعَ ٱلْخَوَالِفِ (with those who stay behind)
Basic form of the word that shows being together with someone or something
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. In this ayah, it connects to ٱلْخَوَالِفِ (those who stay behind) to show who they are 'with'
Changes the root word into a plural noun meaning 'those who stay behind' by adding وَالِف after the first letter
Adds ال to make it specific (like 'the' in English). This word refers to those who stayed behind from battle.

steps.m: with the ones who stay behind (after مَعَ)
steps.n: Adds kasra at the end because it comes after the word مَعَ (with)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: it was sealed (subject of the verb)
steps.n: Changed vowels to show that the action happened to something rather than something doing the action. This word is connected to قُلُوبِهِمْ (their hearts) which were sealed.

steps.m: and it was sealed (connected to رَضُوا - they were pleased)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement about them being pleased to stay behind
Basic preposition showing elevation or being above something
Added vowel marks and connected letters to form the preposition. This word connects to قُلُوبِهِمْ (their hearts) to show what was sealed.
Added the small alif (ٰ) after the ى which is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning
The heart is named this way because it can turn or change direction. It's the center of changing emotions and thoughts.
Changes the word to show we're talking about multiple hearts
Shows this word is owned by something or connected to what comes after it
Adds هِمْ to show these hearts belong to them
This is the basic pronoun for 'they' that will be the subject of the verb 'understand' (يَفْقَهُونَ)
Adding فَ shows this is happening because of what was mentioned before - that their hearts were sealed

This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it (يَفْقَهُونَ - they understand) into its opposite meaning (they do not understand). The fatha (َ) is added to show it's being used as a complete word.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he understood) to present tense (he understands) by adding يَ at the start

steps.m: they understand deeply (referring to the hypocrites mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: The plural form shows multiple people understanding. In this ayah, it refers to the hypocrites who don't understand because their hearts are sealed.
This is the basic form of the word that shows contrast with what came before
The kasra at the end connects this word to what follows, introducing the next subject (the Messenger)
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
The letter ر has a shaddah (double mark) to show it connects with the word before it
Added 'the' at the beginning to show we're talking about a specific messenger
Added ُ at the end to show this word is doing something in the sentence
Starting with the basic relative pronoun that points to someone or something
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding و at the beginning connects this to the previous phrase about the Messenger
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding the long alif at the start changes the meaning from just 'feeling safe' to actively 'believing/having faith'. Like going from feeling safe to putting your trust in something.
The وا at the end shows that many people (all male or mixed group) did this action in the past. This word is the subject of جٰهَدُوا۟ (they strived) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced

steps.m: with him (referring to the Messenger ٱلرَّسُولُ)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who is being accompanied. The ۥ mark is just to help with pronunciation. This word connects to ٱلرَّسُولُ (the Messenger) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to strive or struggle against difficulty

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added alif after first letter to show this is an intense or two-sided action - like striving against something

steps.m: they strived hard (referring to الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ - those who believed)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show a group of people did this action. The alif is written as ٰ when وا is added. This refers back to 'those who believed' mentioned earlier in the verse.
The root letters م-و-ل combine to make مال, where و changes to ا - this is a common pattern for words meaning things you can own or possess
Changed from singular to plural by adding أ at the start and bringing back the و that was hidden in the singular form
Added preposition بِ meaning 'with/by'

steps.m: with their wealth/properties (referring to those who believed with the Messenger)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show possession by a group. The alif changes to a long alif (ٰ) when connected to the ending.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Changed to broken plural by adding hamza prefix
Added conjunction to connect with previous word بِأَمْوٰلِهِمْ (with their wealth)
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by someone

steps.m: and their souls/selves (referring to those who believed with the Messenger)
steps.n: Added pronoun suffix to show who the souls belong to, referring back to الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا (those who believed)

steps.m: those (pointing to the believers mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a demonstrative pronoun that points to people who are far away. Here it refers back to the believers (الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا) who strived with their wealth and themselves.

steps.m: those (pointing to the believers mentioned before)
steps.n: Added the standard vowel marks and elongation signs to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: and those (pointing to the believers mentioned before)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this word to the previous statements about the believers who strived with their wealth and themselves
Added fatha to the letter lam to show its pronunciation
Added هُ (him) to show who the preposition is directed to
Changed from singular (him) to plural (them) by adding مُ
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a basic noun
Adding ات makes it plural, showing multiple good things
Adding ال makes it specific, and ُ shows it's the subject of the sentence
This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to things or people that are far away
Added hamza and kasra at the end for proper pronunciation
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'
Added ك at the end to make it more specific and emphatic, pointing to the particular believers just mentioned who strived with their wealth and themselves
Added optional pronunciation marks (small alif and sukun) that don't change meaning

steps.m: they/them (referring to a group of males)
steps.n: This is a basic pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' for a group of males. It's not derived from a root.

steps.m: they (referring to a group of males, before ال)
steps.n: When هُمْ comes before a word starting with ال like ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ, the sukun (ْ) changes to a damma (ُ) to make it easier to pronounce.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding مُ at start and changing vowels shows who does the action
Adding ون makes it refer to multiple people who succeed

steps.m: the successful ones (those who have achieved true success)
steps.n: Adding ال (the) makes it 'the successful ones' and the َ (fatha) at the end shows it's plural and complete
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start makes it mean 'to make something/someone do the action'
Changed the letters into a word meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel marks
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
This is a basic preposition showing that Allah prepared Paradise FOR them
هُمْ means 'them' (for a group of people). In this ayah, it refers to the believers who Allah has prepared gardens for.
Adding vowel marks creates basic past tense verb form
The ة makes it into a noun meaning 'an enclosed space', which came to mean 'garden' since gardens are enclosed spaces
Adding ات makes it plural - more than one garden. This word is what Allah has prepared (أَعَدَّ) for them (لَهُمْ).
The ٍ shows this word is receiving the action of Allah's preparation (أَعَدَّ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show present tense feminine, changed vowels to show ongoing action. This refers to the gardens (جَنَّاتٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added kasra (ِ) to help with pronunciation. In this ayah, it connects the verb 'flows' (تَجْرِى) with 'beneath it' (تَحْتِهَا), showing where the flowing happens.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make it pronounceable
The -i ending shows this word is connected to 'gardens', telling us what's beneath them
Adds هَا (meaning 'its/their') to show the gardens are what's being referred to underneath
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Verb 'to flow' changed to plural noun meaning 'rivers' by adding أ at start and changing middle vowels
Added ال to make it specific rivers being talked about
Added damma to show this is the subject of the verb تَجْرِى (flows/runs) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show who does the action by adding alif after first letter and kasra after second letter

Shows something is inside something else
Added ها which refers back to the gardens (جَنَّاتٍ) mentioned earlier in the ayah. This shows who or what is being referred to.

The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) above the ذ
Added ل with kasra (ِ ) to change 'this' to 'that' by adding distance

steps.m: that (referring to the great success mentioned after)

Changes verb 'to succeed' into the noun 'success'
Added ٱلْ to make it specific
Added ُ to show this is the subject being referred to by ذٰلِكَ (that is)

Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this with the previous part, like saying 'and'
Added shadda to show doing something a lot
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'person who' and changed inside vowels to show it's someone who does this a lot

steps.n: Added ال to point to specific people. This word is the subject of جَآءَ (came) in this ayah.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something comes from or starts
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word ٱلْأَعْرَابِ (the Bedouins). This helps make the words flow together smoothly when reading.

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb

steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose/intent - 'in order to' or 'so that'
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added هُ (him/it) to show who the action is for

steps.m: for them (referring to المعذرون - those making excuses)

steps.m: Allah (as the object being referred to)

Adding conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'

steps.m: those who (plural, refers to كَذَبُوا۟ - they who lied)
steps.n: Added ن at the end to make it plural. This word refers to those who lied about Allah and His messenger in this ayah.

steps.m: to reject, deny, or cover up truth

steps.n: This is the basic past tense form showing one person did the action. This word is part of the group that سَيُصِيبُ (will afflict) refers to.
The suffix وا changes the subject from 'he' to 'they (masculine plural)'. These are the people who will be afflicted according to سَيُصِيبُ in the ayah.
Added a small alif (۟) above the word as a pronunciation helper to show the long vowel sound
Added sukun to show the end of the preposition part

steps.n: Added the pronoun هُمْ (them) to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to those who lied about Allah and His Messenger.

Adding ي makes the pain more intense
Adding tanween makes it indefinite (like saying 'a' in English)
Adding vowels to make it a usable past tense verb meaning 'is not'
This is a preposition that shows something is being imposed against or upon someone/something. In this context, it's used to show there is no burden against the weak ones.
The vowels show this is about becoming weak (not just being weak)
Changed the word pattern to create a plural noun for people with this quality
Added the definite article to specify these particular weak ones
Added kasra at the end because this word follows عَلَى (upon/for) in the ayah
Starting with the basic negation word لا which means 'not'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is a basic preposition that shows something is physically or metaphorically on top of something else. In this ayah, it's used with ٱلْمَرْضَىٰ (the sick) to show there is no blame upon them.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he became sick' to 'sick ones' by making it a plural noun - putting a sukun on ر and adding ىٰ at the end shows it means multiple sick people
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific, like saying 'the sick ones' instead of just 'sick ones'

steps.m: and not (connecting to previous statement about who is exempt from blame)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement about exemptions
This is a preposition that shows something is placed upon or affecting something else. In this ayah, it shows that no blame or difficulty is placed upon the sick people.

steps.m: those who (points to specific people who لَا يَجِدُونَ - do not find)
steps.n: Added ال at beginning to make it specific. The ل gets a shadda because it combines with the following letter ذ (called solar assimilation)

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense form

steps.m: they find (referring to those who cannot find anything to spend in charity)
steps.n: Changed from 'he finds' to 'they find' by adding ون at the end - makes it plural (many people)

Adding ون shows that many people are doing the action. This refers back to the people mentioned in لَا يَجِدُونَ (those who do not find)
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it into a noun describing the state of being restricted or in difficulty
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject of لَيْسَ (there is not). In this ayah, it means there is no difficulty or blame on certain people.
This is a basic word that refers to a specific point in time

steps.m: when (connects to نَصَحُوا۟ - they were sincere)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end makes it refer to future or conditional time. Here it introduces what happens when they are sincere.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did the action

Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word is connected to the verb نَصَحُوا (they were sincere/loyal) in the ayah.

steps.m: upon/against (shows no blame is upon المحسنين)

Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and', and added hamza with sukun as a helper for pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from a past-tense verb (he forgave) to a special form showing Allah's constant and intense quality of being forgiving

Adding ٱلَّ at the beginning makes it point to someone specific we're talking about
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one person

steps.m: what (that you could use to carry them with)
steps.n: Added fatha to show this is asking about something specific - in this case, what means of transport or resources could be used to carry them.

steps.m: upon him/it (referring to what they could spend on)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show who/what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to what they could spend on.

Added و with fatha (َ) at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, showing these wealthy people are being talked about

steps.m: rich people (who are doing something in the sentence)

steps.m: to seal/stamp (specifically putting a mark or closure on something to prevent access)

steps.m: their hearts (referring to those who ask permission)

Added اِ at beginning and تَ after first root letter to show doing action to oneself or mutually

steps.m: to excuse or give a reason

Added وا at the end to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: Allah (the one true God) (subject of سَيَرَى - will see)
