Changed the word from an action to multiple pieces of news or information
Added كُمْ to show the news belongs to multiple people being spoken to. This word is receiving the action of نَبَّأَنَا (He informed us) in this ayah.
Changed from basic root 'to see' into past tense 'he saw' by adding fathas and using ى at the end
Added يَ at start to show present tense. The middle أ is dropped because it's between two vowels.
Added سَ to show this happens in the future

steps.m: and He (Allah) will see (subject of اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this to what came before. The subject is Allah as shown by اللَّهُ in the next word.
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special divine name
Allah has a special spelling with shadda on ل, starts with a connecting alif, and has a damma mark because it's doing the action (informing us)
Adding vowel marks makes this a basic past tense verb
Added plural 'your' ending. This word is what Allah will see (سَيَرَى) in this ayah
The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'

steps.m: His messenger (subject with Allah of سَيَرَى)
steps.n: Added ُهُ to show it belongs to Him (Allah). This word joins with Allah as the subject of سَيَرَى (will see)

steps.m: and His messenger (subject with Allah of سَيَرَى)
steps.n: The وَ at the start means 'and'
This is the basic particle that shows something happens after something else
Added the vowel marks ُ (u) and ّ (doubled letter) to show how it's pronounced. In this ayah, it shows what happens after Allah and His Messenger see their deeds.
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show doubled middle letter
Added تُ at start to make it 'you' and changed vowels to show someone is being returned (not doing the returning)
Added ون to show this applies to multiple people. This word connects to عٰلِمِ (Knower) in the ayah, showing who you are returned to.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination
The small alif (ىٰ) at the end shows this is connected to what comes next in the ayah - it points to Allah as the Knower (عٰلِمِ)
Changed from action to person who does the action
The alif is written as a long vowel mark (ٰ) above the ع instead of as a full letter
The kasra at the end shows this describes Allah and owns what comes after it (الْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهٰدَةِ - the unseen and the witnessed)

steps.m: to be hidden or absent from view

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding vowel marks. This makes it mean the thing itself rather than the action of being hidden.

steps.m: the unseen (connected to عٰلِمِ - knower of)
steps.n:
1. Added ال at the beginning to make it specific ('the unseen' instead of just 'unseen')
2. Added ِ at the end because it's connected to the word عٰلِمِ (knower of) before it, showing they go together like 'knower of the unseen'
Adding fatha vowels to create basic verb structure
Changed first vowel to kasra and added alif after second letter to create abstract noun pattern
Added و (meaning 'and') at beginning + changed ending to ة to make it a concrete noun meaning testimony
Adding definite article makes the noun definite
Adding kasra for genitive case
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and shows the action is done thoroughly
Changed to present/future tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added فَ to show this happens after something else

steps.m: then He will inform you all (about بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ)
steps.n: Added كُم to show who is being informed. In this ayah, Allah is telling them He will inform them about what they used to do.
This is a basic preposition that shows how something is done or what it relates to
Added ما which means 'what' to make a question word. In this ayah, it refers to the actions they used to do.
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
The middle weak letter و drops out and changes the vowels around it
Added تُمْ to show this refers to multiple people being addressed (you all). This word is connected to تَعْمَلُونَ (you all do/work) in the next part of the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' are doing it, and changed vowels to show it's happening now
Added ون at the end to show many people are being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Added سَ at the beginning to show this will happen in the future
This is the base word - the proper name of God in Arabic. The shadda shows emphasis on the ل.
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is being acted upon by something else (in this case, the بِ before it)

steps.m: by Allah (object of سَيَحْلِفُونَ - they will swear)
steps.n: The بِ at the beginning means 'by' and shows what they are swearing by. The ٱ is added before the ل when it has the shadda.

Added vowel marks: fatha (َ) on ل to show it's a preposition meaning 'to/for', damma (ُ) on ك to show it's connected to the next letter, and sukun (ْ) on م to show end of word. This word is the indirect object of سَيَحْلِفُونَ (they will swear), showing who they are swearing to.
This is a basic time word that connects two events. Here it connects 'they will swear' with 'when you return'.
Added vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce it: 'i-dha'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding اِنْ shows the action happens to or by oneself
Added sukun to prepare for adding the past tense suffix
Adding تُمْ shows the subject is 'you all' in the past tense
Added vowel marks to the basic preposition إلى which means 'to' or 'towards'
Changed the final ى to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is a spelling change that happens when adding pronouns to إلى.

steps.m: to them (returning to them in انقَلَبْتُمْ)
steps.n: Added هِمْ which means 'them' (referring to the hypocrites mentioned earlier). This word connects to انقَلَبْتُمْ (you return) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed first vowel to damma and middle vowel to kasra to show something was done to it
Added ت at start to show a female person is doing the action
Added damma at end to show this is happening now
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: so that you all turn away (from عَنْهُمْ)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show purpose - this explains why they will swear by Allah
This is a preposition that shows moving away from something

steps.m: away from them (referring to the hypocrites mentioned in يَحْلِفُونَ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show it refers to 'them' (the hypocrites who will swear). This connects back to the people doing the swearing (يَحْلِفُونَ) in the previous part of the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed from 'he turned away' to a command meaning 'you turn away!' by adding أ at start and changing the middle vowel to kasra (i sound)
Added وا to make it a command to multiple people
Added فَ to show this is a result of what was mentioned before (their swearing by Allah)
This is a preposition that shows moving or turning away from something
Added attached pronoun هم meaning 'them' (masculine plural). This word shows who you are turning away from in the phrase لِتُعْرِضُوا۟ عَنْهُمْ (so that you turn away from them).
This is a common emphatic particle that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English.

steps.m: indeed they (emphasizes that they are رِجْسٌ/filth)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which means 'they/them' (masculine plural). This connects to the next word رِجْسٌ (filth) to emphasize that they are truly filthy.
Changed the root into a noun describing their state. This word is describing 'them' (إِنَّهُمْ) in the ayah, saying they are impure.
The مَ at the beginning shows this is a place where the action happens (like how 'mosque' is a place for prayer). The ى at the end is because the last root letter is weak.
Adds و at the beginning to connect this with the previous phrase about them being unclean (رِجْسٌ)
Adds هُمْ at the end to show this is their shelter. The next word جَهَنَّمُ tells us this shelter is in Hell.
Added basic vowel marks to show pronunciation of this borrowed word. The word comes from Hebrew 'Gehinnom' meaning 'Valley of Hinnom', which became associated with punishment in the afterlife.
Added shadda (double letter mark) on ن to emphasize the strength and intensity of the word's meaning, which is common in Arabic for words describing powerful concepts.
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence, meaning Hell itself is being discussed.

steps.m: to give back what is due (good or bad)

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding fathas (َ) between letters
Changed from a verb (action) to a noun (thing)

steps.m: as a reward/repayment (for what they used to earn)
steps.n: Added tanween (double vowel mark) at the end to show this is a reward being given to someone
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning by/with
Added مَا which is a special word that means 'what/that which'. When combined with بِ it creates the meaning 'because of what'. In this ayah, it refers to what they were doing (يَكْسِبُونَ).

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb

steps.m: they were (referring to the hypocrites mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did this action in the past
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they gain/earn (referring to the hypocrites mentioned earlier in the verse)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers to the hypocrites mentioned earlier who will swear by Allah.

steps.m: to make a strong promise or oath

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are doing this
Basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'for'
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to you all (object of يَحْلِفُونَ - they swear)
steps.n: Added كُمْ which means 'you all'. This word is receiving the action of swearing in the ayah - they are swearing to you all.
Added vowels (fatha, kasra, fatha) to the root letters to make 'he was pleased' in the past tense
Changed from 'he was pleased' in past to 'you' doing it now/future by adding تَ at start
Added plural marker وا to show multiple people

steps.m: so that you all become pleased (with عَنْهُمْ)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why they are doing the action mentioned in the previous word (يَحْلِفُونَ - they swear)
Basic preposition meaning 'about' or 'concerning'
Added هُمْ at the end to show this is about 'them'. This word connects to تَرْضَوْا۟ (you are pleased) in the ayah, showing who you might be pleased with.
Basic conditional particle meaning 'if'
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement about being pleased with them

steps.m: to be pleased with or approve of

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowels 'a' and 'i'
Changed to present tense with 'you' as the subject
Added plural marker to show multiple people are being addressed
The small circle shows the plural waw is shortened
This is a basic preposition that shows distance or relation
Adds the masculine plural pronoun 'them' to show who the action is directed towards. This word is connected to تَرْضَوْا۟ (you are pleased) in this ayah, showing who you might be pleased with.
This is a basic emphatic word that adds certainty to what comes after it. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English.

steps.m: for indeed (connects to لَا يَرْضَىٰ - does not approve)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. It connects the previous statement about people being pleased with them to Allah not being pleased with them.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
The small alif makes the 'aa' sound longer
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'

steps.m: Allah (as the object being referred to)
steps.n: The word for 'god' (ilah) became the special name 'Allah' by combining 'al' (the) with 'ilah' and changing how it's written. The fatha mark at the end shows Allah is being talked about
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَرْضَىٰ (is pleased) negative. In this ayah, it's saying Allah is not pleased with the disobedient people.
Added fatha mark on the ل to show how it's pronounced

steps.m: to be pleased with or approve of something

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowels 'a' and 'i'
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense. The weak letter و changes to ى at the end. This verb connects to the phrase 'with the people' using عَنِ.
Added superscript alif (ٰ) as a pronunciation guide to show the long vowel sound
Basic preposition meaning 'away from' or 'concerning'
Added kasra at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الْقَوْمِ (the people)
Basic noun form meaning a group of people who stand together
Adding ال makes it refer to specific people rather than just any people
The kasra ending shows this word is being possessed by or connected to something else
Added vowels to connect the root letters together

steps.m: one who breaks away from obedience (describing القوم)
steps.n: Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action. This word describes القوم (the people) in this ayah.

steps.m: those who break away from obedience
steps.n: Makes the word plural to show it's talking about many people
Adds 'the' to make it specific and adds a final vowel to show its connection to القوم
The root ع-ر-ب becomes أعراب by adding أ at the start to make it mean 'desert dwellers/bedouins' (like how we say one bedouin = عربي but many bedouins = أعراب)
Adding ال makes it specific/definite. The ُ shows this word is the subject doing the action of being أَشَدُّ (stronger/more intense) in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to make an adjective showing quality
Added أَ at beginning and changed pattern to show comparison. This word is comparing the intensity of something to كُفْرًا وَنِفَاقًا (disbelief and hypocrisy) mentioned later in the ayah.

steps.m: to reject or hide the truth

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun that names the action itself, like changing 'reject' to 'rejection'

steps.m: rejection (of truth) (object of أَشَدُّ - more intense)
steps.n: The ًا shows this word is being described as 'more intense' by the word أَشَدُّ. Like saying 'The Bedouins are stronger in rejection.'
Changed the root into a noun meaning the act or state of being hypocritical
The ًا shows this word is receiving the action. It's connected to أَشَدُّ (more intense), showing what the desert Arabs are more intense in.
Adding وَ connects this word to the previous word, like saying 'and'
Adding أ at the beginning makes it comparative, showing 'more' or 'most'. This word is being compared with أَشَدُّ (more intense) in describing the qualities of ٱلْأَعْرَابُ (the Bedouins)
The ُ shows this word is a subject like أَشَدُّ before it
Adding وَ connects this word to أَشَدُّ (more intense) before it, listing multiple qualities of the Bedouins
Combined the two separate words أَنْ (that) and لَا (not) into one unit

steps.m: that not (introduces what يَعْلَمُوا۟ - they know)
steps.n: The ن from أَنْ merges into the ل creating a doubled letter (shown by ّ). This is a common pronunciation rule when ن meets certain letters.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding يَ at the start shows this is happening now or in the future
Adding وا makes it talk about a group of people instead of one person

steps.m: to set a limit or boundary

steps.n: Changed the root into a plural noun meaning multiple limits or boundaries by adding ُ (damma) on the first and second letters and و between them. This pattern is often used for important rules or limits in Islamic law.
The َ (fatha) at the end shows this word is receiving the action. These are the limits that the bedouins are less likely to know, according to the verse.
Added fatha to show this is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' or 'that which'
Changed the alif to alif madd (آ) to show extended pronunciation. This is a common spelling variation in the Quran that doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it refers to 'what Allah revealed.'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he sent down (subject of what اللَّهُ sent down)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning makes someone cause the action instead of doing it themselves - like changing 'went down' to 'made go down/sent down'. In this ayah, Allah (اللَّهُ) is the one doing the sending down.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال at the beginning and the shadda on ل make it specific and definite. The ُ at the end shows this is the subject of the sentence.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or about something else. In this ayah, it connects to رَسُولِهِ (His messenger) to show what something is upon.
Added an alif mark (ٰ) at the end to show the long vowel sound. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot
The ِ at the end shows this word is being possessed by something
Adding هِۦ at the end means 'his'
The word already has هِ ('his') and ۦ (elongation mark) - no transformation occurred between before/after
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. It's a unique word that doesn't come from a typical root.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing something in the sentence
Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement
Changed the alif to a waṣla form since it comes after a connecting و

steps.m: One who knows completely (attribute of Allah)
steps.n: Added ي between ل and م and vowels to show this describes someone who has complete knowledge. When used for Allah, it means His knowledge is perfect and complete.
The ٌ at the end shows this is describing Allah in this sentence, like saying 'Allah is All-Knowing'

steps.m: to judge wisely or make wise decisions

steps.n: Forms an adjective meaning 'one who is characterized by complete wisdom'. This word describes Allah in this ayah, emphasizing His perfect wisdom.
The tanween (double damma) here shows this is a complete attribute, emphasizing Allah's perfect wisdom
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
The و at the start connects this word to the previous ayah
The َ (fatha) at the end connects this word to الأعراب (the Bedouins) that follows it

steps.m: to be nomadic, to wander in the desert

steps.n: The root about desert life becomes a plural noun meaning 'desert people/bedouins' by adding أ at start

steps.m: the bedouins (who are mentioned in يَتَّخِذُ - they take)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it specific bedouins, and the ِ shows this word follows مِنَ (from/among). These bedouins are the subject of the verb يَتَّخِذُ (they take).

This is a basic word that introduces someone or something we're talking about. It's like saying 'the one who' or 'whoever' in English. In this verse, it refers to the person who does the action يَتَّخِذُ (considers).
Added the fatha mark (َ) above the م to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'man'.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the beginning and added ت with shadda to show doing something for oneself
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start
This is a basic word that means 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it's connected to the word يُنفِقُ (spends), showing what is being spent.
Added fatha mark on م to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to spend or give away (especially money/resources)
steps.n: This is the basic concept of spending or giving away resources
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Added يُ at beginning to show present tense and ُ at end to show this is the subject doing the action. This word refers to 'whoever' (مَن) in the verse.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added مَ at the beginning and changed vowels to show this is now a noun meaning 'the thing that must be paid'

steps.m: a required payment/fine (object of يَتَّخِذُ - considers/takes)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is what is being acted upon by the verb يَتَّخِذُ (considers/takes). Some Bedouins consider their required spending to be this kind of burden.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: to carefully watch and wait (doing it to oneself)
steps.n: Added ت at start and doubled middle letter to show someone doing this action carefully to themselves
Adds present tense prefix for 3rd person masculine singular

steps.m: he carefully watches and waits (as a fact)
steps.n: Added ُ at the end to show this is stating a fact rather than a possibility

steps.m: and he carefully watches and waits (for something bad to happen to you all)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the start to connect this with the previous part. In this ayah, it describes hypocrites waiting for something bad to happen to the believers.
Added kasra to the letter ب to make it readable
Added the plural pronoun كم meaning 'you all' to show who the preposition affects
Added damma at the end to help connect with the next word

steps.m: to go around or change direction

steps.n: Root letters connected with short vowels (fatha) to make a noun meaning 'turning'
Changed the word to plural by adding ائِ in the middle, turning one bad event into many bad events

steps.m: the bad turns (of fortune), the calamities
steps.n: Added ٱل (the) at the beginning and doubled the د with shadda to show it's specific calamities being talked about

steps.m: the bad turns (of fortune), the calamities (as object)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
When adding pronouns to على, we add ي with a sukun. This helps connect the preposition to the pronoun.
Added هِمْ at the end to show who the action is happening to - in this case, 'them'.

steps.m: to go around or change direction
something that comes around (like a turn of events)
steps.n: Changed و to آئ to show something that keeps happening or coming around
Added ة to show we're talking about one specific instance of something coming around

steps.m: the turn (of bad fortune) (subject of عَلَيْهِمْ)
steps.n: Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is doing the action in the sentence
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun describing the harmful thing itself
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes before it in the sentence
This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Arabic.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show Allah is the subject doing something in this sentence

steps.m: and God (as the subject of سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement. The word Allah is the subject of the adjectives سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (All-Hearing, All-Knowing) that follow.
Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone who hears in a perfect way

steps.m: All-Hearing (a name of Allah describing His perfect ability to hear everything)
steps.n: Added ٌ (tanween) at the end to show this is a complete quality, used here as one of Allah's perfect names
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to the special form used for divine attributes to show this is an eternal, perfect quality of Allah
Starting with the basic preposition مِن which means 'from'
Added an alif at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word (ٱلْأَعْرَابِ)
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Root letters got short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make a noun meaning 'Arab/desert person'
This form creates a broken plural showing multiple desert dwellers/bedouins
Added the definite article ال and kasra at the end because this word is the subject of يُؤْمِنُ (believes) in this ayah
This is a word that means 'whoever' or 'anyone who'. In this ayah, it refers to the people who do the action of believing (يُؤْمِنُ). Think of it like saying 'whoever believes' in English.

steps.m: to trust or feel secure in something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense Form IV verb, which makes the meaning causative. The hamza changes to و after يُ prefix. This word connects to 'Allah' with the preposition بِ showing what is believed in.
Changed from active 'he believes' to passive 'he is made to believe' by changing vowels
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity
Added a double L sound (shadda) to change from 'any deity' to specifically mean Allah, the One True God
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is being done with or by Allah

steps.m: by/with Allah (object of يُؤْمِنُ - believes in)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because it's being acted upon by the verb 'believes in'. The first letter changes to a special alif (ٱ) since it connects to the 'bi' before it.
Added وَ (and) at the start and ال (the) to make it 'and the day'

steps.m: and the day (connected to what follows)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to what follows it in the ayah
Added vowel marks and connected letters to make the active participle form, meaning someone/something that comes last

steps.m: the last (one) (connected to اليَوْمِ - the Day)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite (like 'the' in English) and kasra at the end because it's describing 'Day' (اليَوْمِ) in the phrase 'the Last Day'
This is the basic root meaning
Adding ت shows the person is taking something for themselves
Adding the vowel marks helps show how to pronounce the word correctly
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels

steps.m: and he takes for himself (what he spends as قُرُبٰتٍ)
steps.n: Adds و to connect this verb with what came before. This verb describes how some Arabs take their spending as means of getting closer to Allah.
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it refers to what is being spent and connects to the verb يُنفِقُ (he spends) that comes right after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb

steps.m: he spends (referring to من who believes)
steps.n: Changes past tense to present tense by adding يُ at start, ِ in middle, and ُ at end. This word describes what the person (من) who believes does with their wealth.

steps.m: to come close, to get near
steps.n: This is the basic root meaning
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the state of being close

steps.m: a way to get closer (to Allah)
steps.n: Adding ة makes it mean one specific way of getting closer to Allah
Makes it plural (more than one way) and indefinite (any ways)
Adding kasra to the first letter changes this from an action into a word that shows location or closeness to something
Adding fatha at the end shows this word connects to the word after it (اللَّهِ) to mean 'near/with Allah'
Added shadda to emphasize the uniqueness and greatness of Allah

The هَا suffix adds a feminine pronoun meaning 'it' which refers back to قُرْبَةٌ (means of closeness) since قُرْبَةٌ is feminine in Arabic.

Added damma and sukun to make it into a noun meaning the state of being near

Added ون to make it plural (many people)

Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and ا after first root letter to show who does the action
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: to do good, to make beautiful, to excel
doing good in the best way
