steps.n: Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) to show this is the final form. This word follows عَلَىٰ (upon) in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it's made of
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word (اللَّهِ). This combination means 'from Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters with shadda
Added kasra at the end to show possession, since this word is possessed by رِضْوٰنٍ (pleasure/satisfaction) in the phrase 'pleasure of Allah'

steps.m: to be pleased with or approve of something
steps.n: Basic meaning of being content or pleased
Changed from basic root letters to noun form meaning 'pleasure' by adding vowel marks (kasra and sukoon)
Added ان to make it mean 'great pleasure' instead of just 'pleasure'
Added tanwin (ٍ) to show this is indefinite - like saying 'a' pleasure rather than 'the' pleasure
Added و at beginning to connect this word with تَقْوَىٰ (God-consciousness) mentioned before it
Basic meaning of goodness or being better
Changes the word into a concrete noun meaning 'goodness'
Makes the noun indefinite and in the nominative case

steps.m: or rather (connecting two questions, with the second questioning the first)
steps.n: This is a question particle that connects two alternatives, where the second part (after أَم) questions or expresses doubt about the first part. Here it connects 'Is one who built on fear of Allah better...' with 'or rather one who built on a crumbling edge?'
Basic form of the relative pronoun that means 'who' or 'whoever'
Added shadda for emphasis. In this ayah, it's used to ask 'who?' when comparing two types of people - those who build on piety versus those who build on weak foundations.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he firmly established/laid strong foundations (of بُنْيٰنَهُۥ - his building)
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter shows he did this action thoroughly and firmly. This word describes how someone built their foundation, contrasting two types of builders in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changes from verb 'built' to noun meaning 'building' by adding special vowels and ان ending
Adds 'his' and shows this is what is being built. This building belongs to the person who established it on either piety or a crumbling cliff edge (mentioned in the ayah).
Basic preposition showing something is physically or metaphorically above or upon something else
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show this is connecting to the word تَقْوَىٰ (righteousness) in the ayah, describing what something is built upon
Basic meaning of being at the brink or edge
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The weak letter و changes to ا at the end. This word is connected to جُرُفٍ (cliff) in the ayah, showing what kind of edge it is.
Basic meaning of erosion or being swept away

steps.m: a cliff or bank that's been eroded
steps.n: Adding the vowel marks ُ (damma) changes it from a verb to a noun meaning the result of erosion

steps.m: of a cliff (that is crumbling) (describing شَفَا - edge)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this describes the edge (شَفَا) mentioned earlier in the ayah. The cliff is described as crumbling because the next word هَارٍ means 'crumbling'.
Basic meaning showing the concept of falling or collapsing
Added vowel marks (fatha and kasra) to show something/someone that is falling
The weak letter و changes to ا because it comes between a fatha and a kasra
Added tanween kasra to show this word is describing جُرُفٍ (cliff) in the genitive case
Basic meaning showing the concept of falling or collapsing
Added vowel marks (fathas) to make it mean 'it collapsed' in the past tense
Added اِنْ at the beginning to show the action happens automatically without someone causing it
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of what came before. The اِ changes to ٱ after فَ.
Basic preposition that shows how something is done or connected
Added pronoun هِ (him/it) to show what the preposition connects to. This word connects to فَٱنْهَارَ (so it collapsed), showing what collapsed with.
Added ۦ mark to show proper pronunciation of هِ
Basic preposition showing location or position inside something

steps.m: in (location inside نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ - the Fire of Hell)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it shows location inside Hell-fire, connecting to the words that follow it (نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ).
The weak letter و changes to ا, and we add fatha to make the basic word for 'fire'
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after the word 'in' (فِى) and is connected to the word 'Hell' (جَهَنَّمَ) that comes after it
This is a proper noun for Hell, borrowed from Hebrew 'Gehinnom'. It's the object of the preposition فِى (in) in this ayah.
Added shadda to double the ن letter, which is a common feature in words borrowed from other languages to match Arabic pronunciation patterns.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of the preposition فِى (in).
This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Islam.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence

steps.m: and God (as the subject of لَا يَهْدِى)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. This word is the subject of the verb لَا يَهْدِى (does not guide) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that tells us Allah does NOT guide the wrongdoing people. It directly affects the meaning of the verb يَهْدِى (guides) that comes after it.

steps.m: to guide or show the way
steps.n: Basic meaning of guiding or showing the way
Changed the root letters into past tense by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) to make 'he guided'
Added يَ at the start to show it's happening now. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) and the second vowel changes to kasra (ِ). The final ى shows it's a special type of word ending in ي.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning 'a group that stands together' - which came to mean 'people'
Added ال to make it definite (the people) and َ at the end to show it's the object receiving the action of guidance in يَهْدِى (guides)
Basic meaning of doing wrong or oppressing
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes from showing the action to showing who does the action
Makes the word plural to show many people who do this action

steps.m: the people who harm/treat unfairly (object of يَهْدِى/guides)
steps.n: Adds 'the' at the start and doubles the ظ letter. This word is being acted upon by يَهْدِى (guides) in this ayah - Allah does not guide these people.
This is one of the basic words in Arabic that can't be broken down further. It makes things negative, like adding 'not' in English.
Added the fatha mark (َ) to show how to pronounce it. In this ayah, it combines with يَزَالُ to mean 'does not cease' or 'continues'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he ceases/stops (negated by لَا to mean 'does not cease')
steps.n: Added يَ at beginning to show present/future tense and ُ at end to show it's doing the action. This word is negated by لَا before it, making it mean 'does not cease'.
This root relates to constructing or building things
Root 'build' changed to 'building' by adding damma (u) and sukun (stop mark) marks
Adding ان makes it mean a complete building rather than just the act of building
Changed alif to alif maqsurah for pronunciation
Added هُمُ to show possession and ُ to connect with following word
Added kasra vowel mark to show basic pronunciation

steps.m: the one who/which (referring to بُنْيٰن - building)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to make it more specific, like adding 'the' in English. This word refers back to their building (بُنْيٰن) mentioned earlier.

steps.m: the one who/which (referring to بُنْيٰن - building)
steps.n: Changed spelling to match Uthmani script: replaced ا with ٱ, added shadda on ل, and changed ي to ى. These are just spelling changes that don't affect the meaning.
Added fatha vowels and changed ي to ى because it's at the end of the word. This makes it past tense.
Adding و at the end changes 'he built' to 'they built'
Added وا ending to show multiple people did the action. This word is part of a longer phrase about their building not ceasing to cause doubt.

steps.m: to doubt, be uncertain, or feel suspicion

steps.n: Root letters combined with kasra (i) sound to make the noun meaning 'doubt'
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific instance of doubt

steps.m: a doubt (as something affecting قُلُوبِهِمْ - their hearts)
steps.n: Adding tanween (the -an sound) shows this is 'a doubt' in general, not a specific doubt
This is a basic preposition showing location or position. It has no root since it's a particle. In this ayah, it shows the location where the doubt (رِيبَةً) exists - in their hearts.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with basic vowel pattern to form singular noun
Changes from one heart to many hearts by adding 'u' sounds inside the word
Added هِمْ to show possession by them
This is a basic particle that shows something is being excluded from what was mentioned before. It's like saying 'but not this thing' in English.
Added alif at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word.
Changed the alif to a long alif to show it connects with the next phrase 'that their hearts are cut into pieces'
This is a connecting word that joins two ideas. Here it connects 'their building' with what happens to it - 'that their hearts are cut into pieces'.
Added the fatha mark (َ) above the first letter to show how to pronounce it correctly
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding shadda on middle letter shows the action is done with more force or multiple times

steps.m: it became completely cut up (describes قُلُوبُهُمْ - their hearts)
steps.n: Adding تَ at the beginning shows the action happens to itself - their hearts become cut up on their own
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the singular noun 'heart'
Changed the internal vowel pattern and added و to make it plural

steps.m: their hearts (in relation to the hypocrites mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show the hearts belong to them (the hypocrites)
This is a unique word that is the proper name of God in Arabic. It likely comes from combining ال (the) with إله (deity), but is treated as a single unit.
Added shadda on ل to show it is pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence - Allah is the one who is All-Knowing, All-Wise
Added و at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it. The small alif (ٱ) is added to help pronunciation.
Added basic vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added ي to show this is a permanent, intense quality of Allah
Added two dots (tanween) above to show this is a complete quality of Allah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Forms an intensive adjective describing Allah's perfect wisdom. This word pairs with عَلِيمٌ (All-Knowing) in this ayah to show Allah's complete knowledge and wisdom.
This is the basic conditional word meaning 'if'
Adding the shadda changes this from a conditional word to one that adds emphasis to what follows. Here it emphasizes that Allah is the one who purchased.
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding shadda and replacing the first letter with ل
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily). Also changed the initial alif to a wasl form since it connects with the previous word.
Added basic past tense voweling to connect the root letters
Added اِ at start and ت between first two letters to show doing action for oneself, changing meaning from 'sell' to 'purchase'
Added ى at end to show the long 'aa' sound
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to
Added َ (fatha) at the end to connect smoothly to the next word (اللَّهِ). This is like adding a connecting sound to make the words flow better when speaking.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'one who believes/trusts', like changing 'trust' to 'trustworthy person'
Added ين at the end to show there are many believers
Added ال at start to mean 'the' and َ at end because this word is being acted upon by اشْتَرَىٰ (Allah purchased)
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a word meaning one self or soul
Changed from singular 'self' to plural 'selves' by adding أَ at the start - a common plural pattern
Added fatha at end to show this is the object being purchased by Allah in the verse

steps.m: their selves/souls (being acted upon by اشْتَرَىٰ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show these are the believers' souls that Allah is purchasing
Root letters م-و-ل become مَال (māl) - a simple noun meaning one piece of wealth
Forms broken plural showing multiple types or instances of wealth (used here because the ayah is about believers giving up all their different forms of wealth)
Shows this is the believers' wealth that Allah is purchasing, connecting it back to المؤمنين mentioned earlier in the ayah
Connects 'their wealth' with 'themselves' (أَنفُسَهُمْ) as the two things Allah is purchasing from the believers
This is a basic particle that connects and emphasizes what comes after it
Adding بِ at the beginning changes 'that' to 'because' - it shows the reason for something
Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added هُمُ which means 'them' to show who the preposition is referring to. This word connects to الْجَنَّةَ (Paradise) in the ayah, showing what they will have.

steps.m: to hide or protect by covering completely
steps.n: Added the shadda (doubled letter) to show complete covering

steps.m: a garden (a place completely covered with plants)
steps.n: The ة makes it refer to a place that is covered with plants, creating the meaning of garden
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific, ultimate garden - Paradise

steps.m: Paradise (as the promised reward in لَهُمُ)
steps.n: The َ (fatha) at the end shows Paradise is the object - it's what is being promised to them

steps.m: to fight, to kill in battle

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Adding alif shows mutual action - fighting against each other
Adding يُ at start and changing vowels shows ongoing action

steps.m: they fight/are fighting (in سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ - the cause of Allah)
steps.n: Adding ون shows multiple people doing the action. The alif is written as ٰ in this style.
Basic preposition showing where/how the fighting happens - in Allah's path/way
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how to pronounce it correctly
Changed the final ي to ى which is just a writing style difference - it means the same thing

steps.m: to travel, to take a path

steps.n: Adding ي and vowels changes it from a verb into a noun meaning 'path'
Added kasra at the end to show this is possessed by Allah in the phrase سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ (path of Allah)
Removes the kasra (i) sound at the end that shows it connects to 'Allah'
Added shadda to show emphasis of the letter ل
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif at start. This shows that this is Allah's path being discussed in the verse.
Adds يَ at start to show ongoing action, with َ (fatha) on يَ and ْ (sukun) on ق to connect letters properly

steps.m: they kill/slay (happening now or in future)
steps.n: Adding ون at the end changes 'he kills' to 'they kill' - shows more than one person doing it
Adding فَ shows this follows from previous action (fighting in Allah's cause mentioned earlier in verse)
Added vowels to make it 'he killed' in the past tense
Changed vowels to show the action is being done to someone instead of by someone
Changed from past tense (was killed) to present tense (is being killed) by adding يُ at the start
Added ون to change from 'he' to 'they' (plural) while keeping passive meaning of being killed

steps.m: and they are being killed (connected to يُقَاتِلُونَ - they fight)
steps.n: Added و with fatha to connect this word with the previous action of fighting

steps.m: to promise, to make a commitment

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding vowel marks. This makes it name the action itself rather than doing the action.

steps.m: as a binding promise (emphasizing Allah's commitment in this transaction)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is emphasizing the promise being made. In this ayah, it's emphasizing that Allah's promise is definite and binding.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else

steps.m: upon him (connected to وَعْدًا - a promise)
steps.n: Added هِ (meaning 'him') to show who is being referred to. The ي is added before هِ to connect it smoothly. This refers back to the promise (وَعْدًا) mentioned in the ayah.
Root letters joined together with a shadda (double letter) to make the noun 'truth'
Adding tanween alif changes it to describe how something is - in this case, it describes how Allah's promise is true
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it's used to show that the fighting happens within Allah's cause/path (سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ).
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
This is the base borrowed word from Hebrew 'Torah', adapted to Arabic pronunciation patterns
Added ة at the end to make it the proper name for the Torah (the holy book given to Moses)
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the ت with shadda because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in) in this ayah
This is the base borrowed word from Greek 'evangelion', meaning the holy book given to Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him)
Added the definite article ال to specify this particular holy book
Added و at the beginning to connect it with the previous word (Torah) mentioned in the verse
Added kasra at the end to show it follows the word 'Torah' in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he read' to 'that which is read a lot' by adding قُرْ and ان to make it a special noun
The prefix 'ال' makes the noun definite
The word gets a kasra (i) ending to show it's connected to other holy books mentioned (Torah and Gospel)

steps.m: and the Quran (connected to التَّوْرَىٰةِ وَٱلْإِنجِيلِ)
steps.n: Adding conjunction prefix وَ to connect with previous phrase
Combines conjunction و with relative pronoun من
The root letters represent the basic meaning of fulfilling or completing something
Connected the root letters و-ف-ي with vowel marks (َ) to make 'he fulfilled' in the past tense
Added أ at the beginning to show doing something more completely or thoroughly

steps.m: he fulfilled completely (who is more faithful to عَهْدِهِۦ)
steps.n: Added the alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end because the word ends with a ي sound. This word relates to being faithful to عَهْدِهِۦ (His covenant) in this ayah.
The root relates to making promises or covenants
Added fatha after ع and sukun after ه to form the noun meaning 'promise' or 'covenant'
Added preposition بِ to indicate 'with/by'
Added kasra ending to indicate genitive case after preposition

steps.m: by His promise/covenant (referring to Allah's covenant)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show the covenant belongs to Allah (mentioned earlier in the ayah)
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (the believers). This is like adding glue between words to make them flow better when speaking.
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging it with the first letter
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on lam. This word follows سَبِيلِ (path) to show 'path of Allah'

steps.m: to spread good news or joy
steps.n: Basic meaning of spreading good news or joy
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added است at beginning to show seeking or experiencing the action for oneself
Added فَ to show this is a result or response to what came before
Changed to a command for multiple people by adding وا at the end

steps.m: to exchange or trade something for something else
steps.n: Root contains the weak letter ي
Root letters put together with fatha marks to make a noun meaning 'trade'
Same word with sukun mark added to show the letter is silent - doesn't change meaning
Added بِ at the beginning to show this transaction is being used as a tool or means
Added kasra at the end to show this is connected to what comes after it

steps.m: with/by your (all) trade/transaction (referring to the contract with Allah mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added كُمُ at the end to show this transaction belongs to all the believers being addressed
Basic form of the relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence, like 'who' or 'which' in English
Adding ال makes it more specific, like changing 'which' to 'the one which'
The ل gets a shadda and the initial alif changes to a wasl form (ٱ) for smoother pronunciation
Final ي written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This word connects to بَايَعْتُم (you all made a transaction) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Adding ا shows the trade/pledge is between two parties
Changed from 'he' to 'I' by adding تُ at the end

steps.m: you all made a mutual deal (with Allah)
steps.n: Added م to show plural 'you' as the subject. In this ayah, it refers to the believers making a deal with Allah.
Added kasra to the letter ب to show it's a preposition meaning 'with'
Added ه with kasra at the end to show this is happening to/with 'it' (referring back to the covenant mentioned earlier in the ayah)
Added a small alif (ۦ) after ه to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Starting with the basic demonstrative pronoun. The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) in this form.
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
Adding كَ as a suffix to complete the demonstrative pronoun

steps.m: and that (refers to the great achievement mentioned in the previous part of the ayah)
steps.n: Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to the previous statement, joining the ideas together

steps.m: he/it (referring to the great achievement/success mentioned after)
steps.n: Independent personal pronoun that refers to something coming after it in the ayah. Here it points forward to الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ (the great achievement/success).
Root contains the weak letter و
Added vowels to turn the action into a noun. This word is what هو (it) refers to in ذٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ (that is the success).
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific
Added ُ at the end to show this is what هُوَ (it) is referring to in the phrase ذٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ (that is the great success)
Added ي between second and third letters with vowels to show this describes something with a strong quality
Added ال at beginning to make it specific and show it's describing the triumph mentioned before
Added ُ at the end to show this word matches with الفوز which it describes

steps.m: to turn back from wrong to right

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (action) to describe someone who does the action
Added ون to show we're talking about many people who do this

steps.m: the people who turn back (from wrong)
steps.n: Added ال at the start to point to specific people, and doubled the ت with shadda to show emphasis
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show who does the action
Added ون at the end to show multiple people who worship

steps.m: the worshippers (part of list describing believers)
steps.n: Added ال at beginning to make it specific. The alif in عابد becomes long (ٰ) after ال
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters together

steps.m: one who praises (someone who regularly praises others)
steps.n: Changed to show someone who does the action as their characteristic

steps.m: those who regularly praise (group of men)
steps.n: The masculine sound plural suffix ون indicates multiple male entities who perform the action of praising

steps.m: the ones who regularly praise (this word is part of a list describing qualities of true believers)
steps.n: The word gets 'the' (ال) in front to make it specific. The first letter gets a long 'aa' sound mark (ٰ) to help with reading.

steps.m: to travel/journey (especially for worship or spiritual purposes)

steps.n: Adding alif after first root letter makes this describe the person who does the action. The weak letter ي changes to hamza (ئ) because it comes after alif.
Adding ون to create masculine sound plural

steps.m: the ones who travel/journey (for worship) (part of list describing المؤمنين)
steps.n: Adding ال (the) causes the س to get doubled (shown by ّ). This word is part of a list describing the qualities of the believers who will receive good news.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb

steps.m: one who bows down in worship
steps.n: Changed pattern to show who does the action by adding alif after first letter and kasra under second letter

steps.m: those who bow down in worship
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: the ones who bow down in worship (part of a list describing true believers)
steps.n: Added ال (with assimilation making the ر doubled) to make it specific. This word appears in a list of qualities describing true believers.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who bows down in worship
steps.n: Changed pattern to show who does the action. This word is part of a list describing believers, starting with التائبون (those who repent).

steps.m: those who bow down in worship
steps.n: Added ون to show it's talking about many people. This matches the form of other words in the list like التائبون.

steps.m: the ones who bow down in worship
steps.n: Added ال ('the') at the start and doubled the س with shadda to make it 'the prostrators' specifically
Added vowels to connect the letters together to make a basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (he commanded) to a noun meaning 'commander'
Added ون to show it's talking about many people who command

steps.m: the ones who command (part of a list describing true believers)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and added final vowel mark. This word is part of a list describing qualities of true believers.

steps.m: to know or recognize something as good/right

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he knew' to describe 'something that is known' - like changing 'he wrote' to 'written'
Added ال to make it definite and ِ to show it's affected by a preposition

steps.m: with/by what is right (object of الْءَامِرُونَ - those who command)
steps.n: Added بِ meaning 'with/by'. This word is what is being commanded by الْءَامِرُونَ (those who command) in the ayah.
Changes from basic verb 'prevent' to mean 'one who prevents' by following the فاعل pattern - like changing 'write' to 'writer'
The weak letter ي drops when adding sound masculine plural ending ون
Adding ال makes it specific and causes the ن to be doubled (shown by ّ)
The alif in ال becomes a special connecting alif (ٱ) when joining words
Adds 'and' to connect this word to previous words
This is a basic preposition that shows separation or distance from something
Added kasra at the end to connect it with the next word المنكر (the wrong/evil). Together they mean 'away from the wrong/evil'

steps.m: to reject or disapprove of something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at the start to turn the verb 'reject' into 'something rejected' - like changing 'deny' to 'denied thing'
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after the word عَنِ (meaning 'from/about')
Changes from 'to protect' to 'protector' - like changing from a verb to a person who does that action. Connected to the word حدود meaning it's a 'protector of boundaries'.
Added -oon ending to make it mean 'many male protectors' instead of just one
Making the noun definite

steps.m: and the ones who guard (referring to the believers who guard Allah's limits)
steps.n: The word gained 'wa' (and) at the start and changed to show it's part of a list describing believers
Changes from singular to broken plural form
Added preposition لِ meaning 'for/to' and kasra ending because it's connected to the word اللَّهِ (of Allah) that comes after it
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال and combining letters with a shadda mark
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter makes it mean 'give' instead of just 'bring' news
Added kasra at end to help connect with next word

steps.m: and bring good news! (command to the Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this command with what came before

steps.m: to trust/believe in something or someone

steps.n: The root أ-م-ن becomes مؤمنين by adding مُ at the start (meaning 'one who does') and ين at the end (meaning 'many'). This makes it mean 'many believers' instead of just 'to believe'.
Adding ٱلْ at the start points to specific believers - the ones being described in this verse. The َ at the end shows this word is receiving the action of 'give good news' (بَشِّرِ).
This is a negative particle that makes the verb كَانَ (it was) into 'it was not'. In this ayah, it's saying 'it was not for the Prophet...'
Added fatha vowels to make past tense verb
Changed to common past tense verb with weak middle letter
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic noun
Changes from 'news' to 'news-bringer' by adding ي to make it mean a person who brings news
Adds ال to specify a particular prophet
Adds لِ at the beginning to show this is being done for or to the prophet
Adds kasra at the end to show this is the object being affected by the verb يَسْتَغْفِرُوا (they seek forgiveness)
Added ٱلّ to make it definite. This is the singular form of the relative pronoun.
Added ن at the end to make it plural, referring to multiple people
Added و meaning 'and'. This word is the subject of the verb ءَامَنُوٓا۟ (they believed) in this ayah.
This is the basic meaning that all related words come from
The alif makes it mean to actively show trust/faith in something
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action
This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it introduces what was not appropriate for the Prophet - which was seeking forgiveness for polytheists.
Form X verb meaning 'to seek forgiveness'
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting forgiveness
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added plural marker to show multiple people seeking forgiveness
The verb already has the plural 'they' ending - no real change between words

steps.m: to make something a partner/equal with something else
steps.n: Forms active participle meaning 'one who associates partners with Allah'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does this action
Added ين to make it plural - meaning many people who do this

steps.m: the ones who make partners (with Allah)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific/definite

steps.m: for the ones who make partners (with Allah)
steps.n: Added لِ at start to show 'for/to' and َ at end to show this is being acted upon
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation that didn't happen. In this ayah, it introduces the condition of them being close relatives.
Added و meaning 'and'
Adding the fatha (َ) makes it a past tense verb
Adding another fatha (َ) completes the past tense verb pattern
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Added optional pronunciation marks that don't change the meaning
This is a special word that doesn't come from a root. It's used to show ownership or belonging.
Adding ى at the end makes this word refer to multiple people who possess or are characterized by something. Here it describes people who have قُرْبَىٰ (close relationship).
Added vowel marks to change the verb into a noun meaning the state of being close
Added ى with alif on top to make it refer to a close relationship or kinship. This word relates to النَّبِىِّ (the Prophet) in the ayah, showing who has the close relatives.
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects with the word بَعْدِ (after) to mean 'from after'.
Added a small ن mark (called tanwin) after the main ن to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create the basic word meaning 'after'
Added kasra at the end because this word follows مِنْ (from) in the ayah, showing it's connected to what comes before it
This is a connecting word that joins two parts of a sentence. Here it connects what comes before with تَبَيَّنَ (it became clear) that comes after.
Adding shadda (ّ) and fatha (َ) changes meaning from something being clear to making something clear

steps.m: it became clear/evident (to لَهُمْ - to them)
steps.n: Adding تَ shows the clarity happened by itself. This word connects to لَهُمْ (to them) in the ayah.
Added fatha to the basic preposition ل. This preposition connects to the verb يَسْتَغْفِرُوا (seek forgiveness) to show who they are seeking forgiveness for.
Added هُ with damma to show this refers to a single male person
Added م with sukun to change this from singular (him) to plural (them)
This is an emphatic particle that shows certainty. The shadda makes the meaning stronger.

steps.m: that they (referring to أَصْحٰبُ ٱلْجَحِيمِ - companions of the Fire)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being talked about. This connects to the next words أَصْحٰبُ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (companions of the Fire) which explain who 'they' are.
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from verb (to accompany) to noun (companion/accompanier)
Changed to broken plural pattern by adding hamza at start and modifying internal vowels
Changed to show these companions are the ones doing something (added u-mark at end) and wrote the long a sound differently
Added ي to make it into a noun describing the intense thing
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ to show it follows another word
This is a negative particle that makes the verb كَانَ (was) into 'was not'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added past tense vowels to show this happened in the past. This word is related to يَكُونُ (he is) in other verses.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in past tense. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.

steps.m: to cover up or remove faults

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at beginning to show asking for the action to be done
Changed verb into a noun showing the action itself

steps.m: the seeking of forgiveness (subject of كَانَ)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was) in this ayah. Also changed initial alif to wasl form.
This is a proper noun of Hebrew origin, so it doesn't follow standard Arabic root patterns
Ibrahim's name has a fatha mark to show he is the one being described as tender-hearted and patient
Root letters أ-ب-و joined together with a fat-ha (َ) on أ to make the word 'father'
Added kasra and ي to show this word is being possessed by something
Added هِ to show the father belongs to him
Added لِ at the beginning to show direction or purpose
This is the basic form of the word that means 'except'. It's a particle that helps exclude something from what was mentioned before it.
Adding ا at the end makes the word complete. In this ayah, it shows that Ibrahim's seeking forgiveness was not absolute - there was an exception to it.

This is a preposition that shows separation or moving away from something. In this ayah, it connects to مَّوْعِدَةٍ (a promise) to show that Ibrahim's asking forgiveness came from/because of a promise.

steps.m: to promise or make a commitment
steps.n: This turns the action of promising into a noun meaning 'a promise'
Added مَ at the start and ِ in the middle to show where or when the promise happens
Added ة to show we're talking about one specific promise
Added ٍ (tanween kasra) to show this word is indefinite and receiving an action

steps.m: a specific promise (that was made) (object of عَن)
steps.n: The م has a shadda because it comes after the word عَن, which causes the next letter to double. This is a common rule in Arabic.
This turns the action of promising into a noun meaning 'a promise'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past
Added هَا at the end to show who received the promise
Changed the ending هَا to هَآ to help with pronunciation when stopping on this word
This is the base form used to show something is being acted upon directly

steps.m: him (as direct object of وَعَدَ - he promised)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show it refers to 'him'. This word is receiving the action of the verb وَعَدَ (he promised) in this ayah.
This is a special word that means 'when' but specifically for completed actions. It's like saying 'when [something was done]' in English.

steps.m: and when (something was completed) (connects to تَبَيَّنَ - it became clear)
steps.n: The فَ connects this with what happens next (تَبَيَّنَ - it became clear). It's like saying 'So when this happened, then that happened'
This root became a preposition that shows something is in the middle of other things
Doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'to make something clear' instead of just 'being clear'
Adding ت at the beginning shows the action happened by itself - something 'became clear' rather than someone 'making it clear'
Added fatha to the letter lam to show it's a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who the preposition is directed to

steps.m: for him (connected to وَعَدَهَآ - he promised it)
steps.n: Added pronunciation helpers (ۥٓ) that don't change the meaning. This word shows who Ibrahim made the promise to.
This is an emphasis particle that connects ideas and shows certainty about what follows

steps.m: that he (is عَدُوٌّ لِّلَّهِ - an enemy of Allah)
steps.n: Added the هُ suffix which shows this refers to a male person (in this case, Ibrahim's father). The small و (ۥ) is just a pronunciation mark.
Added vowels to change from action to person doing action. The fatha-damma pattern shows someone who does something.
The double و with tanween (ٌّ) makes this word stronger, showing this is definitely an enemy. In this ayah, it describes someone's relationship to Allah.
This is the unique name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity but is used exclusively as God's proper name.
Added ل at the beginning to show this is being done for or belongs to Allah
Added vowel marks to show proper pronunciation
Added emphasis by doubling the ل
Basic meaning of being free or clear of something
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding ت at start and doubling ر shows doing the action to oneself
Basic preposition meaning 'from' with sukun added to show where the word pauses
Added pronoun هُ (him) to show who the preposition relates to

steps.m: indeed/verily (emphasizes that إِبْرٰهِيمَ is لَأَوَّاهٌ حَلِيمٌ)
steps.n: This is a particle that adds emphasis to the sentence. It makes what comes after it more definite and certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Ibrahim was truly compassionate and forbearing.

steps.m: Abraham (a proper name of Hebrew origin meaning 'father of many')
steps.n: This is a proper noun borrowed from Hebrew, combining 'ab' (father) and 'raham' (multitude). The long ā (ٰ) indicates the extended pronunciation.

steps.m: Abraham (as the subject of لَأَوَّاهٌ - 'indeed tender-hearted')
steps.n: The fatha (َ) at the end shows Abraham is being described by the word لَأَوَّاهٌ (tender-hearted) in this ayah

steps.m: to turn to (God) with sighs and supplications
one who frequently turns to God with deep emotion
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) and long alif show this is someone who does this action intensively and repeatedly

steps.m: one who frequently turns to God (in general)
steps.n: Adding tanween dammah makes this a general description rather than referring to a specific person

steps.m: truly one who frequently turns to God (describing إِبْرٰهِيمَ)
steps.n: Adding لَ at the beginning adds emphasis, showing this is definitely true about Ibrahim
Root ح-ل-م became حَلِيم by adding vowels and ي to make it mean 'very patient' - a lasting quality
Added ٌ at the end to show this is describing Ibrahim in general terms
This is a special word that negates the verb كَانَ (was) that comes after it in this ayah, making it mean 'was not'

steps.m: and not (negates كَانَ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Added vowel marks (fathas) to make it mean 'he was/existed' in the past tense

steps.m: he was (negated by ما before it)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has و in middle). This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning. This word is negated by ما before it to mean 'was not'.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject/doer

steps.m: to lose the way/go in wrong direction

steps.n: Added vowels (َ) to make it 'he' past tense - like changing 'lose' to 'he lost'
Changed to present tense and causative form - now means making someone else go astray

steps.m: in order to make (someone) lose their way (purpose of كَانَ)
steps.n: Added لِ to show this is the purpose or reason for the action in كَانَ (was)

steps.m: to stand together, rise up, establish
people, nation (object of يُضِلَّ - to lead astray)
steps.n: Changed the root meaning of 'standing together' into a noun meaning 'people who stand/rise together as one'. This word is being acted upon by يُضِلَّ (to lead astray) in this ayah.
Added ًا at the end to show this is the thing being acted upon (like saying 'the ball' in 'he threw the ball'). The small ۢ is just for pronunciation.

steps.m: to be distant or come after something

steps.n: Changes the word into a basic noun meaning 'after'
Added fatha at the end to show this word connects to 'guided them' (هَدَىٰهُمْ) in the ayah

steps.m: when (referring to past time, connecting to هَدَىٰهُمْ)
steps.n: This is a special word (particle) that doesn't come from a root. It's used to talk about a specific time in the past. In this ayah, it connects to هَدَىٰهُمْ (He guided them) to show when something happened.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Adding the vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was guided
Added small alif (ٰ) above ى to show it should be pronounced clearly
Added vowel marks and doubled the middle letter to create the basic particle. The shadda (ّ) shows emphasis on the ت sound.

steps.m: until (connects to يُبَيِّنَ - he makes clear)
steps.n: Added the alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end. This particle connects to the next word يُبَيِّنَ (he makes clear), showing that something continues until something else is made clear.

steps.m: to make something clear or distinct

steps.n: Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger - from just being clear to actively making something clear
Adding يُ at the start and changing the middle vowel to kasra (ِ) makes this a present tense verb. The word comes after حَتَّىٰ (until) which requires this form.
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: for them, to them (referring to قَوْمًۢا/people in this ayah)
steps.n: Added هُم which means 'them' to show who the action is for. This connects to the word قَوْمًۢا (people) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
This is a basic word that points to things or actions being discussed. In this ayah, it connects to يَتَّقُونَ (they fear/are conscious of) to show what they should be conscious of.
The shadda (ّ) shows this word is strongly connected to the word before it, like joining two words together when speaking
Changes from base form to present tense verb meaning 'he protects'
Added ت with shadda (ّ) to show the person is doing the action to themselves

steps.m: they protect themselves (from what Allah has forbidden)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The final ن gets a fatha (َ) because it connects to the next word in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the word that means 'if' or 'whether'
Adding the shadda changes this from a conditional word to one that emphasizes what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah truly knows all things.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity'
Added ال to make it definite and specific, doubled the ل with shadda, and changed the first letter to show this is the proper name for the one true God
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in this ayah
The doubled ل with ُ makes it mean 'all' or 'every'
Adding بِ shows this word connects to عَلِيمٌ (All-Knowing), telling us Allah is knowing with/of every thing

steps.m: with every (connected to عَلِيمٌ and شَىْءٍ)
steps.n: The ِ shows this word connects to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'with every thing'

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: The root letters ش-ي-أ form the noun شَيْء (thing) by adding fatha on ش and sukoon on ي. The hamza at the end is pronounced but sometimes written without dots.
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end because this word is owned by كُلِّ (every/all). The ي is written as ى but pronounced the same.
Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone who has this quality very strongly
Added ٌ (tanween) at the end to show this is a complete description of Allah
This is a special word that comes at the start of a sentence to show that what follows is definitely true. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah truly has dominion over the heavens and earth.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال and joining letters together

steps.m: Allah (as the object being verified by إِنَّ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is the object (ism) of إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in this ayah. Also changed initial alif to wasl form.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added هُ (him) to show who something is for. This word connects to مُلْكُ (dominion) in this ayah, showing that the dominion belongs to Him.
Added a small و mark (ۥ) after هُ to help with pronunciation
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the power or authority one has. This word is connected to اللَّهَ (Allah) in the ayah, showing that all dominion belongs to Him.
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject of the sentence, emphasizing that dominion itself belongs to Allah
Forms the basic word for 'sky'
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added اء to change from 'rising up' (verb) to 'sky' (noun) - the place that is high above us
Added ات to make it plural. The اء changes to و before ات
Added ٱل to make it definite and doubled the س with shadda
Changed vowel marks to show this word is possessed by مُلْك (dominion/kingdom) in this ayah
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Adding ال makes it refer to THE earth rather than any earth/ground
Adding و connects this to the previous phrase about the heavens (السَّمٰوٰتِ)
Adding kasra shows this belongs to Allah (اللَّهِ) as mentioned in the phrase 'to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth'
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'being alive' to 'making something alive'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels and weak letter to make it causative - making something happen to something else
Added يُ at the beginning to show it's happening in the present/future
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word with the previous one (يُحْىِۦ - he gives life)
This is a negative particle that makes the statement negative. In this ayah, it's used with the word لَكُم (for you) to say 'you do not have'.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement about Allah giving life and causing death (يُحْىِۦ وَيُمِيتُ)
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose
Adds كُم to show this applies to multiple people being addressed
This is a basic particle that shows something comes from or originates from something else
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a word ending in ن (دُونِ). When ن meets م, the ن sound blends into the م sound, making it stronger.

steps.m: to be lower than or beneath something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes the meaning to express being lower or beneath
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes right after it in the ayah
Added ال and merged it with the word to create the proper name Allah, referring specifically to the One True God
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on ل to emphasize the name. The ٱ is a spelling variation of ا.
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from, starts, or what it's made of. In this ayah, it comes before the word دُونِ to help express 'besides/other than Allah'.

steps.m: to protect, to be close to and take care of

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes this into a noun meaning someone who does this action as their role
Adding kasratain (ٍ) makes it indefinite (any/a) and shows it comes after the word مِن (from/of)
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this negation with the previous phrase. This word negates وَلِىٍّ (protector) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Added ي between second and third letters and changed middle vowel to kasra to show this describes someone who helps a lot

steps.m: any helper/supporter (in a phrase like 'of any helper')
steps.n: Added tanween kasra at the end to show this is indefinite (any helper) and is being used after مِن (from/of) in the ayah
This is a basic particle in Arabic that shows certainty about past events or readiness for future events
Adding ل at the beginning makes it more emphatic, like adding 'indeed' in English
The shadda (doubled letter) adds even more emphasis. This word introduces the past event of Allah turning in mercy (تَّابَ) that follows in the ayah

steps.m: to turn back or return (from wrong to right)
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of returning from sin

steps.m: he turned back (from wrong to right)
steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowel marks. The middle letter و changes to ا in past tense. This shows someone did this action in the past.

steps.m: He definitely turned back (subject of اللَّهُ - referring to Allah accepting repentance)
steps.n: Added shadda (double mark) on first letter to show emphasis. In this ayah, it's Allah who is accepting the repentance of others.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic word for 'deity'
Changed to the unique name for the one true God by adding ال with special pronunciation, doubling the ل with shadda, and adding ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in this verse
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or above something
Adding ى at the end makes it work as a preposition. In this ayah, it connects with the verb تَابَ (He turned in mercy) to show Allah's mercy coming down upon someone.
This root is about giving important news or information
Root letters got vowels to make it mean 'important news' instead of 'to tell news'
Changed the middle vowel and added ي to show someone who does this action
The hamza (ء) changes to a doubled ي (يّ) which is common in Arabic
Added ٱل (the) at the beginning and doubled the ن with shadda
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by تَّابَ (He turned in forgiveness)
Changes the root 'to move away' into 'one who moves away' by adding an alif after first letter and kasra under second letter - like changing 'write' to 'writer'

steps.m: one who leaves their home for a cause
steps.n: Adding مُ at the start shows this person is currently doing the action of leaving home

steps.m: those who leave their homes for a cause
steps.n: Adding masculine plural suffix to indicate multiple people

steps.m: the ones who leave their homes for a cause
steps.n: Adding 'the' to make it specific
Adding 'and' to connect it to the previous word (the Prophet) in the verse
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it 'he helped' in the past tense
Changes verb 'helped' into a noun meaning 'helper' by adding alif after first letter and kasra under second letter
Changes the word to show many helpers instead of just one
Adds 'the' to specify these particular helpers and shows this word follows another word in the sentence
Adds 'and' to connect this word with 'the emigrants' mentioned before it
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence, like 'who' or 'which' in English.
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to multiple people or things
Adding ال with shadda makes it mean 'those who' instead of just 'who'. This word connects to ٱتَّبَعُوهُ (followed him) in the ayah, describing the people who followed.

steps.m: to follow closely or go after

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added اِ at start and doubled the ت with shadda to show doing something with extra effort or purpose
Added وا at the end to show many people did the action
Changed اِ to ٱ and added هُ at the end to show who they followed
This is a preposition that shows location or time. In this ayah, it connects to the word سَاعَةِ (time/hour) to show when something happened.
Connected the root letters with fatha. The middle weak letter و changes to ا when between two fathas.
Added ة to make it into a noun meaning one unit or instance of time
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to العُسْرَةِ (hardship) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'time of hardship'
Added damma (ُ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on second letter to make it a noun meaning the state of being difficult
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of hardship
Added ال to make it definite (the) and kasra at the end because it follows في (in) in the phrase 'hour of the hardship'
This is a preposition that shows where something starts from. Here it connects with the word بَعْدِ (after) to mean 'from after'
Added a small ن mark (called tanwin) after the ن to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word
Added vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make a word meaning 'after' or 'following something'
Added kasra at the end because this word follows the preposition مِنۢ (from/after) which requires the following word to take kasra

steps.m: that which (connects to كَادَ - was about to)
steps.n: In this ayah, مَا connects what comes before with what comes after, specifically linking to the verb كَادَ (was about to). It introduces what almost happened.

steps.m: to almost do something, to be on the verge of

steps.n: Added short vowels (fatha marks) to make it a complete past tense verb meaning 'he almost did'

The middle letter و changed to ا because it's in the middle of the word. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say. In this ayah, it connects to the verb يَزِيغُ (deviate/swerve) to mean 'their hearts almost deviated'.

steps.m: to turn away or deviate from the right path
steps.n: Basic meaning focuses on the concept of straying or deviating
Added vowel marks (fathas) to make it mean 'he deviated' in the past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding damma at end. This word describes what happens to the hearts (قُلُوبُ) of some people.
This root relates to turning or changing, which is why it's used for 'heart' - something that constantly turns and changes
Takes the root meaning 'to turn/change' and makes it into the noun 'heart' (because hearts can change feelings)

Added vowels (fat-ha) to make it past tense 'he was'

This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَتُوبُونَ (they repent) into 'they do not repent'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb. The middle و changes to ا which is common in hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle).
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now or in the future
Adding ونَ makes it plural for 'they'
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to the verb يَرَوْنَ (they see) in the ayah
This is a pronoun that refers to a group of people (all male or mixed gender). In this ayah, it refers to the same people who are being tested (يُفْتَنُونَ) once or twice every year but don't repent.

steps.m: to remember or keep something in mind

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added emphasis by doubling middle letters with shadda and changing vowels. This form often means to think deeply about something or take a lesson from it.
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is a basic conditional particle that shows when something happens. It's like saying 'whenever' or 'at the time when' in English
The و connects this phrase to what came before it, showing these events happen in sequence
Added fatha (َ) on م to show how to pronounce it. This word is a question word that means 'what' and connects to the next word أُنزِلَتْ (was sent down).
Changed the regular alif (ا) to an alif with madda (آ) which shows a longer sound. This is just a writing style change and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أُ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ makes it passive - shows something receiving the action rather than doing it

steps.m: it (referring to سُورَةٌ) was sent down
steps.n: Adding ت shows this is about سُورَةٌ (chapter/surah) being sent down. The ت is needed because سُورَةٌ is feminine.
This root relates to something that surrounds or contains
Root letters were given vowels to make a noun meaning a physical barrier that surrounds
Added ة to specify one complete unit, like one chapter that contains verses
Added ٌ at the end to show this is any chapter, not a specific one

steps.m: to look or gaze at something

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added shadda on first letter to show they looked with extra attention. This word connects to بَعْضُهُمْ (some of them) in the ayah to show who they were looking at.
Basic noun pattern showing a portion or part of something

steps.m: some (as the doer of an action)
steps.n: Added damma to show this word is doing something in the sentence

steps.m: some of them (looking at إِلَىٰ بَعْضٍ - to one another)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who the 'some' refers to. In this ayah, some hypocrites are looking at others among themselves.
Basic preposition indicating direction or destination. Often used to show movement towards something or someone.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is just a spelling variation of regular alif. In this ayah, it points towards بَعْضٍ (others/some), showing who they were looking to.
Added vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make it mean 'some' or 'part'
Added two dots under the last letter to show we're talking about 'some' of something specific mentioned in the sentence
Basic question word that expects a yes/no answer. In this ayah, it's asking if anyone sees them, connecting to the verb يَرَىٰكُم (sees you).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding ي at the start changes it from past to present tense
Added pronoun suffix for 'you all'
Basic preposition showing something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
The 'n' sound is doubled (has a shadda) because it joins with the next word أَحَدٍ, making it flow better when read
Added sukun at the end to show this is the end of the word before it connects to أَحَدٍ (anyone)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic word form
The tanwin kasra (ٍ) shows this word is indefinite (any) and is being affected by the word مِنْ (from) that comes before it
This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. Here it connects to the next word ٱنصَرَفُوا۟ (they turned away), showing this happened after what was mentioned before.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added اِنْ at the beginning to show the action happens to oneself
Added وا to show multiple people did the action, and changed اِ to ٱ as it's more common in the Quran
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. This word is connected to قُلُوبَهُم (their hearts) which it acts upon in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject

steps.m: to turn, flip, or transform (also refers to the heart as the transformer of emotions)

steps.n: Noun form meaning the physical heart or metaphorical center. In this ayah, قُلُوبَهُم (their hearts) is the direct object of the verb صَرَفَ (turned away), showing what Allah turned away.
This is a broken plural form, which means it changes the internal structure of the word rather than just adding a suffix
Added هُم (their) at the end to show whose hearts + فتحة (a) to show it's the object
Starting with the base particle أَنَّ which connects ideas and shows cause/effect
Added بِ at the beginning to show this explains the reason for something

steps.m: because they (are قَوْمٌ لَّا يَفْقَهُونَ - a people who do not understand)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who this is about. This explains why Allah turned their hearts away - because they are people who don't understand.
Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands/rises together'
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is the subject of the verb 'they do not understand' in the ayah.
Negative particle with emphasis (shadda). Base form لا with added shadda for emphasis.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form
Changes from past tense (he understood) to present tense (he understands) by adding يَ at the start

steps.m: they understand deeply (referring to the hypocrites who don't understand)
steps.n: Changed from 'he understands' to 'they understand' by adding ون at the end. Used with لا to say 'they do not understand' about the hypocrites
This is an emphasis particle that shows certainty about what follows

steps.m: indeed certainly (introducing جَآءَكُمْ/has come to you)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning for extra emphasis. This combination is often used to start important statements, like the one here about the messenger coming.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks and changed ي to ا because it's a weak letter. This word describes what رَسُولٌ (messenger) did.
The long آ adds emphasis to show this was a definite or important coming

steps.m: he definitely came to you all
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who he came to - all the people being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و
Added tanween damma to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a messenger' instead of 'the messenger'). This messenger refers to Prophet Muhammad in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or that something is part of something else. It's like saying 'from' or 'of' in English.
The م gets doubled (with shadda) because it comes after another word ending in a vowel. This helps connect the words smoothly when speaking.
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun form meaning 'soul' or 'self'
Added أَ at the beginning and changed the middle vowel to ُ to make it plural, like changing 'foot' to 'feet' in English

steps.m: yourselves (referring to those being addressed in رَسُولٌ مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ - a messenger from yourselves)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show it means 'your souls/selves'. This word is describing where the messenger comes from - he is from among the same people he is speaking to.
Added ي in the middle and vowels to show this describes someone or something with a permanent quality of being strong
The ٌ shows this is describing the messenger mentioned before (رَسُولٌ)
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: upon him (referring to رَسُولٌ - the messenger)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end which means 'him'. This shows who the preposition is affecting. In this ayah, it refers back to the messenger (رَسُولٌ) mentioned earlier.
In this ayah, مَا connects to the word عَنِتُّمْ (your difficulties/hardships) that comes after it. It's showing what the Prophet ﷺ finds difficult - which is when the believers face hardship.
Added vowels to make a past tense verb showing someone experiencing distress
The shadda was not added to strengthen meaning - this is incorrect. The 'ta' at the end changed because it's connecting to another word, but the core meaning stays the same

steps.m: you all experienced distress (describing what distresses رَسُولٌ)
steps.n: Added تُم at the end to show this refers to all of you (plural). This describes what causes distress to the messenger (رَسُولٌ) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to be eager or concerned about something
steps.n: This is the basic root meaning showing the concept of being very concerned about or devoted to something
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb showing a state of being eager
Added ي to make it into an active participle describing someone who has this quality. This describes رَسُولٌ (messenger) in the ayah.

steps.m: one who is eager/concerned (as main quality describing رَسُولٌ)
steps.n: Added tanween damma to show this is a main quality describing the messenger
This is a preposition derived from the concept of being above or elevated
The ى at the end changes to يْ to make it easier to add endings. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: upon you all (referring to the believers/المؤمنين)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. In this ayah, it refers to the believers who are being addressed.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling safe
Changed from a past action (trusted) to describe a person who believes/trusts
Changed to show many people instead of one
Added a mark to show this word is being acted upon
Added 'the' to make it specific

steps.m: with/towards the believers (in relation to رَءُوفٌ - compassionate)
steps.n: Added 'with/towards' to show how it connects to the word رَءُوفٌ (compassionate) in the ayah
Basic root letters connected with vowels to make a word meaning 'showing mercy'
The و in the middle and ُ on the hamza make it an intensive form, showing someone who has this quality very strongly
The tanween shows this is describing the messenger (رَسُولٌ) in the ayah. Both words end with ٌ because they go together.
Adding vowels َ and ِ creates basic past tense verb meaning 'to show mercy'
Added ي to show this is a lasting quality someone has
Added tanween dammah to show this is any merciful one (not specific)
The doubled ر (r) sound shows extra emphasis, making it mean 'intensely merciful' instead of just 'merciful'
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition, similar to saying 'if' in English
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from something before. This word connects to تَوَلَّوْا۟ (they turn away) to mean 'then if they turn away'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding تَ shows he did this action to himself
Doubling the last letter shows the action was done thoroughly or completely
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action together

steps.m: to speak, tell, or say something

steps.n: Changed the word into a command form by adding ُ on first letter and ْ on second letter. The middle letter و drops out because it's a 'weak' letter that often disappears in Arabic.

steps.m: so/then say! (responding to إِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ - if they turn away)
steps.n: Added فَ at the start to show this is a response or result. Here it responds to 'if they turn away' by telling what to do next.

steps.m: to be sufficient, to be enough

steps.n: Root letters turned into a simple word meaning 'enough'
Added ي with an alif (ىَ) at the end to mean 'my/for me'. This word is describing Allah in this ayah - 'Allah is sufficient for me'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
This is a basic negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'. It's one of the most common words in Arabic.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
The alif is elongated (with a straight line above it) to add emphasis to the negation, which is important here because it's part of the phrase 'There is NO god except Him'

steps.m: to be worshipped, to be taken as divine

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to worship' into noun 'a deity'
The small alif above makes the 'aa' sound longer when reading

steps.m: any god (object of لَآ meaning 'there is no')
steps.n: Added َ at the end because this word is the object of لَآ (no/not) in the phrase لَآ إِلٰهَ (there is no god)
Basic form of the exception particle
Adding shadda and alif makes the exception stronger and clearer. In this ayah, it's making an exception to say there is no deity EXCEPT Him.

steps.m: he (referring to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers back to Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah. The هُ shows it's talking about one male/masculine thing, and the وَ makes it a separate word instead of attaching to another word.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for connecting with the next part

steps.m: upon him (connected to تَوَكَّلْتُ - I relied)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show this applies to 'him'. This word connects to تَوَكَّلْتُ (I relied) to show what was relied upon.

steps.m: to trust or depend on someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: he put his complete trust (in someone)
steps.n: Added تَ at start and doubled the middle letter to show doing this action to yourself with intensity
Changed from a statement (he trusted) to a command (trust!)

steps.m: I put my complete trust (in عَلَيْهِ - in Him)
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action in the past. This word connects to عَلَيْهِ (in Him) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to show the correct pronunciation: damma (ُ) on ه and fatha (َ) on و
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to mean 'and'. This word connects to رَبُّ (Lord) in the next ayah, showing who the Lord is.

steps.m: Lord (the one who nurtures and cares for everything)
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes it refer to the one who does this action perfectly - in this case Allah as the ultimate caregiver and nurturer
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this word is the subject being described by what follows it (العَرْشِ - 'the throne')

steps.m: to raise up, to build something high

steps.n: Root meaning 'to build high' becomes a simple noun meaning 'throne' - like how we build a high seat for a king. In this ayah it's connected to 'Lord' to say 'Lord of the throne'.
Added 'the' at the beginning to make it specific. The ِ at the end shows it belongs to something (in this case, it belongs to رَبُّ / Lord in the phrase 'Lord of the throne')
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a quality of the throne mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: the great, the mighty (describes العَرْشِ - the throne)
steps.n: Adding definite article makes the adjective definite

steps.m: the great, the mighty (describes العَرْشِ - the throne, showing possession)
steps.n: Adding kasra shows this describes العَرْشِ (the throne) in a possessive relationship
